tasks.title: Define lease UX status contract and concise blocked responses
tasks.approach: Narrow Phase 93 to the foundation that downstream UX work needs: expose a stable lease status vocabulary and make lease-denied command responses compact, truthful, and actionable. Follow-up tickets in the same phase cover extension controls, bounded wait behavior, and suspect drift detection.
technicalScope: Extend lease status/classification helpers so callers with an agentSessionId can distinguish lease-free, lease-held-by-me, lease-held-by-other, and stale/invalid lease states; reserve suspect/drift semantics for the suspect-drift follow-up ticket.
Revise workspace-edit-lease-held and related non-owner responses to include holder session id, task id when present, expiry, available alternatives, and one recommended next action.
Keep blocked responses compact for agents and avoid suggestions that require broad instruction/catalog reloads or expensive polling.
Document the response/status vocabulary consumed by the extension and follow-up same-phase tickets.
Add focused tests for status classification and denied-response payloads.
acceptanceCriteria: Denied claim, heartbeat, and release paths for active leases held by another session return holder identity, task id when present, expiry, alternatives, and one recommended next action in stable JSON.
A caller-provided agentSessionId can classify lease-free, lease-held-by-me, lease-held-by-other, and stale/invalid states without opening task SQLite or mutating the lease.
Agent-facing blocked copy is concise and does not tell agents to reload broad instructions or poll aggressively.
The shared vocabulary is documented in the relevant command instruction or contract surface for extension consumers.
Automated tests cover same-holder, other-holder, missing, stale/invalid, and compact blocked-response cases.
tasks.summary: Define the shared lease status contract and low-token blocked-response behavior that Phase 93 UX work builds on.
tasks.description: Define the shared lease status contract and low-token blocked-response behavior that Phase 93 UX work builds on.
tasks.risk: Medium: this changes command response contracts that agents and the extension will consume; keep the schema additive and deterministic.

metadata.roadmapSlice: multi-agent workspace lease and task-state authority
goals: Make the safe path feel like guidance instead of a mysterious failure.
Reduce wasted AI credits when an agent cannot edit yet.
Help users recover from stale or suspect lease states without manual file surgery.
blockers: T100188 is completed and provides clone-local lease commands under git common-dir.
Extension work still needs a stable session identifier where available; fallback behavior must be explicit when no session id exists.
The exact edit-capable flows that should honor waitForLease are not enumerated yet; this phase must define the first supported integration points before wiring broad enforcement.
implementationNotes: Use git common-dir for clone-local coordination state; do not store active workspace leases in branch-local task SQLite.
Keep responses compact for agents and avoid broad instruction/catalog reloads in blocked or read-only states.
Prefer fail-safe behavior for file mutation, stash, checkpoint, and task-state authority conflicts.

Current code exposes claim/heartbeat/release/status commands, workspace-coordination-status, a status-bar posture, and a read-only Coordination status card. It does not yet expose held-by-me vs held-by-other status, suspect drift status, extension lease actions, or waitForLease behavior.

ticketReview.verdict: Direction is truthful, but the original ticket was too broad for one implementation task and implied several states/behaviors that are not present yet.
evidence: T100188 is completed with lease command delivery evidence.
src/modules/task-engine/workspace-edit-lease-commands-runtime.ts has claim, heartbeat, release, and workspace-edit-status command behavior.

src/modules/task-engine/coordination/build-workspace-coordination-status.ts emits read-only posture and lease active/stale information, but not holder identity or drift comparison against the lease fingerprint.
extensions/cursor-workflow-cannon/src/extension.ts renders only a compact coordination posture in the status bar.
extensions/cursor-workflow-cannon/src/views/status/render-status-tab.ts renders a read-only Coordination card with lease active/stale/none only.
missingInformation: Which VS Code/session identifier should be treated as the durable agentSessionId for held-by-me vs held-by-other classification.
Which commands or edit-capable flows should accept waitForLease in the first implementation slice.
Whether recover stale and claim/release actions should require explicit per-click policy approval copy in the extension or reuse existing command-client approval handling.
How suspect state should be prioritized relative to dirty_workspace, dirty_task_db, and lease_held posture when multiple signals are true.

tasks.title: Bug: list-tasks rejects documented includeTaskIntake argument
tasks.approach: Treat this as a contract drift bug between generated agent instructions and the runtime argument validator/schema for list-tasks. Decide whether includeTaskIntake should be implemented in the CLI or removed from the generated instruction contract, then add a regression check so docs and runtime cannot diverge again.
technicalScope: Reproduce with pnpm exec wk run list-tasks '{"phaseKey":"90","includeTaskIntake":true,"limit":100}' on Node v22.22.2.
Inspect src/modules/task-engine/instructions/list-tasks.md and the list-tasks runtime argument parser/schema to identify why includeTaskIntake appears in docs but is rejected by the command.
If includeTaskIntake is intended, add it to the list-tasks runtime args schema and wire it to the existing task intake resolver output described by the instruction file.
If includeTaskIntake is not intended for the current runtime, remove it from the generated instruction contract and any agent-facing docs/snippets that advertise it.
Add a contract/regression test that compares documented list-tasks args against accepted runtime args for optional flags such as includeQueueHints and includeTaskIntake.
acceptanceCriteria: The documented list-tasks arguments match the live CLI validator behavior.
Running list-tasks with includeTaskIntake either succeeds with the documented taskIntakeByTaskId payload or the docs no longer advertise that argument.
A regression test or generation check prevents this specific instruction/runtime mismatch from recurring.
Agent-facing instructions remain clear about how to discover task intake gaps when includeTaskIntake is unavailable or unsupported.
The fix is verified with pnpm exec wk run list-tasks using the corrected argument surface and the relevant task-engine tests/checks.
tasks.summary: The list-tasks instruction file documents includeTaskIntake as a supported read argument, but the live CLI rejects includeTaskIntake as invalid when used during Phase 90 task verification.
tasks.risk: Medium: agents may rely on the generated instructions for task intake reads; mismatch causes failed verification steps and undermines confidence in machine guidance.

metadata.observedBehavior: list-tasks rejected includeTaskIntake as an invalid argument during Phase 90 task verification.
metadata.expectedBehavior: Either includeTaskIntake is accepted and returns taskIntakeByTaskId as documented, or the generated list-tasks instructions do not mention the flag.
metadata.recommendedSolution: Prefer implementing includeTaskIntake if the resolver path already exists, because the instruction file describes a useful agent read surface. Otherwise remove the flag from generated docs and snippets, then add a docs-vs-runtime argument contract test.
evidence: Live CLI rejected includeTaskIntake during verification after Phase 90 dashboard prompt tasks were created.

tasks.title: Guidance Composer for CAE authoring

metadata.problemStatement: Workflow Cannon's CAE/Guidance authoring already supports artifacts, activations, preview evidence, audit rows, and guarded publish, but users still have to translate plain-English policy intent into CAE artifact and activation fields manually. We want a full Guidance authoring panel prompt box where a user can state intent in plain English, answer clarifying questions inside the webview, review the proposed CAE implementation, accept draft creation plus preview, and optionally guarded-publish after evidence review.
metadata.expectedOutcome: Processing this wishlist must create a phased task breakdown for a Guidance Composer feature. The resulting proposed tasks should implement: a full authoring-panel composer UI; an in-webview clarification loop; a typed read-only cae-authoring-propose command that returns questions or a proposal; proposal rendering with plain-English summary, artifact markdown preview, activation scope/family/priority, preview/blast-radius evidence, raw JSON diff, and rollback plan; Accept Draft flow that creates a workspace artifact, creates a draft activation, runs cae-guidance-preview, and leaves the activation draft; guarded Publish flow that requires confirmation/rationale, passes caeMutationApproval, and calls cae-activate-draft-activation with fresh preview evidence; tests and docs.
metadata.impact: Makes CAE authoring accessible from natural language while preserving Workflow Cannon's deterministic, schema-backed, audit-backed mutation model. This reduces maintainer friction, makes guidance creation safer, and turns CAE from an expert-only registry editor into a guided product workflow.
metadata.constraints: Use the full Guidance authoring panel first, not the Dashboard overview. Keep the clarification interaction inside the webview. Do not let an LLM or chat-only approval write registry state directly. Prompt interpretation may produce intent and candidate fields, but Workflow Cannon must validate/coerce into typed CAE proposals and use existing governed mutation commands for writes. First Accept should create draft plus preview, not directly publish. Publish must be guarded, require fresh preview evidence, and use caeMutationApproval/audit rows. Preserve existing CAE authoring governance, optimistic concurrency, SQLite registry authority, workspace artifact conventions, and module boundary rules. True GitHub PR blocking is out of v1 unless a proper integration/check exists; v1 should hook workflows Workflow Cannon can observe.
metadata.successSignals: After processing, proposed phased tasks exist with dependencies and phase assignment. Implementation success is: users can type a request like 'XYZ unit tests must be run before a PR is opened'; the UI asks ambiguity-reducing questions including advisory vs ack-required vs satisfaction-required vs hard-stop; the proposal view shows summary, markdown preview, activation details, preview/blast-radius evidence, raw JSON diff, and rollback plan; Accept creates a workspace artifact and draft activation and runs preview; Publish is disabled until evidence is fresh and then records a governed audited activation; renderer/message-handler/command tests cover the flow and degraded states; CAE docs and authoring runbook are updated.
metadata.evidenceRef: Chat brainstorm 2026-05-11: CAE UI Guidance Composer plan. tasks.title: Add isolated proposal mode and state branch preparation
tasks.approach: Introduce proposal branches/worktrees as explicit artifacts surfaced through diffs and PR actions. In parallel, add deterministic task-state export artifacts so a future workflow-cannon/state branch can use mergeable event/snapshot data while SQLite remains a runtime cache.
technicalScope: Add isolated proposal creation for a task with branch/worktree metadata, base branch, changed-file tracking, and discard/recover actions.
Surface proposals as View Diff, Apply, Open PR, and Discard rather than silently changing the visible workspace.

Add deterministic task-state export of sorted snapshot JSON and append-only event JSONL from current SQLite state.
Prototype internal workflow-cannon/state backend design using event/snapshot artifacts as merge surface and SQLite as generated cache.
acceptanceCriteria: Users can create an isolated proposal for parallel work without changing the visible workspace edit lease holder.
Proposal changes are visible as bounded diffs and can be applied, opened as PR, or discarded explicitly.
Proposal task metadata and generated task intents are recoverable after restart.
Task-state export is deterministic across repeated runs from the same DB state.
A design note or ADR documents the future state branch backend, event replay model, and SQLite cache boundary.
tasks.summary: Support explicit parallel implementation proposals and prepare a mergeable task-state backend without making invisible edits the default.

metadata.roadmapSlice: multi-agent workspace lease and task-state authority
goals: Enable true parallel implementation without surprising invisible edits.
Prepare for a global task-state branch only after leases and authority rules are proven.
Move toward mergeable task-state artifacts while preserving current SQLite runtime performance.
blockers: Depends on stable lease, authority, and intent flows from prior phases.
State export must avoid leaking local-only policy traces or secrets.
implementationNotes: Use git common-dir for clone-local coordination state; do not store active workspace leases in branch-local task SQLite.
Keep responses compact for agents and avoid broad instruction/catalog reloads in blocked or read-only states.
Prefer fail-safe behavior for file mutation, stash, checkpoint, and task-state authority conflicts.

tasks.title: Make checkpoints stash and branch guards lease-aware
tasks.approach: Thread workspace edit lease checks through auto-checkpoint, stash, rewind, and branch-helper surfaces. Prefer exact checkpoint refs or patch artifacts over anonymous stash positions, and mark leases suspect when external branch changes are detected.
technicalScope: Update auto-checkpoint to allow dirty checkpoint/stash only when no lease exists or the caller owns the active lease.
Fail closed when another session owns the lease and a command would stash, rewind, checkout, reset, or otherwise alter workspace files.
Record exact stash commit SHAs or checkpoint IDs and never rely on stash@{0} for recovery semantics.
Add patch/checkpoint artifact mode for dirty work where feasible, with stash as fallback only.
Add branch switch guard helpers and extension warnings for external branch/HEAD changes during active lease.
acceptanceCriteria: Auto-checkpoint refuses to stash when a different active lease holder exists.
Lease holder checkpoint behavior records exact recoverable refs and excludes task-state files according to existing safety rules.
Branch switch helpers refuse or require explicit owner override during an active lease.
External branch changes mark the lease suspect and appear in coordination status.
Tests cover no-lease, owner-lease, other-lease, stale-lease, exact stash SHA recording, and suspect branch change cases.
tasks.summary: Prevent Workflow Cannon safety tooling from stashing or switching underneath another active agent.
tasks.description: Prevent Workflow Cannon safety tooling from stashing or switching underneath another active agent.
tasks.risk: High: checkpoint and rewind paths are safety-critical; incorrect behavior can lose or misapply local work.

metadata.roadmapSlice: multi-agent workspace lease and task-state authority

implementationNotes: Use git common-dir for clone-local coordination state; do not store active workspace leases in branch-local task SQLite.

Store v1 intents at .git/workflow-cannon/intents/*.json with atomic writes and validation.

tasks.approach: Add an explicit must-level rule in `.ai/AGENTS.md` (structured rule row, next free id) stating agents must not use GitHub Issues as the defect/backlog system for this repo; use `workspace-kit run create-task` for defects and scoped execution items, and improvement pipelines where applicable. GitHub remains for PRs/review/merge per maintainer delivery. Add a one-line cross-reference from repo-root `AGENTS.md` if it still only forwards to `.ai/AGENTS.md`, so cold-start agents see the distinction. Optionally add the same constraint as a short bullet under `.cursor/rules/agent-doc-routing.mdc` exceptions or workspace-kit section only if maintainers want IDE reinforcement — keep duplication minimal.
technicalScope: .ai/AGENTS.md — new rule directive + refs to create-task instruction path and `.ai/WORKSPACE-KIT-SESSION.md`
Repo-root AGENTS.md — single clarifying sentence if needed without duplicating maintainer docs/
Optional: `.cursor/rules/agent-doc-routing.mdc` — one alignment sentence (requestable or always-on per maintainer preference)
acceptanceCriteria: Agents reading only `.ai/` can infer that GitHub issues are not the backlog sink for this workspace
`create-task` / task-engine instructions are the cited action path for new defect records
No reliance on per-session chat rules to override machine canon; gh CLI remains valid only where forge ops explicitly require it (e.g. PRs), not general issue filing
Related defect T100156 remains valid (patch vs updates bug); this canon task prevents recurrence of wrong tracker choice
tasks.summary: Canon: defects/backlog live in task engine (create-task), not GitHub Issues.

tasks.title: Bug: update-wishlist JSON schema (patch) disagrees with handler (updates)
tasks.approach: Align CLI argv validation, generated schema snapshots, and task-engine-wishlist handler: normalize patch vs updates before persistence (prefer accepting patch as alias for updates when updates omitted), regenerate agent-cli-snippets and instruction markdown, add a contract test so schema-only matches runnable argv.
technicalScope: src/modules/task-engine/commands/task-engine-wishlist-on-command.ts — normalize args.patch into updates (or vice versa) with single canonical internal shape
Pilot/schema snapshot + .ai/agent-cli-snippets/by-command/update-wishlist.json — keep in sync with implemented argv
src/modules/task-engine/instructions/update-wishlist.md — document the single public shape
Optional test: update-wishlist succeeds with patch-only payload when planning token valid
acceptanceCriteria: pnpm exec wk run update-wishlist succeeds without duplicating identical patch and updates objects
--schema-only matches required keys for the handler
Instruction file matches behavior; CI or tests guard regression
expectedPlanningGeneration behavior unchanged when tasks.planningGenerationPolicy is require
tasks.summary: Normalize update-wishlist patch vs updates so schema-only matches handler and docs.

tasks.title: Task engine: future phase planning versus current workspace phase
tasks.approach: Extend task-engine and workspace phase snapshot behavior so operators can assign tasks to future phaseKeys while summaries clearly distinguish workspace current phase from per-task scheduled phase; expose permissible future targets and validation errors without breaking existing assign-task-phase flows.
technicalScope: Task engine validation rules for phaseKey assignment relative to workspace kit phase context
List and summary surfaces (CLI or internal APIs consumed by the dashboard) that enumerate current and future phase buckets consistently
Regression coverage for assign-task-phase and phase-aligned rollups when tasks sit in future phases
acceptanceCriteria: Engine accepts documented future phase assignment patterns that match the maintainer phase ladder, with clear errors for invalid targets
Current workspace phase remains the authoritative where-we-are signal in summaries; per-task phaseKey may exceed it for planning
Existing phase move and dashboard paths from Phase 85 remain green under test
tasks.summary: Distinguish workspace current kit phase from per-task phaseKey; validate assign-task-phase against ladder; regression tests.

tasks.title: Dashboard UX: phase selection and interaction patterns for moving tasks between phases
tasks.approach: Design and ship intuitive UX (phase picker and/or drag-drop between phase columns) aligned with dashboard-summary bucket semantics; empty states; keyboard and accessibility; follows wishlist best-practice intent.
technicalScope: Dashboard task list or sidebar presentation components
Interaction pattern implementation (picker vs drag-drop) with sensible defaults
Visual and state polish consistent with existing Workflow Cannon dashboard styling
acceptanceCriteria: Users can move tasks across phases with clear affordance (picker or drag-drop as implemented)
Future phases appear when present in engine or list surfaces rather than a fixed hard-coded phase list
Basic accessibility: focus order and labels for phase controls
tasks.summary: QuickPick-based phase move with filterable suggestions from phase buckets; Phase controls on ready/proposed/blocked rows; aria-labels; execution-queue scope footnote on Ready Execution rollup.

tasks.title: Dashboard: wire task phase reassignment to kit assign-task-phase
tasks.approach: Implement secure invocation from the Cursor Workflow Cannon dashboard/extension to run assign-task-phase (or equivalent batch path), including listing permissible phase targets, handling planning-generation preludes when mutations chain, and surfacing structured errors to the UI.
technicalScope: extensions/cursor-workflow-cannon integration path for phase mutation command(s)
Contract tests or smoke path proving phaseKey updates persist via workspace-kit
Maintainer-facing notes on phase UX constraints vs workspace phase snapshot
acceptanceCriteria: Operators can change a task phaseKey from the dashboard without manual CLI when permitted by policy
Failures show actionable kit error codes or messages in the UI surface
Task list or summary views refresh after phase change so buckets stay consistent
tasks.summary: Dashboard wires Phase row actions to assign-task-phase with planning-generation prelude, phase key suggestions from kit summaries, post-mutation dashboard refresh, and VS Code error surfaces.

tasks.title: Dashboard Plugin - Phase Management

metadata.problemStatement: THe user has no easy way to move tasks to different phases.
metadata.expectedOutcome: The user is able to transition tasks to different phases in the dashboard UI using best UX practices. Either drag and drop, or a button that opens a phase selection. Future phases should be possible and handled by the task engine.
metadata.impact: The user is not able to plan future tasks by selecting phases for them to be worked.
metadata.constraints: None.
metadata.successSignals: High quality UX exists that enables the user to changes task phases.
metadata.evidenceRef: NA

wishlistConversionDecomposition.rationale: Separate kit/extension plumbing from dashboard UX so phase moves remain auditable and reversible.
wishlistConversionDecomposition.boundaries: Does not change workspace current-phase snapshot semantics (set-current-phase); only per-task phase buckets via assign-task-phase path used by the dashboard.
wishlistConversionDecomposition.dependencyIntent: T100150 blocks T100151 — UI depends on working phase mutation contract.

tasks.title: Task Engine: Add Policy Configuration UX And End-To-End Coverage
technicalScope: Add tests covering config defaults, module overrides, task overrides, resolver precedence, and conflict warnings for maintainerDelivery.
Add tests covering intake requiredFields/recommendedFields/forbiddenFields/fieldRules and enforcement modes across create-task and accept transitions.
Add tests covering phase-delivery-preflight with mixed GitHub PR and local-reviewed-merge tasks in the same phase.
Add examples to config docs or command instructions for setting workspace default profile, module override, local review artifact path, and stricter accept requirements.
Run pnpm run check, pnpm run test, and targeted command smoke tests for the new resolver commands.
acceptanceCriteria: A default workspace still behaves like the current repo rules without additional config.
A module can choose local-reviewed-merge and complete with v2 local review evidence without GitHub PR fields.
A module can require additional task intake fields before proposed tasks are accepted to ready.
Explain output clearly identifies built-in, workspace, module, and task-level sources for policy values.
The final validation suite covers config, resolver, guard, command, and guidance behavior without relying on external network calls.
tasks.summary: Add examples, explain output, and regression coverage for configurable delivery and intake policies across config, resolver, guard, CAE, and runbook surfaces.
tasks.risk: Medium: cross-cutting tests can become brittle. Favor command-level fixtures and resolver unit tests over network-dependent or UI-heavy coverage.

metadata.goal: Add examples, explain output, and regression coverage for configurable delivery and intake policies across config, resolver, guard, CAE, and runbook surfaces.
metadata.implementationDetails: Treat this as the closing integration slice: examples, explainability, and regression tests that prove the configurable policy model works end to end without changing defaults.

tasks.title: Task Engine: Update Runbooks And Instructions For Policy Resolvers
technicalScope: Update .ai/playbooks/task-to-phase-branch.md and phase-closeout-and-release.md to reference resolve-maintainer-delivery-policy before branch/review/merge steps.
Update .cursor/rules/maintainer-delivery-loop.mdc and requestable playbook rules to describe resolver-driven defaults instead of GitHub-only non-negotiables where policy allows alternatives.
Update create-task, apply-task-batch, run-transition, phase-delivery-preflight, resolve-maintainer-delivery-policy, and resolve-task-intake-policy instruction docs.
Update .ai/MACHINE-PLAYBOOKS.md and .ai/WORKSPACE-KIT-SESSION.md summaries if their hard-coded PR language becomes stale.
Run the repo doc-generation/check commands appropriate for instruction and maintainer-doc parity.
acceptanceCriteria: Runbooks keep today's GitHub PR phase-branch flow as the default but no longer require prose edits when a module changes review or branching preference.
Task intake docs explain how required/recommended/forbidden fields are resolved and enforced.
Agent-facing docs point to resolver commands rather than asking agents to infer policy from config manually.
Generated maintainer docs remain aligned with canonical .ai sources where the repo expects parity.
Documentation checks pass or any non-applicable generated-doc steps are explicitly documented.
tasks.summary: Revise agent runbooks and task-engine instruction docs once so future preference changes are data/config changes rather than prose rewrites.
tasks.risk: Medium: these are canonical agent instructions. Changes must be concise, consistent with source-of-truth order, and validated by docs checks.

metadata.goal: Revise agent runbooks and task-engine instruction docs once so future preference changes are data/config changes rather than prose rewrites.
metadata.implementationDetails: Make one deliberate runbook update after the resolver contracts exist, changing runbooks from hard-coded policy to resolver-first instructions while preserving the default flow in examples.

tasks.title: Task Engine: Surface Intake Policy In CLI And CAE Guidance
technicalScope: Thread intake resolver summaries into readouts for proposed tasks and ready-queue triage where useful.
Update CAE task-engine guidance so create-task and accept workflows can show requiredFields and missingRequiredFields.
Add response-template or presentation fields only where existing command response architecture supports them cleanly.
Keep guidance compact: field names, source profile, and direct next action rather than long policy prose.
Ensure guidance can handle module-specific requirements such as acceptanceCriteria, goals, risk, implementationDetails, or metadata fields.
acceptanceCriteria: Agents can see required task fields before attempting to accept a proposed task.
CAE guidance appears for incomplete tasks and stays quiet for complete tasks.
CLI output identifies the intake profile and missing fields without requiring agents to inspect config files.
Guidance works for both default and module-overridden intake profiles.
No command response includes excessive catalog dumps or unrelated config details.
tasks.summary: Expose task intake requirements through CLI readouts and CAE guidance so agents know which fields to fill before creating or accepting tasks.
tasks.risk: Low to medium: the main risk is guidance noise. Trigger only when the user or agent is in a task intake/acceptance workflow.

Document that live agent activity is not delivery evidence and that resolved policy is advisory until guards enforce evidence.

Existing maintainerDelivery fields remain present for clients that only understand v1 hints.
CAE guidance tells the agent the configured branch/review/evidence path instead of hard-coding GitHub PR language.
No GitHub or external provider network lookup is introduced in dashboard-summary or get-next-actions.
Tests cover at least one default GitHub PR card and one local-reviewed-merge policy card.
tasks.summary: Thread resolved maintainer delivery policy through agent-bootstrap, get-next-actions, agent-session-snapshot, dashboard readouts, and CAE guidance cards.
tasks.risk: Low to medium: guidance changes can confuse agents if verbose or contradictory. Keep output compact and source-backed.

tasks.title: Task engine: quiet intake off-mode and complete delivery-evidence advisory
technicalScope: Short-circuit intake mutation policy and transition guard when enforcementMode is off (and confirm profile-level off semantics).
Adjust or scope delivery-evidence evaluation on run-transition complete so advisory guardResults do not imply missing PR evidence when not applicable; add focused tests.
acceptanceCriteria: With tasks.intakePolicy.enforcementMode off (and off profile mode where applicable), create-task, apply-task-batch create, and accept paths emit no intake warnings and perform no blocking intake checks.
Default and advisory configs unchanged for existing integration tests.

run-transition complete no longer surfaces advisory delivery-evidence-missing in guardResults when delivery evidence is not required by policy, OR documented behavior matches code with a single intentional advisory code.
pnpm run test passes.
tasks.summary: Close two advisory gaps: (1) tasks.intakePolicy enforcement off should not run intake resolution or emit intake warnings or noise; (2) run-transition complete should not surface misleading delivery-evidence-missing advisory when evidence is intentionally absent or policy says not required.
tasks.description:
Follow-ups from T100114 and T100113 delivery notes.

1) Intake off mode: when workspace or effective profile intake enforcement is off, skip resolve and evaluate paths on create, batch, and accept guard; keep responses minimal and add regression tests.

2) Complete transition delivery evidence: align delivery-evidence guard or presentation so complete transitions do not log advisory delivery-evidence-missing when the policy context says evidence is not required or the signal is redundant; document intended behavior for agents.

tasks.title: Task Engine: Apply Intake Policy To Create And Accept
technicalScope: Add an intake policy guard/helper to create-task command flow before persistence.
Add an intake policy transition guard for proposed to ready transitions in run-transition/task intent commands.
Ensure apply-task-batch receives consistent validation behavior for create-task ops.
Return policyProfile, enforcementMode, missingRequiredFields, missingRecommendedFields, and forbiddenPresentFields in warnings or errors.
Avoid treating generated/system fields such as id, createdAt, updatedAt, and archived as agent-fillable required fields unless explicitly supported.
acceptanceCriteria: With default config, existing valid create-task and accept flows continue to work.
With advisory intake config, missing required fields produce structured warnings but do not block.
With enforce-on-accept, incomplete proposed tasks can be created but cannot be accepted to ready until required fields are present.
With enforce, create-task status ready and proposed-task acceptance both block when configured required fields are missing.
Batch task creation reports intake warnings/errors per operation without corrupting the transaction.
tasks.summary: Wire task intake policy into create-task and run-transition accept so agents get structured warnings or blocking errors when required task information is missing.
tasks.risk: High: task creation and transition paths are central. Keep enforcement opt-in beyond current behavior and test batch rollback semantics.

metadata.goal: Wire task intake policy into create-task and run-transition accept so agents get structured warnings or blocking errors when required task information is missing.
metadata.implementationDetails: Apply the resolver at both creation and lifecycle-transition boundaries so configured task-detail expectations become operational rather than only descriptive.

tasks.risk: Medium: overly strict defaults could slow task capture. Preserve today's behavior by default and make stricter enforcement opt-in.

metadata.goal: Implement configurable task intake profiles that define required, recommended, forbidden, and validated fields for task creation and proposed-task acceptance.

run-transition complete respects delivery evidence enforcement mode exactly as before.
phase-delivery-preflight can audit mixed delivery profiles within the same phase.

Same commands run as before merge (no dropped gates).
parity job still gates after successful merged job.

tasks.summary: Combine GitHub Actions jobs test and release-readiness into a single workflow job so PR CI performs one checkout + one pnpm install while preserving the same steps and artifacts behavior.
tasks.description: Second quick win: ci.yml runs three jobs each with full install; merging test + release-readiness removes duplicate cold start on every PR. Steps should remain equivalent (build, check, test with no redundant build per sibling task, plugin smoke, cursor workspace, release metadata, pack dry-run, maintainer-gates, advisory hand-edit). Keep parity as separate job that needs the merged job.
tasks.risk: Medium

tasks.title: CI: Run tests without redundant TypeScript build
technicalScope: Add npm script that runs find test -name '*.test.mjs' | xargs node --test without calling build.
Update ci.yml test job: after build+check, invoke the new script instead of pnpm test.
Document in maintainer or AGENTS-facing note only if an existing doc promises CI behavior.
Verify full test file list matches prior pnpm test invocation.
acceptanceCriteria: CI test job runs each *.test.mjs file exactly once after a single build+check.
Local pnpm test still performs build then tests unless intentionally changed with maintainer agreement.
pnpm run check still passes; CI green on a PR exercising the workflow.
tasks.summary: Add a package script that executes the test suite without invoking pnpm run build, and wire the CI test job to use it after build+check so tsc is not run twice.
tasks.description: Quick win to shorten PR CI: package.json currently defines test as build+find|node --test while ci.yml already runs pnpm run build && pnpm check before pnpm test, causing duplicate tsc. Deliver a dedicated script (e.g. test:run or test:ci), update .github/workflows/ci.yml Test step, keep local pnpm test behavior unchanged for developers.
tasks.risk: Low

tasks.title: First-Class Human Gate States

metadata.problemStatement: Agents currently overload blocked or activity hints for states like awaiting review, awaiting policy approval, or awaiting external decision, which blurs true dependency blockers with human gates.
metadata.expectedOutcome: Add first-class task/activity semantics for human gates, including awaiting_review, awaiting_policy_approval, and awaiting_external_decision, with clear transitions back to ready/in_progress/blocked.
metadata.impact: Makes dashboards and next-action selection more honest about why work is paused and who must act next.
metadata.constraints: Use workspace-kit task-engine owned state and existing policy lanes; preserve deterministic JSON output, optimistic concurrency, idempotency, and agent routing rules; avoid requiring agents to hand-edit SQLite, JSON stores, or docs/ maintainer prose.
metadata.successSignals: Human-gated work is visible separately from dependency-blocked work, with owner, requested decision, age, and resume command.
metadata.evidenceRef: Chat request 2026-05-09: prioritized agent task-engine feature requests

tasks.title: Improvement Dedupe Explainer

metadata.problemStatement: When reviewing proposed improvements, agents must manually compare similar tasks, evidence keys, lineage, and prior decisions to decide whether to accept, merge, or reject.
metadata.expectedOutcome: Add an improvement-dedupe-explain command that surfaces similar active/completed/cancelled items, evidenceKey overlaps, lineage events, and a recommended triage action.
metadata.impact: Improves improvement backlog quality and reduces duplicate or vague feature work.
metadata.constraints: Use workspace-kit task-engine owned state and existing policy lanes; preserve deterministic JSON output, optimistic concurrency, idempotency, and agent routing rules; avoid requiring agents to hand-edit SQLite, JSON stores, or docs/ maintainer prose.
metadata.successSignals: For a proposed improvement, the engine returns similarity clusters and clear accept/merge/reject rationale with linked task ids.
metadata.evidenceRef: Chat request 2026-05-09: prioritized agent task-engine feature requests

tasks.title: Idempotency Key Registry

metadata.problemStatement: Agents know they should use clientMutationId, but keys are ad hoc and can be lost across output parsing failures or session reloads.
metadata.expectedOutcome: Add an idempotency helper/registry that issues stable recommended clientMutationIds per intended operation and records their status for retry-safe replay.
metadata.impact: Makes retries safer and easier to reason about when CLI output is lost or sessions resume mid-mutation.
metadata.constraints: Use workspace-kit task-engine owned state and existing policy lanes; preserve deterministic JSON output, optimistic concurrency, idempotency, and agent routing rules; avoid requiring agents to hand-edit SQLite, JSON stores, or docs/ maintainer prose.
metadata.successSignals: An agent can ask for or reuse the registered key for a task/action and see whether it is unused, applied, replayed, or conflicting.
metadata.evidenceRef: Chat request 2026-05-09: prioritized agent task-engine feature requests

tasks.title: Validation Recommendation Engine

metadata.problemStatement: Agents choose validation commands manually from repo habits, touched files, modules, and task metadata, which risks running too little or wasting time on redundant checks.
metadata.expectedOutcome: Add recommend-validation that suggests check/test commands based on task features, touched modules/files, package scripts, and recent delivery evidence patterns.
metadata.impact: Improves verification quality while avoiding unnecessary local and CI work.
metadata.constraints: Use workspace-kit task-engine owned state and existing policy lanes; preserve deterministic JSON output, optimistic concurrency, idempotency, and agent routing rules; avoid requiring agents to hand-edit SQLite, JSON stores, or docs/ maintainer prose.
metadata.successSignals: For a task or diff, the engine returns a prioritized validation plan with rationale and expected evidence fields.
metadata.evidenceRef: Chat request 2026-05-09: prioritized agent task-engine feature requests

tasks.title: Task Context Bundle

metadata.problemStatement: Before implementation, agents gather task details, acceptance criteria, notes, dependencies, feature links, prior transitions, and relevant module hints through multiple reads and searches.
metadata.expectedOutcome: Add get-task-context to return a bounded task-centered bundle: task body, allowed actions, dependencies, linked features/components, relevant phase notes, transition history summary, assignments, and suggested instruction files.
metadata.impact: Cuts context-gathering time and helps agents start with the right constraints in view.
metadata.constraints: Use workspace-kit task-engine owned state and existing policy lanes; preserve deterministic JSON output, optimistic concurrency, idempotency, and agent routing rules; avoid requiring agents to hand-edit SQLite, JSON stores, or docs/ maintainer prose.
metadata.successSignals: Given a task id, agents receive enough structured context to plan implementation without raw SQLite reads or broad doc searches.
metadata.evidenceRef: Chat request 2026-05-09: prioritized agent task-engine feature requests

tasks.title: Agent-Safe Remediation Links

metadata.problemStatement: Some machine-readable remediation output points agents at docs/maintainers paths even though agent routing says routine work should use .ai/ and instruction surfaces.
metadata.expectedOutcome: Update remediation contracts and command outputs so every error includes an agent-safe instructionPath or .ai path, with maintainer doc paths only as secondary human references.

tasks.title: Tiered CI: slim PR checks vs full merge pipeline

metadata.problemStatement: Every pull_request runs the full CI matrix (test + release-readiness + parity) with multiple installs, which is slow for small task PRs and burns runner minutes.
metadata.expectedOutcome: PRs use a fast tier (build, check, tests) while merge to main or release/* triggers full gates including parity, pack dry-run, and maintainer-gates; branch protection matches the policy.
metadata.impact: Faster feedback for agents and humans on task branches; release confidence unchanged where full tier runs.
metadata.constraints: Must not silently skip parity on shipping branches; branch protection and docs must stay aligned; path filters only if blast radius is acceptable.
metadata.successSignals: Median PR CI wall time drops; required checks documented; no regressions in release discipline.
metadata.evidenceRef: Discussion: CI quick wins vs tiered CI (workflow-cannon chat); .github/workflows/ci.yml structure

tasks.title: Task Engine: Detect Replaced SQLite Planning DB And Reopen Safely
tasks.approach: Track file identity for the open planning DB, cheaply detect external replacement before handle reuse, and transparently close/reopen plus reload planning state when the underlying file changes. Keep optimistic planningGeneration semantics intact, avoid broad persistence rewrites, and add a regression test that simulates one consumer holding a handle while another code path replaces the DB file.
technicalScope: Add file identity tracking to SqliteDualPlanningStore for the opened DB path and invalidate the cached Database handle when the underlying file has been replaced.
Ensure long-lived read and write paths re-open cleanly and refresh in-memory planning/task documents after replacement without bypassing existing planningGeneration concurrency checks.
Verify extension-side state watching still refreshes correctly when the SQLite file is created, changed, or replaced, and adjust watcher expectations only if needed.
Add regression coverage that simulates external DB replacement and proves a stale in-process consumer reopens the database instead of continuing on the orphaned file handle.
acceptanceCriteria: A long-lived SqliteDualPlanningStore instance detects external replacement of .workspace-kit/tasks/workspace-kit.db and reopens before subsequent reuse.
After replacement, reads reflect the new planning DB contents instead of the stale pre-replacement image.
After replacement, mutating commands either succeed against the reopened DB or fail with the existing planningGeneration mismatch semantics when the caller used a stale token.
The change is covered by a focused regression test in test/task-engine.test.mjs that fails before the fix and passes after it.
Normal single-process task-engine reads and writes continue to work without changing public command contracts.
tasks.summary: Prevent stale SQLite handles after external planning DB replacement so agents always reread current task-engine state.
tasks.description:
Implement the highest-value multi-agent hardening slice for the task engine: detect when .workspace-kit/tasks/workspace-kit.db has been replaced externally and reopen the SQLite handle before subsequent reads or writes.

Problem:
Long-lived consumers can keep using an open better-sqlite3 handle after another agent, git operation, or restore rewrites the tracked planning DB file. The task engine already has optimistic planningGeneration checks for stale writes, but it does not currently detect file replacement and refresh the open handle.

Goal:
Make task-engine consumers resilient when the planning DB file is replaced out from under them, without regressing normal command execution.

Primary implementation anchors:
- src/modules/task-engine/persistence/sqlite-dual-planning.ts
- src/modules/task-engine/persistence/planning-open.ts
- extensions/cursor-workflow-cannon/src/runtime/state-watcher.ts
- test/task-engine.test.mjs

Use the supported workspace-kit command path for any task-state mutations; do not hand-edit kit-owned SQLite state.
tasks.risk: Medium: reopening too aggressively could add churn or mask real SQLite errors; reopening too late leaves agents on stale state. Keep the detection narrow to true file replacement and cover both read and write reuse paths with focused tests.

metadata.goal: Prevent stale SQLite handles after external planning DB replacement so agents reread current task-engine state.
metadata.issue: Long-lived task-engine consumers can continue using an open better-sqlite3 handle after another agent or git operation replaces the tracked planning DB file, causing stale reads and confusing follow-on mutations.
metadata.supportingReasoning: Repository analysis on 2026-05-07 showed WAL and planningGeneration optimistic concurrency are already in place, but SqliteDualPlanningStore reuses an open Database handle until closeDatabase is called and does not verify inode or file identity before reuse. That leaves a gap when the tracked .db file is rewritten externally.
metadata.implementationDetails: Prefer a narrow fix in SqliteDualPlanningStore: record file identity when opening, check for replacement before reusing the cached handle, close/reopen plus reload task/wishlist documents when the file changed, and add a regression test for stale-handle recovery. Keep planningGeneration enforcement unchanged.

localValidation: node --test --test-name-pattern 'external planning DB replacement|planningGenerationPolicy require rejects wrong' test/task-engine.test.mjs
implementation: Tracked open planning DB file identity and reopened long-lived stores when the DB path is replaced.
Refreshed cached task/wishlist documents on stale reads and deferred stale-token reloads without changing planningGeneration mismatch semantics.
Preserved the replacement DB while closing stale SQLite handles so old WAL state cannot overwrite the new file.
Added a focused regression in test/task-engine.test.mjs for replacement under a long-lived SqliteDualPlanningStore.
deliveryEvidence.backfillRationale: Phase 82 closeout preflight requires full delivery evidence fields for completed PR-delivered tasks.

tasks.title: Design agent presentation policy contract and resolver
technicalScope: Add src/core/agent-presentation-policy.ts with exported types and resolveAgentPresentationPolicy().
Add defaults under agentPresentation in src/core/workspace-kit-config.ts.
Add config metadata for agentPresentation.mode, workLog, rationale, technicality, and finalAnswerDetail in src/core/config-registry.json.
Use existing role/tier helpers from agent-guidance-catalog and behavior profile dimensions; do not create a parallel role or temperament model.
Make private reasoning disclosure a non-configurable invariant in the resolver output, e.g. privateReasoning: never_disclose.
acceptanceCriteria: Resolved policy is deterministic and fully covered by unit tests for role tier, temperament nudges, and explicit override precedence.
Config validation accepts only known enum values and explain-config/resolve-config surfaces the new keys.
No config option can enable chain-of-thought disclosure or instruct the agent not to reason internally.
Default behavior preserves current balanced presentation when no new config is set.
tasks.summary: Define the normalized presentation policy that controls visible work-log cadence, rationale summary style, technicality, and final-answer detail without ever requesting private chain-of-thought.
tasks.description: Create the core contract and pure resolver for Workflow Cannon agent presentation policy. The resolver must combine baseline config, kit.agentGuidance role/tier, active agentBehavior temperament dimensions, and explicit overrides into one deterministic policy object usable by generated rules, dashboards, and command metadata.
tasks.risk: If role tier is mapped too aggressively, users may see surprising verbosity changes. Keep derivation conservative and let explicit overrides win.

metadata.goal: Create the stable baseline policy layer for agent-visible presentation behavior.
metadata.implementationDetails: Prefer a pure resolver in core with no file IO. Return both machine fields and a short generated instruction snippet that downstream surfaces can embed.
metadata.blockers: Needs agreement on final enum names before downstream tasks consume the contract.
metadata.supportingReasoning: This is the root contract for the final plan; doing it first prevents generated rules, dashboard UI, and CAE integrations from inventing separate presentation semantics.

deliveryWaiver.rationale: Phase 80 implementation landed before the current deliveryEvidence schema was enforced; it is included in the v0.81.0 release closeout and covered by the combined release validation gates.

tasks.title: Sync presentation policy into always-applied agent instructions

Render a stable Presentation Policy section into .cursor/rules/workflow-cannon-effective-agent-behavior.mdc.
Use terms visible work-log and rationale summary; avoid user-facing labels like show thoughts.
Keep existing role/tier and temperament sections intact and subordinate to PRINCIPLES and policy gates.
Ensure auto-sync after set-agent-guidance and behavior profile mutations refreshes the presentation section.
acceptanceCriteria: Generated Cursor rule includes the resolved presentation policy and the private-reasoning invariant.
Rule output changes when relevant config, role tier, or active temperament profile changes.
Rule output does not expose or request chain-of-thought language.
Existing behavior-rule tests are updated or new tests cover the rendered presentation section.
tasks.summary: Make the resolved presentation policy available at chat start by embedding it in the generated effective behavior Cursor rule.
tasks.description: Extend the agent-behavior rule sync path so agents receive presentation instructions before they begin work, not only after running a diagnostic command. The generated rule must describe visible work-log, rationale summary, technicality, and final-answer detail while preserving the invariant that private reasoning is not revealed.
tasks.risk: If the policy is only surfaced in dashboard or command output, agents will receive it too late. This task must preserve early instruction timing.

metadata.goal: Provide optimal instruction timing for presentation behavior.
metadata.implementationDetails: Use the existing generated Cursor rule path as the primary instruction surface instead of adding a second rule file unless tests show separation is cleaner.
metadata.blockers: Depends on T100049 resolver contract and enum names.
metadata.supportingReasoning: Always-applied rules are the only existing Workflow Cannon surface that reliably instructs the chat agent before visible updates begin.

Avoid making responseTemplates the source of truth for chat instruction timing.

tasks.risk: Operators may think dashboard settings directly control already-running chat behavior. Copy and docs must state that generated rules are the instruction source.

metadata.goal: Give operators visibility into the active presentation policy.

tasks.title: Add CAE-scoped presentation override guidance
technicalScope: Define a documented CAE artifact pattern for presentation guidance using existing family/scope semantics, likely think or do family depending on whether it changes explanation style or execution updates.
Add curated example draft rules for onboarding simple-language mode, run-transition complete technical evidence mode, and sensitive-command remediation mode.
Ensure cae-guidance-preview can show blast radius for broad presentation overrides before activation.
Document conflict posture when multiple presentation rules match; prefer highest-priority CAE rule as advisory text rather than mutating baseline config.
Keep CAE overrides advisory unless existing CAE enforcement allowlists explicitly govern the underlying operation.
acceptanceCriteria: At least three scoped CAE presentation guidance examples are available in docs or registry fixtures without enabling broad global behavior by default.
Preview output demonstrates whether a draft presentation rule matches the primary command and representative samples.
Broad always-on presentation overrides produce warnings through existing scope/blast-radius machinery.
Documentation explains when to use config baseline versus CAE scoped override.
tasks.summary: Use CAE for situational presentation overrides such as onboarding, release closeout, policy failures, and dashboard-triggered flows.
tasks.description: Define how CAE guidance can temporarily narrow or raise presentation detail for a specific task, command, workflow, phase, or tag. Baseline config remains global; CAE owns contextual exceptions with preview, priority, lifecycle, and conflict behavior.
tasks.risk: A broad CAE rule could unexpectedly override all chats. Scope warnings and examples should bias toward workflow/command/task-scoped rules.

metadata.goal: Put situational presentation behavior in CAE, where scope and blast radius are already modeled.
metadata.implementationDetails: Start with patterns and fixtures before adding new CAE schema fields; only add schema if existing artifact text is insufficient for reliable instruction generation.
metadata.blockers: Needs T100049 terminology to avoid conflicting names between baseline policy and CAE guidance artifacts.
metadata.supportingReasoning: CAE is the existing architecture for contextual guidance and should own exceptions instead of expanding config into a large rule matrix.

Presentation metadata does not become the only instruction source for chat behavior.

tasks.summary: Attach presentation metadata to CLI outputs without making response templates responsible for chat instruction timing.

tasks.title: Persist short-lived WC Agent activity leases
technicalScope: Define a schema-versioned AgentActivity record with agentId, sessionId, kind, label, startedAt, updatedAt, expiresAt, optional taskId, command, phaseKey, prNumber, version, and details.
Persist activity in the existing planning SQLite/module-state model where practical, or add a small kit_agent_activity table if migration/versioning is cleaner.

Add focused commands or internal APIs to set, clear, heartbeat, and read current activity; reads should be non-sensitive and writes should follow normal planning-generation/idempotency patterns when exposed through wk run.
Update StateWatcher patterns only if activity does not live in the existing watched planning SQLite path.
Merge fresh live activity into dashboard-summary.agentStatus with source live_activity and fall back to derived status after expiresAt.
acceptanceCriteria: A fresh activity lease can override derived status in dashboard-summary with source live_activity.
Expired activity leases are ignored and do not keep showing stale PR/release/task labels.
The activity store records enough identity to avoid unrelated sessions clobbering each other accidentally.
Tests cover set, heartbeat, clear, expiry, corrupt/unknown records, and fallback to derived status.
The persistence map or command documentation explains where activity leases live.
tasks.summary: Add an explicit activity lease store so live statuses like Reviewing Pull Request 192 or Releasing Build 0.9.1 can be tracked without stale claims.
tasks.description: Create the durable but expiring live-activity layer that supplements derived dashboard status. Activity records should be leases with updatedAt/expiresAt so the UI can fall back to derived state when an agent session disappears.
tasks.risk: Persistent live status can become stale or misleading if it has no TTL. The store must expire records and expose freshness explicitly.

metadata.goal: Enable the next-step statuses that cannot be inferred safely from existing task state alone.
metadata.implementationDetails: Use leases rather than permanent state. Keep labels generated from structured fields where possible, not arbitrary long free text.
metadata.blockers: Depends on T100056 so live activity and derived activity share one dashboard contract.
metadata.supportingReasoning: PR review and release work are workflow activities, not task lifecycle states; they need explicit short-lived instrumentation to be trustworthy.

deliveryEvidence.summary: Added kit_agent_activity_leases, internal set/heartbeat/clear/read helpers, live_activity dashboard overlay, documentation, and focused store/migration/dashboard tests.

tasks.title: Instrument CLI and extension flows to update WC Agent activity
technicalScope: Add a small activity recorder helper that command handlers and extension code can call without duplicating label logic.

acceptanceCriteria: Starting or claiming a task produces a fresh Working on Task T### dashboard status.
Completing, cancelling, or pausing the current task clears or downgrades the live activity so the dashboard does not stay stuck on old work.
Planning wizard actions produce a Planning status while active and clear on completion/discard.
Failures to record activity are best-effort and never fail the underlying task mutation or dashboard action unless an explicit activity command is being tested.
Tests cover both CLI-side and extension-side instrumentation paths with mocked command clients where needed.
tasks.summary: Wire real workflow events into the activity lease store from CLI command boundaries, task transitions, dashboard actions, and planning flows.

tasks.summary: Subscribe to existing kit state change events with a debounced pushUpdate; flush immediately on manual refresh and panel visibility; document timing constant.
tasks.description:
extensions/cursor-workflow-cannon

Goals:
- When StateWatcher / onKitStateChanged fires, schedule dashboard-summary refetch with debounce (recommended 300–800ms constant in one place, e.g. STATUS_PANEL_DEBOUNCE_MS).
- On debounced fire, call the same refresh path as the Refresh button.
- Immediate refresh (no wait) when: user clicks Refresh; panel transitions to visible (onDidChangeWebviewPanelViewState optional); first open.
- Ensure rapid file churn does not spawn parallel overlapping wk processes — cancel or ignore superseded refresh if one is in flight (choose minimal strategy: flag or abort controller).
- Singleton: reopening the palette command reveals the existing panel instead of stacking duplicates.

Implementation notes:
- Hook next to where DashboardViewProvider uses onKitStateChanged; share emitter or add narrow subscription for status panel only.
- Optional: WORKSPACE_KIT_DEBUG_EXTENSION logs when debounce drops vs fires.

tasks.title: CAE: phase-scoped activations + runbook for Phase Journal workflow
tasks.approach: Phase 1: registry artifact + phaseKey-scoped activations with priorities that coexist with pilot policy activations. Phase 2 (optional): bounded context hydration behind schema version gate + tests.
technicalScope: .ai/cae/registry/artifacts.v1.json — new artifact row + markdown body under allowed ref.path (or inline per registry schema)
.ai/cae/registry/activations.v1.json — new activation(s): family think/do, scope phaseKey (+ optional commandName), artifactRefs, flags shadowEligible/advisoryOnly as appropriate
schemas/cae/activation-definition.schema.json — only if new fields required (prefer reusing existing activation shape)
src/core/cae/cae-registry-load.ts / validate paths — only if loader changes
Optional: src/core/cae/evaluation-context-builder.ts + schemas/cae/evaluation-context.v1.json + fixtures for phaseJournalPreview

Documentation: .ai/runbooks or .ai/cae short cross-link from PHASE_JOURNAL.md "CAE integration" subsection (one short paragraph + pointer to registry IDs) — keep under agent-doc routing (machine paths)
CI: existing check stages that validate CAE registry and evaluation-context fixtures must pass
acceptanceCriteria: Registry validates: cae-registry-validate (or equivalent repo check) passes with new artifact + activation rows committed

At least one activation matches evaluation contexts where workspace currentKitPhase equals the target phase and task.phaseKey is absent (fallback) OR present — behavior documented in task description comments or .ai/cae doc
Artifact text lists concrete wk run JSON shapes or points to .ai/agent-cli-snippets for add-phase-note, get-phase-context, run-transition phaseNotes (no fictional command names)
Activations use stable activationId and priority ordering documented so Phase 79 bundle does not silently override higher-priority pilot policy activations without explicit maintainer review

Tests: extend fixtures/cae/evaluate or cae-evaluate unit tests to assert activationMatches for a synthetic context { task.phaseKey: 79, command.name: run-transition } surfaces new bundle entry when registry loaded (or add focused test in existing test/cae*.test.mjs pattern)
If evaluation context extension ships: schema + builder + at least one valid/invalid fixture; bundleId / hash stability tests updated per T860 canonicalization rules
CHANGELOG or maintainer-facing note not required unless release cuts same PR — optional docs/maintainers line only if maintainer asks
tasks.summary: Wire Context Activation (CAE) so operators and agents working under a given workspace phase consistently receive bounded guidance to read/write Phase Journal via task-engine commands — without implying CAE executes wk run automatically.
tasks.description:
Context: Phase Journal (task-engine) persists phase notes and surfaces them via get-phase-context, agent-session-snapshot.phaseJournal, get-next-actions.phaseContext, and optional run-transition.phaseNotes. CAE today matches activations on evaluation context (including task.phaseKey with workspace phase fallback) but does not hydrate journal text into evaluation context (see .ai/cae/evaluation-context.md and src/core/cae/evaluation-context-builder.ts).

Deliverable A (registry-only, ship first): Add a CAE artifact (runbook or short playbook ref) that documents the operator/agent checklist: when to run get-phase-context, add-phase-note, propose/convert, dismiss/supersede; caps and idempotency; Tier A/B policyApproval reminders for mutators. Register one or more activations in .ai/cae/registry/activations.v1.json scoped with { kind: phaseKey, value: <N> } for the active phase(es) and optional { kind: commandName, match: prefix, value: run- } so think/do/review families fire during normal wk run usage. Mirror patterns from cae.activation.policy.phase70-playbook and cae.activation.think.run-transition. Ensure new artifactIds exist in artifacts.v1.json; run cae-registry-validate / existing CI gates.

Deliverable B (optional follow-on in same task if scope fits, else child task): Extend CAE evaluation context v2 OR add a documented preflight advisory path that attaches a bounded phaseJournalPreview (phaseKey + top N summaries only) built from the same projection logic as agent-session-snapshot — must satisfy evaluation-context contract rules (no raw SQLite, no full argv, metadata allowlist discipline). If schema bump: update schemas/cae/evaluation-context.v1.json (or v2), fixtures under fixtures/cae/evaluation-context/, evaluation-context-builder.ts, .ai/cae/evaluation-context.md, and any CAE explain/trace expectations.

Explicit non-goals: Do not auto-invoke add-phase-note or get-phase-context from CAE evaluate (no hidden side effects). Do not store unbounded note bodies in CAE traces. Do not bypass task-engine command policy tiers.
tasks.risk: Medium-low — registry mistakes can spam CAE surfaces or mis-scope phase keys; mitigate with narrow command prefixes, advisory flags, and shadowEligible pilot. Context hydration increases payload size — must cap strictly.

testStrategy: Unit: extend existing CAE evaluate tests (see test/*cae* and fixtures/cae/bundles) to load registry fixture or production registry slice and assert activationMatches + merged bundle includes new activation for synthetic CaeEvaluationContext with phaseKey 79 and commandName run-transition / get-phase-context.
Contract: cae-registry-validate CLI or script used in pnpm run check must pass after JSON edits.

If builder/schema extended: add fixtures/cae/evaluation-context/valid/… with phaseJournalPreview small array; invalid oversized or secret-shaped payloads rejected; Ajv tests mirror existing T859 patterns.
Regression: run pnpm run check && pnpm run test (or pre-merge-gates) before PR; optional parity unchanged unless core touched broadly.
Manual: wk run cae-evaluate with dry/shadow and trace explain — operator verifies artifact text renders and does not duplicate policy noise.

tasks.title: Native SQLite: extension Node runtime remediation
tasks.approach: Capture structured diagnostics for each Node candidate considered by the extension, including executable path, version, arch, and better-sqlite3 probe error classification. Surface a concise remediation that matches CLI doctor guidance.
acceptanceCriteria: When better-sqlite3 cannot load in the extension runtime, the user sees which Node executable was selected and why it failed.
Extension remediation suggests using the same Node architecture that installed node_modules and points to WORKSPACE_KIT_NODE or workflowCannon.nodeExecutable.

Extension docs align with CLI native SQLite recovery language.

tasks.description: Cursor can launch workspace-kit with a Node executable that differs from the runtime used to install node_modules. Improve the extension runtime selection and remediation path when native SQLite probing fails for all candidates.

goals: Reduce attached-workspace confusion when Cursor launches workspace-kit with an incompatible Node.
Keep extension behavior consistent with CLI doctor diagnostics.
Help users repair runtime selection without deleting task data.
implementationDetails: Capture candidate probe diagnostics instead of returning only the first compatible Node or fallback.
Expose concise remediation in dashboard/status views.
Update command-client tests for architecture/native load failure messaging.

tasks.title: Native SQLite: runtime architecture diagnostics
tasks.approach: Extend native SQLite load checks so architecture mismatch, missing binding, and Node ABI mismatch are classified separately. Surface Node path, process.version, process.arch, platform, expected binding path, and concise recovery steps in postinstall, doctor, and storage-read-error output.
acceptanceCriteria: Architecture mismatch messages such as have arm64 need x86_64 are classified distinctly from generic ABI mismatch.
doctor reports active Node path, Node version, process.arch, platform, and native SQLite remediation when better-sqlite3 cannot load.
Postinstall recovery logs explain when rebuild is attempted and which install root/runtime is being used.

tasks.summary: Classify and remediate better-sqlite3 architecture drift before users hit opaque native binding errors.
tasks.description: Workflow Cannon can install better-sqlite3 under one Node architecture and later run workspace-kit under another, especially on Apple Silicon systems with arm64 and Rosetta x64 Node installs. Add first-class diagnostics and remediation for this runtime identity drift.

goals: Prevent opaque better-sqlite3 native binding failures.
Give developers and consumers actionable diagnostics when Node architecture drifts.
Keep SQLite as default while improving recovery on mixed-architecture machines.
implementationDetails: Add a reusable native addon error classifier.
Thread classifier output into ensure-native-sqlite and doctor persistence checks.
Update .ai and maintainer native SQLite recovery docs with architecture-specific recovery.
blockers: Need to decide whether classifier lives in scripts only or shared core code used by doctor.

tasks.title: Defect: generate-document for README.md overwrites curated .ai/README.md (chat_feature source) by default
tasks.summary: Documentation module uses a two-line AI stub as aiOutput when overwriteAi is true, so it never reads existing .ai/README.md and then writeFile replaces the keyed source.
tasks.description: In src/modules/documentation/runtime.ts, generateDocument seeds aiOutput with a minimal stub. Existing .ai/README.md is only read when existsSync(aiOutputPath) && !canOverwriteAi. Default options set overwrite/overwriteAi to true, so a normal generate-document for README.md clobbers chat_feature| records. Human README injection (injectReadmeChatFeaturesFromNormalized) then uses the parsed stub (empty chat features). Fix options: default overwriteAi false for README.md, or always merge existing AI file for README before validation, or refuse to write AI surface when stub would drop chat_feature records. Workaround: pass options.overwriteAi false and overwriteHuman/overwriteRepoRootReadme as needed.

metadata.workaround: Use generate-document with options.overwriteAi false to preserve .ai/README.md; use dryRun to preview.
