tasks.title: Single operator path: advance phase snapshot and kit.currentPhaseLabel

metadata.problemStatement: After release, advancing the workspace phase required separate config set for kit.currentPhaseLabel so doctor stayed green; easy to miss as two-step tribal knowledge.
metadata.expectedOutcome: One documented workflow or command updates phase snapshot YAML and kit phase label fields together, with doctor as the success check.
metadata.impact: Phase rollover after publish is repeatable with fewer stale-label doctor failures.
metadata.constraints: Respect policy tiers for update-workspace-phase-snapshot and config mutations; may compose existing commands.
metadata.successSignals: Runbook or AGENT-CLI-MAP copy-paste covers the combined path; doctor clean after advance without hunting config keys.
metadata.evidenceRef: phase_64_release.md — post-release snapshot + kit.currentPhaseLabel alignment (2026-04-07 export)

tasks.title: Phase closeout messaging: synthesize features from CHANGELOG and tasks

metadata.problemStatement: The phase-closeout template used placeholder feature bullets; the agent correctly refused to invent shipped features. Operators still need a fast, honest summary for chat or release posts.
metadata.expectedOutcome: A documented or automated path (prompt, generate-document slice, or small CLI) assembles feature bullets from CHANGELOG or release notes plus completed task titles for a phaseKey, without hallucination.
metadata.impact: Phase announcements stay evidence-backed and faster to produce.
metadata.constraints: Must not bypass RELEASING evidence gates; output should cite or trace sources.
metadata.successSignals: Playbook or command produces a starter markdown block; empty state is explicit when no source data exists.
metadata.evidenceRef: phase_64_release.md — section 7 template and refusal to invent features (2026-04-07 export)

tasks.title: Reduce merge-before-green-CI risk on phase integration PRs

metadata.problemStatement: A feature PR into the phase train merged while CI was still running; follow-up was to eyeball the run. Fast merges can hide regressions.
metadata.expectedOutcome: Branch protection or documented maintainer gate requires green required checks before merge, or kit surfaces a post-merge CI verification step tied to the PR.
metadata.impact: Fewer regressions on phase trains and cleaner release evidence.
metadata.constraints: Respect forks and maintainer workflow; automation only where GitHub and policy allow.
metadata.successSignals: Playbook or tooling references the gate; operators are not relying on chat-only reminders to verify CI.
metadata.evidenceRef: phase_64_release.md — PR #82 merged while CI still running (2026-04-07 export)

tasks.title: Phase closeout guardrail: phaseKey work vs git merge story

metadata.problemStatement: Git showed release/phase-64 already merged into main while the task store still had six Phase 64 execution tasks in ready, so playbook closeout gates and reality diverged.
metadata.expectedOutcome: Doctor, a closeout command, or playbook-mandatory checks warn or block when non-terminal execution tasks remain for a phaseKey while the phase train is treated as merged to main, with an explicit remediation path.
metadata.impact: Maintainers cannot accidentally treat a phase as shipped while runnable work still sits in the queue for that phase.
metadata.constraints: Avoid noisy false positives for intentionally deferred work; allow maintainer override with documented policyApproval where needed.
metadata.successSignals: Documented operator path; at least one automated or checklist signal catches the drift class from the transcript.
metadata.evidenceRef: phase_64_release.md — Phase 64 ready-task counts vs merged branch (2026-04-07 export)

tasks.title: list-tasks: accept common status alias or fail closed on typo

metadata.problemStatement: During Phase 64 closeout, filtering list-tasks with status complete returned zero rows; the engine expects completed. Operators and agents can silently conclude no work remains.
metadata.expectedOutcome: CLI accepts complete as an alias for completed, or returns a clear validation error that names valid status values.
metadata.impact: Prevents false closeout conclusions and wasted debugging during phase and release checks.
metadata.constraints: Stay backward compatible with existing scripts; prefer explicit errors over silent coercion when ambiguous.
metadata.successSignals: Documented behavior in task-engine instructions; tests cover alias or error path; no silent empty filters for this typo.
metadata.evidenceRef: phase_64_release.md — Cursor note on complete vs completed (2026-04-07 export)

tasks.title: TEST-IMPROVEMENT: Verify GitHub branch protection required checks

metadata.problemStatement: Release policy assumes green gates before merge/publish, but required status checks are a GitHub org/repo setting—not provable from YAML alone—so process docs can drift from enforcement.
metadata.expectedOutcome: Maintainer verifies main (and release/phase-* if applicable) requires CI jobs test, release-readiness, and parity (or documented equivalents); capture check names and a verification note in machine or maintainer evidence (not chat-only).
metadata.impact: Closes the gap between documented discipline and actual merge blocking.
metadata.constraints: Cannot commit branch protection JSON without admin APIs; outcome may be a checklist + screenshot/issue link or org policy reference—keep scope honest.
metadata.successSignals: GitHub branch protection lists required checks matching CI; contributors cannot merge with failing test/release-readiness/parity.
metadata.evidenceRef: Transcript 2026-04-08; .github/workflows/ci.yml job ids test, release-readiness, parity

tasks.title: TEST-IMPROVEMENT: Node.js version matrix in CI

metadata.problemStatement: All workflows pin Node 22 only; upcoming Node LTS/current changes can break before developers notice.
metadata.expectedOutcome: CI matrix includes Node 22 plus at least one additional supported version (e.g. current LTS or next) for key jobs; engines/package metadata stay aligned.
metadata.impact: Forward-compat signal without abandoning a primary supported Node.
metadata.constraints: Keep matrix small to control minutes; native module prebuilds must exist for chosen versions; document supported Node range in machine or maintainer canon if it changes.
metadata.successSignals: Actions UI shows multiple Node versions; breakage on non-primary Node fails CI.
metadata.evidenceRef: Transcript 2026-04-08; .github/workflows/ci.yml node-version 22; package engines if any

tasks.title: TEST-IMPROVEMENT: Multi-OS CI matrix (Ubuntu + macOS + Windows)

metadata.problemStatement: CI jobs use ubuntu-latest only; better-sqlite3 and path semantics differ across OS—CLI users on macOS/Windows are not continuously validated.
metadata.expectedOutcome: Primary workflow runs build+test (and optionally parity subset) on ubuntu-latest, macos-latest, and windows-latest with pnpm frozen install; document any justified skips.
metadata.impact: Reduces shipped surprises on non-Linux developer machines.
metadata.constraints: Windows native build for better-sqlite3 must succeed in GHA; watch job time and caching; parity artifacts paths may need OS-specific handling.
metadata.successSignals: CI matrix shows three OS lanes green on main; failure on one OS blocks required checks.
metadata.evidenceRef: Transcript 2026-04-08; .github/workflows/ci.yml runs-on ubuntu-latest; dependency better-sqlite3

tasks.title: TEST-IMPROVEMENT: Scheduled CI (nightly or weekly)

metadata.problemStatement: CI is push/PR/workflow_dispatch only; native better-sqlite3, pnpm lockfile drift, and GitHub runner image changes can break main without a merge event.
metadata.expectedOutcome: A schedule trigger runs the same or a slimmed CI smoke (build, test, parity or subset) on a cadence; failures notify maintainers.
metadata.impact: Earlier detection of environment and dependency rot; complements event-driven gates.
metadata.constraints: Avoid burning minutes: consider matrix subset or nightly full / weekly full; document in .ai or maintainer runbook if jobs differ from PR CI.
metadata.successSignals: GitHub Actions shows scheduled workflow runs; a simulated break (e.g. deprecated runner) would surface without a PR.
metadata.evidenceRef: Transcript 2026-04-08; .github/workflows/ci.yml on: push/pull_request only

tasks.title: TEST-IMPROVEMENT: Run Cursor extension tests in CI

metadata.problemStatement: .github/workflows/ci.yml runs cursor-workflow-cannon check+compile only; the extension package defines pnpm test (compile + node:test) but those tests never run in CI, so dashboard/UI regressions can ship while root kit stays green.
metadata.expectedOutcome: CI job invokes pnpm --filter cursor-workflow-cannon test (or equivalent) on push/PR alongside existing extension check/compile; failures block merge.
metadata.impact: Closes the biggest product-surface testing hole without changing release philosophy.
metadata.constraints: Headless VS Code test harness may be heavy—prefer minimal node:test paths already in extensions/cursor-workflow-cannon/test; keep job time bounded.
metadata.successSignals: CI test job logs show extension tests executed; a deliberate failing extension test would fail CI.
metadata.evidenceRef: Transcript 2026-04-08; .github/workflows/ci.yml lines 41-42; extensions/cursor-workflow-cannon/package.json scripts.test

tasks.title: TEST-IMPROVEMENT: Coverage instrumentation and CI thresholds

metadata.problemStatement: Root test suite exercises built dist via node:test but there is no collected or enforced coverage, so release risk is judged by green tests only—not by measured surface area.
metadata.expectedOutcome: CI collects coverage (e.g. c8 or Node coverage flags), stores or summarizes artifacts, and fails on configurable thresholds or regressions for the workspace-kit package (and optionally scoped paths).
metadata.impact: Turns “probably tested enough” into evidence-backed governance aligned with PRINCIPLES (correctness, operability).
metadata.constraints: Must stay compatible with ESM, dist-first tests, and pnpm; avoid flaking on native better-sqlite3 paths; do not weaken existing gates.
metadata.successSignals: package.json or workflow runs coverage; CI fails when coverage drops below threshold; maintainer can see untested hotspots from CI output or artifacts.
metadata.evidenceRef: Transcript testing review 2026-04-08; root package.json test script; .github/workflows/ci.yml

tasks.title: CLI: richer error payloads with copy-paste next argv (schema + remediation)

metadata.problemStatement: Contract sprawl across instructions, pilot schemas, and modules forces agents to run --schema-only after failures. principal-architectural-review-themes notes parallel schema surfaces.
metadata.expectedOutcome: On invalid-run-args and selected stable errors, success:false JSON includes suggestedNextCommand or full suggested argv object; tighten default agent path in AGENT-CLI-MAP to minimal command ladder.
metadata.impact: Less trial-and-error; faster recovery without opening multiple instruction files.
metadata.constraints: Must not leak secrets in suggested argv; keep remediation instructionPath; optional strict size cap on hints.
metadata.successSignals: Contract tests for error envelope; docs updated; sample failures show copy-paste line in stdout JSON.
metadata.evidenceRef: Chat: agent CLI completeness 2026-04-07; .ai/runbooks/principal-architectural-review-themes.md

tasks.title: CLI: explicit reconciliation mutators for queue-git drift (no hand-editing)

metadata.problemStatement: queue-git-alignment and similar surfaces report drift between git and task engine but do not fix state. Agents may be tempted to hand-edit SQLite or JSON to reconcile.
metadata.expectedOutcome: For each safe mechanical reconciliation, provide an explicit mutating command gated by policyApproval (e.g. complete when merge invariants satisfied) instead of expanding silent auto-fix or manual store edits.
metadata.impact: Drift gets resolved through auditable CLI evidence; maintains trust boundaries.
metadata.constraints: Each mutator needs strict preconditions; default remains read-only diagnostics; no bypass of Tier A completion semantics.
metadata.successSignals: Runbook lists when to use reconcile command vs human decision; stable error codes when preconditions fail.
metadata.evidenceRef: Chat: agent CLI completeness 2026-04-07; queue-git-alignment; agent-task-engine-ergonomics.md

tasks.title: CLI: first-class playbook/workflow and batch transition commands

metadata.problemStatement: Maintainer playbooks (task-to-phase-branch, improvement triage, wishlist intake) are documented as long command sequences. Agents must re-derive order from markdown and issue many separate wk run calls with repeated policyApproval payloads.
metadata.expectedOutcome: Stable workflow command IDs (e.g. run-playbook-step) or documented composites; optional run-transition-batch for bounded Tier A actions (e.g. accept up to three improvements) under one policyApproval and one SQLite transaction.
metadata.impact: Fewer round trips, fewer ordering mistakes, clearer evidence in transition logs.
metadata.constraints: Tier A/B policy lanes unchanged; batch operations must reject partial illegal mixes; document idempotency and planning generation for batches.
metadata.successSignals: ADR or runbook lists supported workflows; pilot schemas; AGENT-CLI-MAP copy-paste for triage/delivery happy paths.
metadata.evidenceRef: Chat: agent CLI completeness 2026-04-07; .ai/playbooks/task-to-phase-branch.md

tasks.title: CLI: opaque planning read tokens (replace manual expectedPlanningGeneration threading)

metadata.problemStatement: Under tasks.planningGenerationPolicy=require, agents must read planningGeneration from list-tasks/get-next-actions/get-task, copy the integer into every mutating command, and retry on planning-generation-mismatch. That is extra round trips and error-prone bookkeeping for LLM agents.
metadata.expectedOutcome: Successful reads return a short-lived planningReadToken (or opaque blob embedding planningGeneration + store revision). Mutating commands accept the token and resolve expectedPlanningGeneration server-side. Retry story becomes: re-read, new token, retry mutate—no manual integer copy.
metadata.impact: Fewer failed mutations, simpler agent prompts, same optimistic-concurrency guarantees.
metadata.constraints: Must preserve Tier A/B policyApproval semantics; tokens must be bounded/expiring to avoid stale writes; document interaction with clientMutationId idempotent replays.
metadata.successSignals: ADR + task-engine implementation; AGENT-CLI-MAP documents token flow; extension/dashboard callers updated if they pass generation today.
metadata.evidenceRef: Chat: agent CLI completeness discussion 2026-04-07; .ai/AGENT-CLI-MAP.md planning generation lap

tasks.title: Planning: align operator docs with shipped build-plan behavior

metadata.problemStatement: src/modules/planning/README.md still claims future Phase 17 work that is already implemented. .ai/runbooks/planning-workflow.md omits outputMode tasks and persistTasks. Response code names in the runbook do not match build-plan instruction codes (e.g. planning-wishlist-ready vs planning-ready). Operators get a false picture of capabilities and finalize semantics.
metadata.expectedOutcome: README, planning-workflow.md, and cross-links (extension playbook prompt if needed) match current CLI codes, config knobs, and output modes; optional short TERMS pointer for planning module vs persistence.
metadata.impact: Reduces wrong assumptions and support churn for humans and agents following .ai runbooks.
metadata.constraints: Follow agent-doc-routing: prefer .ai and module instructions; avoid expanding docs/ unless maintainer edit.
metadata.successSignals: PR updates the listed files; pnpm run check passes; no stale Phase 17 scaffold wording where behavior shipped.
metadata.evidenceRef: chat:planning-module-ux-gaps 2026-04-07

tasks.title: Planning: shared or team-visible in-progress sessions

metadata.problemStatement: build-plan session state lives only in a gitignored file under .workspace-kit/planning/. Teammates and CI cannot see an interview in progress; there is no shared resume surface beyond copy-paste CLI.
metadata.expectedOutcome: Operators can choose a persistence strategy (e.g. optional committed snapshot, team-scoped row in planning SQLite, or documented export) so planning interviews are visible to collaborators when the workspace policy allows.
metadata.impact: Reduces duplicate planning work and makes handoffs between humans/agents reliable.
metadata.constraints: Must not leak sensitive answer payloads to dashboard-summary unless explicitly designed; respect gitignore defaults for secrets; any SQLite shape must honor tasks.planningGenerationPolicy.
metadata.successSignals: Documented operator path for shared visibility; optional implementation behind config flag; contract tests for redaction if dashboard exposes metadata only.
metadata.evidenceRef: chat:planning-module-ux-gaps 2026-04-07

tasks.title: Audit: normalize `ok`/`code`/`data` envelope across `wk run` command handlers
tasks.approach: Inventory -> document contract -> shared wrapper -> migrate high-traffic commands first -> tests.
technicalScope: Run router + module handlers inventory
Shared response helper + migration of outliers
Tests or script: assert ok field presence for representative commands
.ai runbook or AGENT-CLI-MAP note
acceptanceCriteria: Documented envelope contract referenced from AGENT-CLI-MAP
Representative Tier C read and Tier A/B write paths return consistent ok/code on sampled tests
Grandfathered exceptions listed with remediation timeline or waiver rationale
tasks.summary: Agents expect a consistent JSON contract; audit all run command responses for `ok`, `code`, and error shapes, then fix outliers and add contract tests or schema checks.
tasks.description:
## Goal
Reduce 'did it work?' heuristics: success and failure should be predictable across modules.

## Implementation strategy
- Inventory run command handlers; classify responses with/without `ok`, string vs object errors.
- Define a minimal shared contract (document in task-engine or cli module): success always `ok: true`; failure `ok: false` + stable `code`.
- Migrate outliers incrementally (feature-flag or module-by-module) to avoid big-bang risk.

## Technical implementation
- Shared helper `respondRunResult` or typed wrapper in run router.
- Deprecation notes for any legacy shape.

## Test changes
- Contract tests per module or sampling integration tests; CI gate optional (lint script).

## Acceptance criteria
1. Written contract doc under `.ai/` or `src/modules/task-engine` describing run JSON envelope.
2. Outlier commands listed and either fixed or explicitly grandfathered with issue link.
3. New commands use the shared helper (enforced by review or lint).

metadata.issue: Mixed JSON shapes across `wk run` commands make agents infer success from absence of stderr or prose fields.
metadata.supportingReasoning: Earlier analysis noted generate-document-style handlers use `ok`; other paths may vary — explicit audit aligns agent expectations with runtime.

tasks.description:
## Goal
Eliminate drift between extended tier tables and runtime by surfacing sensitivity from manifest/policy metadata in the instruction catalog JSON.

## Implementation strategy
- Extend instruction-surface builder to attach `policy` or `jsonApprovalRequired` + `operationId` per command row (source: same data as --schema-only / manifest).
- Keep lean digest stable: either include tier in digest inputs or document omission for lean mode.

## Technical implementation
- Manifest or policy registry read path shared with schema-only pilot.
- Types for instruction rows; doctor --agent-instruction-surface serialization.

## Test changes
- Snapshot test for agent-instruction-surface JSON including new fields for known commands.

## Acceptance criteria
1. Full `doctor --agent-instruction-surface` output includes per-command policy fields for Tier A/B/C classification.
2. Values match `pnpm exec wk run <cmd> --schema-only '{}'` `policy` block for sampled commands.
3. Document lean vs full behavior in AGENT-CLI-MAP.

metadata.issue: Tier tables in markdown can drift; agents should read one JSON catalog for policy sensitivity per command.
metadata.supportingReasoning: Single source of truth should be manifest-driven; instruction surface is already the heavy JSON catalog for agents.

tasks.description:
## Goal
One-hop discovery for agents: checklist + AGENTS refs + help text that matches actual behavior.

## Implementation strategy
- Add `.ai/CLI-BOOTSTRAP.md` (or similar name per repo naming) with: doctor → bootstrap → get-next-actions / schema-only → Tier A/B policyApproval.
- Add `ref` or rule cross-links from `.ai/AGENTS.md` / `agent-source-of-truth-order.md` without duplicating tier tables (link to AGENT-CLI-MAP).
- Update `printWorkspaceKitTopLevelHelp` in `src/cli.ts` when related CLI tasks land; until then document "planned" vs implement in same phase as JSON flags.

## Technical implementation
- Markdown + optional machine extract (if documentation module generates nothing here, hand-maintain short file).
- AGENTS.md meta block ref if required by doc contract.

## Test changes
- Orphan/doc check scripts if repo has `check-orphan-ai-sources` or similar — register new path.
- Optional: lightweight script asserting help contains string `pnpm exec`.

## Acceptance criteria
1. New checklist file exists under `.ai/` and is linked from AGENTS / source-of-truth order.
2. `wk --help` mentions `pnpm exec wk` and points to AGENT-CLI-MAP paths (relative as today).
3. No maintainer-only content in the new file that violates agent-doc-routing (stays machine canon).

metadata.issue: Discovery is spread across WORKSPACE-KIT-SESSION, AGENT-CLI-MAP, and help; agents do not always open the right file first.
metadata.supportingReasoning: Single checklist + AGENTS pointer + help blurb reduces onboarding variance; pairs with bootstrap JSON task.

tasks.description:
## Goal
Teach agents in the same JSON response they already fetch, instead of relying on long `.ai/*.md` reads.

## Implementation strategy
- Add fields such as `cliInvoke`: { `preferred`, `antiPatterns[]`, `policyLanes`: { `runJsonApproval`, `envApprovalForTopLevel` } }.
- Pull copy from `.ai/WORKSPACE-KIT-SESSION.md` bullet on `pnpm` `--` (validated for accuracy).
- Version bump or `bootstrapSchemaVersion` if clients need to detect new fields.

## Technical implementation
- `agent-bootstrap` handler in task-engine (or whichever module owns it) + types.
- Snapshot tests on bootstrap JSON shape.

## Test changes
- Extend existing bootstrap tests / golden JSON fixture to include new keys.
- Ensure lean projection still works if applicable.

## Acceptance criteria
1. `pnpm exec wk run agent-bootstrap '{}'` includes new structured CLI guidance keys.
2. Keys are stable strings suitable for agents (no ambiguous prose-only blobs).
3. WORKSPACE-KIT-SESSION references the field names so humans and agents align.

metadata.issue: Agents miss `pnpm exec` vs `pnpm run wk --` pitfalls and approval lanes despite prose in WORKSPACE-KIT-SESSION.
metadata.supportingReasoning: Cold-start JSON should be self-contained; embedding footguns reduces mis-invocation without opening multiple docs.

tasks.title: CLI: policy-denied run responses include remediation + sample argv
tasks.approach: Trace policy-denied code path; add remediation builder; test with a Tier B command in dry or mock policy.
technicalScope: Policy denial JSON payload extension (run path)
Optional tie-in to generate-agent-cli-snippets or schema-only sampleArgs
Tests: denial response shape
.ai/POLICY-APPROVAL.md + AGENT-CLI-MAP.md
acceptanceCriteria: At least one integration/unit test asserts remediation fields on simulated policy-denied
Tier A run-transition denial includes policyApproval hint
Human-readable stderr message can remain; JSON carries structured remediation
Security review: no accidental approval in JSON
tasks.summary: When Tier A/B `wk run` fails with policy denial, agents lack a self-service path. Include structured hints (operationId, sample policyApproval placement) in JSON output.
tasks.description:
## Goal
Reduce retry loops after `policy-denied` by telling the caller exactly what approval shape is expected.

## Implementation strategy
- Locate policy denial path for `workspace-kit run` (policy layer / run router).
- Extend JSON error or success-false payload with `remediation`: { `lane`, `operationId`, `pasteTemplate` or `sampleArgsWithPolicyApproval`, `docPath` }.
- Ensure no secret or environment leakage; rationale still required from actor at invoke time.
- Align with `.ai/POLICY-APPROVAL.md` two-lane table.

## Technical implementation
- Changes in policy enforcement + run command wrapper; possibly shared helper for `pasteTemplate` from same snippets as `--schema-only`.
- Optional: link to `.ai/agent-cli-snippets/by-command/<cmd>.json` path as string.

## Test changes
- Unit tests: denied without approval returns remediation object; approved path unchanged.

## Acceptance criteria
1. Policy-denied JSON includes machine-readable `remediation` block for at least one Tier B command in tests.
2. Docs updated: POLICY-APPROVAL + AGENT-CLI-MAP mention remediation field.
3. No change to policy semantics (still fail-closed); only observability + UX.

metadata.issue: After `policy-denied`, agents re-read long policy docs or guess `WORKSPACE_KIT_POLICY_APPROVAL` on the wrong lane.
metadata.supportingReasoning: POLICY-APPROVAL.md defines JSON argv vs env lanes; embedding hints in the denial response shortens the feedback loop without weakening gates.

tasks.title: CLI: structured JSON envelope for `pnpm exec wk doctor` success and failure
tasks.approach: Add JSON doctor mode after inventory of current stdout; align error object with existing issue shape; add tests before wiring docs.
technicalScope: src/cli.ts doctor code path + new --json flag
JSON schema or TypeScript type for doctor result envelope
Tests: success/failure JSON; optional delivery-loop fields when flags set
.ai/AGENT-CLI-MAP.md + WORKSPACE-KIT-SESSION step 1 clarification
acceptanceCriteria: `wk doctor --json` is parseable in one JSON.parse on stdout
Failure cases include actionable issue list in JSON, not only stderr
Default doctor output unchanged without --json
Help text lists the new flag
tasks.summary: Default `doctor` success prints human lines; agents assume stdout is machine-parseable. Add an explicit JSON mode for pass/fail with stable fields.
tasks.description:
## Goal
Make health checks consumable by agents and CI without regex-parsing human lines.

## Implementation strategy
- Introduce `--json` (or `--format json`) on `doctor` that prints one JSON object for both success and failure (failure may still use stderr for messages if documented, but prefer structured `ok` + `issues[]`).
- Map existing `collectDoctorContractIssues` and delivery-loop outcomes into the envelope.
- Keep default doctor behavior unchanged when `--json` is absent.

## Technical implementation
- Extend `src/cli.ts` doctor branch; add serializer for contract issues + delivery-loop state.
- Document contract in `.ai/AGENT-CLI-MAP.extended.md` or runbooks shell-scripts section.

## Test changes
- Golden tests for doctor --json success (minimal fixture repo or mocked cwd) and failure (missing manifest) with stable `code`/`issues`.

## Acceptance criteria
1. `pnpm exec wk doctor --json` returns parseable JSON with `ok: true|false` and structured issues when `ok: false`.
2. Without `--json`, behavior matches prior human-oriented output (smoke assertion or snapshot).
3. Documented alongside `doctor --agent-instruction-surface` so agents know: plain doctor ≠ JSON; use the right flag.

metadata.issue: Agents conflate default `doctor` stdout with machine output; only `--agent-instruction-surface` is clearly JSON today.
metadata.supportingReasoning: User-requested improvement batch 2026-04-29: separate human success lines from agent/CI JSON contract.

tasks.title: CLI: machine-readable JSON for bare `pnpm exec wk run` (command menu)
tasks.approach: Phased: (1) schema + handler branch + tests, (2) wire manifest fields, (3) doc + optional INDEX.json cross-link.
technicalScope: src/cli run path: no-subcommand + JSON format flag
Shared command enumeration used by text list and JSON (single source of truth)
Tests: stdout JSON parse + parity or sampling against text menu
.ai/AGENT-CLI-MAP.md + optional WORKSPACE-KIT-SESSION bootstrap bullet
acceptanceCriteria: JSON command menu flag works from repo root with pnpm exec wk; documented
Tests cover success path and JSON parseability
No regression in default bare `wk run` listing for existing consumers
Planning generation / prelude unchanged for normal `run <cmd>` invocations
tasks.summary: Agents scrape human prose from the bare `wk run` menu; add a JSON mode that lists runnable commands with stable metadata so automation does not hallucinate names or tiers.
tasks.description:
## Goal
Eliminate ambiguity when an agent discovers which `workspace-kit run` subcommands exist by providing a parseable alternative to stdout text.

## Implementation strategy
- Add a flag (e.g. `--format json` or `--json`) on the no-subcommand `run` path only, documented in top-level help and AGENT-CLI-MAP.
- Emit a single JSON object on stdout with a versioned schema (e.g. `schemaVersion`, `commands[]` with `name`, `moduleId`, instruction path or snippet path when available).
- Prefer reusing existing manifest/instruction-index data built for `doctor --agent-instruction-surface` where possible to avoid drift.
- Keep default (no flag) behavior unchanged for humans and scripts that already parse text.

## Technical implementation
- Touch `src/cli` run handler (likely `handleRunCommand` or equivalent): branch when argv is `run` + format flag + no command name.
- Add a small shared type + JSON serializer; avoid duplicate hard-coded command lists.
- Update `.ai/AGENT-CLI-MAP.md` / machine-cli-policy quick line for agent discovery.

## Test changes
- Unit or integration tests: snapshot JSON shape; assert `commands` includes at least one known builtin command name; stderr clean on success.
- Optional: guard that pretty-print vs single-line is stable (document choice).

## Acceptance criteria
1. `pnpm exec wk run --format json` (or agreed flag) exits 0 and prints exactly one JSON object parseable without non-JSON prefix/suffix on stdout when doctor would allow `run`.
2. JSON includes every runnable command name that the text menu lists (parity test or shared source).
3. Default `pnpm exec wk run` output without the flag remains compatible with existing expectations (spot-check or contract test).
4. Documented in `.ai/WORKSPACE-KIT-SESSION.md` or AGENT-CLI-MAP bootstrap steps for agents.

metadata.issue: Agents often mis-parse the plain-text `wk run` menu or invent subcommand names; there is no single machine-readable discovery path aligned with the actual manifest.
metadata.supportingReasoning: Discussion 2026-04-29: AGENT-CLI-MAP prefers `pnpm exec wk` and schema-only; a JSON menu closes the loop between discovery and argv shape without reading markdown tables.

tasks.title: [Guidance authoring] Add real draft rule impact preview
tasks.approach: Implement a read-only draft impact preview command that validates draft rule content, overlays it on the active registry in memory, evaluates bounded sample contexts, and returns match/no-match/conflict/noise data without writing registry rows.
technicalScope: Define draft rule input fields: title, body/source content, family, strength/attention, acknowledgement strength, scope draft, priority, lifecycle target, and optional source refs.
Build an in-memory artifact + activation overlay and evaluate it with the same deterministic CAE evaluator used by live previews.
Support preview against the current selected task/workflow plus representative sample contexts for broad scopes.
Return matched contexts, unmatched contexts, conflictShadowSummary, family counts, pending acknowledgement effect, broad-scope warnings, and debug overlay ids.
Guarantee no SQLite registry mutations, no active version changes, and no audit writes for preview.
acceptanceCriteria: Draft preview evaluates actual draft rule content and scope, not only an argv summary string.
The command is read-only and safe to call repeatedly from the dashboard.
Users can see whether the draft would appear for the selected task/workflow before saving.
Broad/global drafts return blast-radius and noise warnings sufficient to stop accidental overreach.
Tests cover always, workflow, completing-task, phase, task, and tag scopes plus conflict scenarios.
tasks.summary: Evaluate draft Guidance rules against sample contexts before saving or activating them.
tasks.description: The current draftPreview entrypoint is a useful UI affordance, but it does not evaluate a real draft artifact/activation. Add a server-side impact preview that accepts draft rule fields, builds a temporary registry overlay, and reports where the draft would match.
tasks.risk: High leverage; incorrect previews could make users trust a rule that will not behave the same after activation.

metadata.issue: Draft preview needs to exercise actual CAE activation semantics before users can trust authoring.
metadata.supportingReasoning: The current UI has draft-preview controls, but the backend path should accept draft rule fields and use an overlay evaluator.

tasks.title: [Guidance authoring] Add blast-radius and conflict summary before activation
tasks.approach: Extend draft impact preview with a bounded sample matrix and activation-readiness summary. Use existing precedence/conflict output but shape it into an explicit “safe to enable?” product response.
technicalScope: Build a bounded sample set from current ready/in-progress tasks, current phase tasks, common workflow intents, and representative command args.
Return counts by scope bucket: always/global, workflow, phase, task, tag/type, and unknown/custom.
Summarize same-family conflicts, policy/advisory overlap, acknowledgement load, and duplicate/near-duplicate guidance.
Define thresholds for warning vs stop-and-confirm, especially for always/global and policy-family rules.
Render a dashboard-ready enablement summary with examples, warnings, and debug trace links.
acceptanceCriteria: Before activation, broad rules show affected sample counts and representative examples.
Conflicts and duplicate/noisy overlaps are visible without opening raw JSON.
Always/global and policy-family rules receive stronger warnings than narrow task/workflow rules.
The output is deterministic for the same task store, registry snapshot, and draft input.
Tests cover narrow, broad, conflicting, duplicate, and acknowledgement-heavy drafts.
tasks.summary: Show how many workflows/tasks a draft Guidance rule would affect, plus conflicts/noise, before enablement.
tasks.description: Global and broad rules are powerful and easy to abuse. Before activation, users should see blast radius, representative matches, conflicts, acknowledgements introduced, and noisy overlap with existing rules.
tasks.risk: Medium-high; this is the main safety net against accidental global guidance spam.

metadata.issue: Users need safety evidence before enabling broad or global Guidance rules.
metadata.supportingReasoning: The evaluator can detect conflicts, but authoring needs a product-shaped pre-activation summary.

tasks.title: [Guidance authoring] Complete dashboard Guidance Rules authoring wizard
tasks.approach: Add a dedicated Guidance Rules panel or wizard in the extension using the product catalog, scope builder, and draft impact preview APIs. Keep raw JSON/debug details available but out of the happy path.
technicalScope: Add rules list navigation with New rule, Improve this guidance, Edit draft, and Duplicate existing rule entrypoints.
Implement wizard steps for content/title, family, strength/acknowledgement, scope, priority/lifecycle, impact preview, and save/enable handoff.
Use shared intent/scope labels from the product model instead of hardcoded extension-local labels.
Preserve user input and focus during reloads, failed previews, and kit-state refreshes.
Add extension renderer/provider tests for happy path, validation errors, admin-off state, broad-scope warning, and preview result rendering.
acceptanceCriteria: A maintainer can draft a new Guidance rule without writing CAE artifact or activation JSON.
The wizard requires a successful impact preview before save/enable actions become available.
Admin-off/read-only workspaces can still draft and preview, but cannot silently mutate the registry.
Improve this guidance pre-fills the wizard from an existing card or noisy feedback item.
Renderer/provider tests cover the wizard states and prevent regression to raw-JSON-first UX.
tasks.summary: Turn the dashboard affordances into a full New/Edit Guidance Rule wizard backed by product-shaped commands.
tasks.description: The dashboard has Manage Guidance, Improve entrypoints, and draft-preview controls. Complete the user workflow so maintainers can create or edit draft rules through friendly fields, preview them, and intentionally hand off to governed enablement.
tasks.risk: Medium; mostly UX, but it touches governance affordances and user trust.

metadata.issue: Existing dashboard affordances stop short of a complete product authoring workflow.
metadata.supportingReasoning: Phase 73 added Manage Guidance and Improve entrypoints, but full create/edit/save flow still needs product-backed wizard semantics.

tasks.title: [Guidance authoring] Build friendly scope builder for rules
tasks.approach: Define a GuidanceScopeDraft model with presets and converters to CAE activation conditions. Include special handling for completing a task by mapping to run-transition plus commandArgs.action = complete semantics where available.
technicalScope: Define scope presets for Always, Workflow/intent, Completing a task, Phase, Task id/pattern, Task tag/type, and advanced custom command.
Map each preset to CAE activation scope conditions and a plain-English scope summary.
Add validation for invalid combinations, overly broad scopes, unknown workflow names, malformed task id patterns, and missing phase keys/tags.
Represent command argument matching needs for cases like run-transition complete; define whether commandArgs matching requires new CAE condition support.
Add tests for every preset and for broad-scope warning metadata.
acceptanceCriteria: A caller can construct CAE activation conditions from product-level scope fields without hand-writing activation JSON.
The completing-a-task preset is represented explicitly and can be previewed against run-transition complete scenarios.
Each scope draft returns both machine conditions and a human-readable scope summary.
Invalid or dangerous broad scopes return actionable validation errors or warnings.
The scope builder is reusable by CLI commands and the Cursor extension.
tasks.summary: Translate product scopes like Always, completing a task, phase, task, tag, and workflow into CAE activation conditions.
tasks.description: The CAE schema already supports always, commandName, taskIdPattern, taskTag, and phaseKey, but users need to choose scopes without writing activation JSON. Build a typed scope builder that can generate and explain CAE conditions from product choices.
tasks.risk: Medium-high; scope semantics affect when guidance appears and must remain deterministic.

metadata.issue: Rule authoring cannot be safe if users must understand raw CAE condition JSON.
metadata.supportingReasoning: The desired UX asks for always/workflow/phase/task/task-shape scopes, including completing tickets, while the current UI only previews an argv summary.

tasks.title: [phase 75] Retire blob-era task persistence hot paths

Make any remaining blob mirrors explicitly compatibility/export surfaces with freshness checks or generation metadata.
Finish feature storage canonicalization around task_engine_task_features and remove legacy feature fallbacks from hot paths.

tasks.title: [Guidance authoring] Define enforcement-readiness gates for authored rules
tasks.approach: Create an enforcement-readiness contract for authored rules that ties together draft impact preview, blast-radius checks, governance approval, audit evidence, rollback, and release validation. Keep enforcement opt-in and blocked until those checks are satisfied.
technicalScope: Define readiness fields for a Guidance rule/version: previewedAt, previewDigest, affectedScopeSummary, conflictStatus, approval actor/rationale, audit id, rollback target, and enforcementEligibility.
Specify which families/strengths can ever become blocking and which remain advisory/read-required only.
Add validation that prevents enabling hard-stop/enforcement metadata without successful impact preview and governance evidence.
Define UI copy and CLI payload shape for enforcement-eligible vs advisory-only rules.
Update CAE docs/runbooks and tests to keep acknowledgement, CAE mutation approval, policyApproval, and enforcement gates distinct.
acceptanceCriteria: There is a documented and testable gate for when an authored rule can be considered enforcement-eligible.
No dashboard path can accidentally turn an advisory draft into a hard-stop rule without explicit readiness evidence.
Conflict or broad-scope warnings block or require stronger confirmation according to the readiness contract.
Rollback and audit evidence are part of the enforcement-readiness story.
Docs and tests preserve the separation between acknowledgement, CAE mutation approval, policyApproval, and enforcement.
tasks.summary: Defer hard-stop enforcement until authored Guidance rules have clear readiness, preview, and governance gates.
tasks.description: Hard-stop Guidance/enforcement should not advance just because authoring controls exist. Define the conditions under which authored rules may become enforceable, including evidence requirements, conflict handling, rollback, and release gates.
tasks.risk: High if rushed; enforcement affects operator trust and should remain gated until authoring is mature.

metadata.issue: Hard-stop behavior should not advance until authored-rule preview and governance are demonstrably safe.
metadata.supportingReasoning: The roadmap explicitly says enforcement comes later; this task defines the readiness bar instead of implementing broad enforcement immediately.
metadata.softGate: Do not implement broad enforcement without explicit maintainer approval and release evidence.

Decide which fields become physical columns, generated columns, indexed expressions, or contract-level projections.

metadata_json remains supported for module-specific extension data.

tasks.summary: Promote high-use agent routing metadata into stable columns or projections.
tasks.description: Keep metadata_json for extension data, but stop forcing agents to inspect arbitrary metadata for common routing, evidence, queue, source, and policy fields.

tasks.title: [Guidance authoring] Add product-shaped Guidance Rules catalog

metadata.supportingReasoning: The current Manage Guidance section is useful but still closer to registry introspection than product authoring.

tasks.title: [Guidance authoring] Extract server-side Guidance product model
tasks.approach: Create a narrow Guidance product-model layer in the context-activation module that wraps CAE registry, trace, version, config, and workflow-label data into stable UI-oriented DTOs. Preserve existing CLI payload compatibility by adding fields rather than breaking current read-only commands.

Move workflow intent labels and scope preset definitions out of GuidanceViewProvider into a shared catalog exported by the package or returned by a command.
Add a read-only command or augment cae-dashboard-summary with a product-shaped guidance model while keeping existing fields additive and backward compatible.
Update the Cursor extension to consume the product model for status, Manage Guidance, intent labels, and mutation availability instead of assembling raw CAE/config data itself.

The product model clearly separates Guidance acknowledgement, CAE mutation approval, and Tier A/B policyApproval.

Before adding more authoring behavior, define a typed product view model and adapter so the extension renders stable Guidance concepts instead of raw CAE registry pieces.

metadata.issue: Guidance product logic is split between CAE commands and the Cursor extension, which will make authoring flows brittle.
metadata.supportingReasoning: Current Manage Guidance rendering combines cae-dashboard-summary, cae-list-registry-versions, cae-list-artifacts, cae-list-activations, and resolve-config in the provider instead of consuming one typed product view.

tasks.title: [phase 75] Move task transition and mutation evidence to append-only tables
tasks.approach: Turn task evidence into queryable append-only rows indexed for agent handoff, audit, and debugging instead of keeping the good stuff buried in giant JSON blobs.
technicalScope: Design transition and mutation evidence tables with task_id, timestamps, actor, command/action, from/to status, guard results, dependency effects, and policy/approval metadata where available.
Backfill existing transition_log_json and mutation_log_json into relational evidence rows.
Update write paths to append relational evidence while maintaining a compatibility mirror during migration.
Add read commands or projections for recent task history and per-task evidence retrieval.
acceptanceCriteria: Agents can retrieve recent and per-task evidence without parsing workspace_planning_state JSON blobs.
Transition and mutation evidence remains append-only and ordered by timestamp/sequence.
Existing evidence consumers continue to work during the compatibility window.
Migration tests prove no existing transition or mutation evidence is lost.
tasks.summary: Move task evidence out of blob logs into queryable append-only tables.
tasks.description: Transition and mutation history are core agent evidence. Store them as indexed rows so agents can inspect task history without parsing large workspace_planning_state JSON blobs.

metadata.issue: Task transition and mutation logs are stored in large JSON blobs that are expensive and awkward for agents to query.

tasks.title: [phase 75] Normalize task dependency edges
tasks.approach: Introduce a canonical dependency edge table and make dependsOn the source relationship while deriving unblocks through reverse lookup. Keep compatibility projections so existing callers do not faceplant.
technicalScope: Add task_engine_dependencies with task_id, depends_on_task_id, timestamps/provenance where useful, uniqueness, no-self-dependency guard, and task FKs.
Backfill dependency edges from task_engine_tasks.depends_on_json after readiness checks pass.
Update dependency reads/writes to use the edge table while preserving legacy JSON compatibility during the transition.
Expose an agent-friendly dependency projection with task_id, depends_on_task_id, dependency status, and satisfaction state.
acceptanceCriteria: Dependency existence and uniqueness are enforced by SQLite for canonical dependency edges.
Agent reads can query dependency edges and satisfaction state without parsing depends_on_json or unblocks_json.
Existing add-dependency/remove-dependency and dependency graph commands keep their external behavior.
Unblocks is derived or compatibility-only, not a second source of truth for new runtime behavior.
tasks.summary: Introduce canonical relational dependency edges and derive reverse relationships.
tasks.description: Replace JSON-array dependency persistence as the canonical source with a proper edge table. Preserve compatibility while giving agents a direct dependency projection with satisfaction state.
tasks.risk: High; dependency semantics drive queue correctness and task lifecycle gates.

metadata.issue: Dependencies are currently stored as JSON arrays, limiting database enforcement and agent query ergonomics.
metadata.supportingReasoning: Schema review showed dependency target existence and reverse relationships are application-managed rather than SQLite-enforced.

tasks.title: [phase 75] Add task persistence doctor and migration readiness checks
tasks.approach: Build a preflight layer that tells agents and maintainers whether the DB is safe to migrate, with repair guidance that is copy-pasteable instead of cryptic SQLite failure confetti.
technicalScope: Add checks for JSON validity, enum validity, archived flag consistency, timestamp shape, orphaned references, dependency graph integrity, legacy feature storage, and blob/envelope drift.
Report each finding with stable codes, affected row counts, sample IDs, severity, and remediation guidance.
Expose dry-run readiness output through workspace-kit doctor or a task-engine command suitable for agent automation.
Cover empty DB, first-run DB, no task rows, and no evidence rows as explicit non-error states where appropriate.
Cover readiness checks with focused tests using representative malformed planning DB fixtures or in-memory stores.
acceptanceCriteria: Doctor/readiness output identifies known migration blockers before schema migrations run.
Each known blocker has actionable remediation guidance and stable machine-readable codes.
Dry-run output is safe for agents to call repeatedly and does not mutate task state.
Empty/first-run/no-data states are reported predictably without stack traces or false-positive migration blockers.
The readiness surface reports the current planning generation and schema/user_version context.
tasks.summary: Add pre-migration health checks with repair guidance for task persistence changes.
tasks.description: Before tightening constraints or moving data, agents need a safe readiness surface that can identify malformed rows, legacy state, and migration blockers with stable codes and clear remediation.
tasks.risk: Medium-high; this reduces migration risk but must avoid false positives that block normal work.

metadata.issue: Schema migrations need deterministic preflight evidence before constraints and table moves hard-fail.
metadata.supportingReasoning: The current DB is valid, but the schema permits bad enum values, orphanable references, and blob drift unless application checks catch them.

tasks.title: [phase 75] Define agent-facing task DB contract
tasks.approach: Design the stable agent-facing read contract before changing storage internals. Prefer versioned CLI JSON/read models that expose task list, task detail, next actions, dependency edges, and evidence without requiring agents to parse raw blob columns.
technicalScope: Inventory existing agent task reads such as get-next-actions, list-tasks, get-task, queue-health, dashboard-summary, and dependency graph commands.
Define versioned fields for agent task list, task detail, next-action, dependency edge, and evidence read models.
Specify compatibility guarantees, naming, deprecation behavior, expected JSON response shape, and empty/first-run/no-data behavior for agent consumers.
Identify whether each surface is best delivered as CLI JSON, SQLite view, or both.
acceptanceCriteria: A versioned agent-facing task contract exists for normal agent workflows without requiring raw SQLite or JSON blob parsing.
The contract covers ready queue, blocked reasons, dependencies, task details, evidence pointers, phase fields, and routing metadata.
Empty workspace, no ready tasks, missing evidence, and first-run DB states return stable successful envelopes with explicit empty arrays/nulls rather than ambiguous failures.
Existing get-next-actions/list-tasks/get-task behavior remains compatible or has an explicit compatibility plan.
Follow-on schema tasks can cite this contract as their required external interface.
tasks.summary: Define the stable agent-facing contract for task DB reads before deeper schema changes land.
tasks.description: Agents should consume predictable CLI JSON/read models instead of raw SQLite blob columns. This task establishes the versioned contract for task list, task detail, next actions, dependency edges, and evidence retrieval, including empty and first-run behavior.
tasks.risk: High leverage contract work; changes here set compatibility expectations for the rest of the phase.

metadata.issue: Task-engine persistence has outgrown blob-era storage, but agents need a stable interface before internal schema changes.
metadata.supportingReasoning: Schema review found JSON/envelope storage, dependency arrays, and evidence blobs that are awkward for agents to query safely.

tasks.title: [architecture] Add task-engine agent protocol compliance suite
tasks.approach: Produce a test strategy and initial compliance matrix that turns agent protocol expectations into CI-enforced checks. The proposal should define what every command must prove, how failures are reported, and how modules opt into stricter coverage over time.
technicalScope: Define a compliance matrix for command schema coverage, instruction coverage, policy metadata, planning-generation metadata, idempotency declarations, response templates, and stable remediation codes.
Specify golden tests for run-transition, create-task, update-task, persist-planning-execution-drafts, set-current-phase, and representative non-task-engine commands.

Design fixtures for common failure responses: policy-denied, planning-generation-required, planning-generation-mismatch, invalid-run-args, idempotency-key-conflict, unknown-command, and dependency-unsatisfied.
Propose CI integration and a path for explicit exclusions with rationale.
Identify which tests depend on the unified command contract registry and which can land immediately.
acceptanceCriteria: The proposal defines pass/fail rules for agent protocol compliance and maps them to concrete test files or test suites.
The compliance matrix covers every router-registered command category or records an explicit exclusion policy.
The proposal includes golden response examples for the major agent failure modes.
The CI plan prevents new executable commands from shipping without schema/docs/policy/remediation coverage.
The proposal produces follow-on implementation tasks that can be delivered independently.
tasks.summary: Architecture proposal: define an executable compliance suite for the agent-facing task-engine CLI protocol.
tasks.description: Proposal intent: make agent reliability an executable contract. This should cover schema/docs/policy/concurrency/idempotency/remediation/response-shape requirements and feed back into the command contract registry proposal.
tasks.risk: Agent-facing reliability rules remain advisory unless CI can catch missing schema coverage, stale docs, weak errors, and drifted transition metadata.

metadata.issue: The agent-facing CLI protocol currently relies too much on convention and scattered documentation.
metadata.supportingReasoning: A protocol compliance suite makes reliability expectations executable.

tasks.title: [architecture] Introduce unified command contract registry
tasks.approach: Produce an architecture proposal before implementation. The proposal should identify the current scattered metadata surfaces, define the target registry model, specify migration steps, and decide how runtime handlers, schema-only output, docs, and CI checks consume the registry.
technicalScope: Inventory existing command metadata sources: builtin manifest, pilot schemas, planning-generation prelude, instruction markdown, response templates, and handler-local checks.

Define a versioned command contract shape covering args schema, examples, policy sensitivity, operationId, dry-run behavior, planning-generation requirement, idempotency, response presentation, and remediation.
Map how module authors declare contract rows and how the router validates missing or duplicate metadata.
Propose a migration plan from current scattered files to registry-backed generation/validation with compatibility milestones.
Identify implementation tasks that can be split from the proposal, including tests and docs generation changes.
acceptanceCriteria: The proposal names every current metadata source and states whether it is replaced, generated, or retained as compatibility output.
The proposal includes a concrete TypeScript/JSON schema sketch for command contract rows.
The proposal explains how schema-only, instruction generation, policy checks, planning-generation checks, and response templates consume the registry.
The migration plan is incremental and lists at least three reviewable implementation tasks.
Open questions and compatibility risks are documented explicitly for maintainer decision.
tasks.summary: Architecture proposal: design a unified command contract registry as the source of truth for CLI schema, policy, concurrency, idempotency, examples, and docs generation.
tasks.description: Proposal intent: this is the architectural backbone behind several Phase 74 fixes. The outcome should be a reviewed design artifact and follow-on implementation slices, not a giant one-shot refactor.
tasks.risk: Without a shared contract registry, fixes for schema-only, docs generation, policy metadata, and command examples may continue to duplicate metadata and drift.

metadata.issue: Command behavior metadata is split across several files and runtime handlers.
metadata.supportingReasoning: A unified registry would reduce drift and give agents one machine-readable protocol surface.

tasks.title: [defect] Add atomic task-intent commands for common workflows
tasks.approach: Layer higher-level commands over TransitionService rather than bypassing it. The commands should use the same policy, planning-generation, idempotency, dependency, and evidence rules as run-transition while reducing orchestration mistakes for common flows.
technicalScope: Design commands for claim-next-task, start-task, complete-task, block-task, pause-task, and unblock-task or a smaller first slice if needed.
Implement commands as wrappers around existing transition validation/evidence paths, not separate lifecycle logic.
Support expectedPlanningGeneration, clientMutationId, actor, and policyApproval according to equivalent transition sensitivity.
For claim-next-task, atomically select the first runnable suggested task in the requested queueNamespace and transition it to in_progress or return a structured no-op reason.
Add docs/tests for success, dependency-blocked, stale generation, idempotent replay, and policy-denied cases.
acceptanceCriteria: claim-next-task starts the same task get-next-actions would suggest when it is still runnable at mutation time.
If the suggested task changes, is dependency-blocked, or no runnable task exists, the command returns a structured no-op/violation without mutating state.
start-task and complete-task preserve the same transition evidence and policy trace semantics as run-transition.
Intent commands support retry-safe clientMutationId behavior where run-transition supports it.
Docs clearly state when agents should prefer intent commands and when raw run-transition is still appropriate.
tasks.summary: Add intent-level task workflow commands that safely perform common agent lifecycle sequences without manual read-check-mutate choreography.
tasks.description: Implementation intent: agents usually want to claim, start, complete, block, pause, or unblock work. Those intents should be available as atomic operations that return clear no-op/violation reasons instead of requiring agents to script several reads and writes.
tasks.risk: Manual orchestration around get-next-actions/get-task/run-transition is easy to race, duplicate, or run from stale queue assumptions.

metadata.issue: Agents repeatedly perform fragile read-check-mutate sequences for common task workflows.
metadata.supportingReasoning: Higher-level intent commands reduce orchestration mistakes while keeping task-engine invariants central.

tasks.title: [defect] Add agent-facing mutation planner for workspace-kit run
tasks.approach: Build an agent preflight command around existing command registry, policy, planning generation, schema-only, and get-task allowedActions data. Use T985 schema-only coverage as the minimum machine-readable command shape, and coordinate with T989 so the planner can migrate to the eventual unified contract registry.
technicalScope: Add a read-only command such as agent-mutation-plan or prepare-run with args for commandName and optional taskId/action/context.

Return schema/sample args, policySensitivity, operationId, approval lane, planningGeneration, planningGenerationPolicy, expectedPlanningGeneration requirement, idempotency support, and remediation paths.
For run-transition with taskId, include task status, allowedActions, dependency blockers, and whether the requested action is currently valid.
Return a ready-to-run argv example with placeholders for human-only rationale where appropriate.
Add tests for sensitive run-transition, non-sensitive update-task, dry-run sensitive-with-dryrun command, unknown command, and stale/blocked task cases.
acceptanceCriteria: An agent can request a mutation plan for run-transition with a taskId and receive current allowedActions plus the exact JSON fields needed.
The response clearly distinguishes JSON policyApproval from WORKSPACE_KIT_POLICY_APPROVAL env approval.
The response includes current planningGeneration and whether expectedPlanningGeneration is required.
The response reports clientMutationId support and retry recommendation for commands that support it.
The command is read-only, requires no policyApproval, and has schema-only coverage/tests.
tasks.summary: Add a read-only mutation planner that returns the exact schema, policy, planning-generation, idempotency, and lifecycle requirements for a requested command.
tasks.description: Implementation intent: before mutating state, an agent should be able to ask the CLI what fields are required right now. This should proceed after T985 provides broad schema metadata, while treating T989 as future architecture alignment rather than a hard prerequisite unless T989 is accepted first.
tasks.risk: Agents currently compose a mutation protocol from multiple docs and commands, which makes reliable automation harder than it needs to be.

metadata.supportingReasoning: A single preflight command would collapse the most error-prone parts of the agent protocol.

tasks.title: [defect] Generate task-engine instructions from command contracts
tasks.approach: Introduce deterministic generated or verified sections for arguments, examples, lifecycle/action tables, and retry/concurrency notes while preserving hand-written narrative around those sections. Coordinate with T989 so this does not lock in a competing command-contract model.

Implement generation or verification for run-transition from transition/command contract metadata.
Add generated markers or snapshot checks that preserve non-generated prose while failing on stale command facts.
Extend checks to create-task, update-task, persist-planning-execution-drafts, and phase/status commands as feasible.
Document how maintainers update generated instruction sections when command contracts change.

tasks.title: [defect] Make schema-only discovery available for every run command
tasks.approach: Create a universal command schema metadata path using the smallest compatible contract that can later be absorbed by T989. Commands may start with permissive schemas, but every executable command should return structured schema-only output with sample args, instruction path, examples, policy metadata, and planning-generation metadata.
technicalScope: Inventory all router-registered executable commands and classify current schema-only coverage gaps.
Define a minimal command args schema format for commands that do not yet have strict validation.
Extend command registration/manifest metadata so schema-only output is available for every executable command.
Include sampleArgs, examples, instructionPath, policySensitivity, operationId, planningGeneration requirement, idempotency support, and remediation paths where known.
Add CI coverage that fails for executable commands without schema-only metadata or an explicit documented exclusion.

No router-registered executable command returns schema-only-unsupported unless it has an explicit checked exclusion with rationale.

CI fails when a new executable command ships without schema-only coverage.

tasks.title: [defect] Add retry-safe idempotency to run-transition
tasks.approach: Mirror the retry contract used by create-task/update-task, but adapt it to transition evidence. Store a stable digest for taskId, action, from/to state, actor-insensitive payload fields as appropriate, and return replay/conflict outcomes without duplicating evidence.
technicalScope: Add optional clientMutationId to run-transition args, schema-only output, and instruction docs.
Define transition idempotency digest semantics, including taskId, requested action, source/target state expectations, and relevant payload fields.
Persist idempotency metadata in transition evidence or mutation log without breaking existing transitionLog consumers.
Return transition-idempotent-replay for same clientMutationId/same payload and idempotency-key-conflict for same key/different payload.
Add tests for replay after success, conflict, stale planningGeneration replay, and normal invalid-transition behavior without clientMutationId.
acceptanceCriteria: A repeated run-transition with the same clientMutationId and same logical payload returns a replay success and does not append duplicate transition evidence.
A repeated run-transition with the same clientMutationId and different payload returns idempotency-key-conflict.
Replay succeeds without requiring a fresh expectedPlanningGeneration when the original mutation already landed.
Schema-only output and docs show clientMutationId and retry behavior.
Tests cover successful replay, conflict, planningGenerationPolicy=require, and non-idempotent invalid retry behavior.
tasks.summary: Add clientMutationId idempotency to run-transition so lifecycle mutations are safe to retry after ambiguous command outcomes.
tasks.description: Implementation intent: lifecycle transitions are the most important mutations agents perform. They need the same retry safety as other task writes, especially because CLI output parsing or shell timeouts can leave the client unsure whether a mutation landed.
tasks.risk: Retried lifecycle commands can produce invalid-transition noise, duplicate evidence attempts, or uncertain task state after transport ambiguity.

metadata.issue: Lifecycle transitions are the highest-value mutations, but they lack the retry-safe clientMutationId pattern used by other task mutations.
metadata.supportingReasoning: Agents often retry commands after timeout or parse ambiguity; transitions need first-class idempotency.

tasks.title: [defect] Align run-transition contract with runtime actions
tasks.approach: Use the transition map as the source of truth for a narrow tactical fix. This task may export or verify run-transition action metadata, but it must not invent the general command-contract registry or schema framework that T989 is meant to design.
technicalScope: Export or centralize the runtime transition action map in a form usable by run-transition schema/docs/tests only.
Update run-transition schema-only output to include an action enum or state-aware allowedActions metadata derived from the transition map without creating a general command-contract framework.
Regenerate or verify src/modules/task-engine/instructions/run-transition.md against runtime actions, including decline.
Add a contract test comparing runtime transitions, schema metadata, and instruction action tables for run-transition only.
Document the preferred agent discovery path: get-task allowedActions for task-specific decisions, schema-only/model output for global actions.
acceptanceCriteria: The documented run-transition action list includes every action in the runtime transition map, including decline.
workspace-kit run run-transition --schema-only exposes allowed actions or points to a machine-readable action table.
A test fails if a runtime transition action is not represented in run-transition docs/schema metadata.
The implementation does not introduce a general command-contract registry or framework that conflicts with T989.
Existing transition behavior and task statuses remain backward compatible.
tasks.summary: Generate or validate run-transition action docs/schema from the runtime transition map so agents discover every valid action.
tasks.description: Implementation intent: agents should not have to read runtime code to discover lifecycle actions. Keep the scope limited to run-transition action discovery and drift prevention so this can land before the broader T989 architecture proposal without creating a competing metadata system.
tasks.risk: Runtime action/doc/schema drift causes agents to miss valid lifecycle paths or retry invalid transitions.

metadata.supportingReasoning: The engine supports decline from in_progress to cancelled, but the instruction file does not advertise it.
