tasks.title: Add explicit plan mode with editable, approvable execution plans

metadata.problemStatement:
Concept: Before mutating code, produce a structured plan artifact reviewable by human or supervisor agent—editable and approvable. Maps to Claude Code plan review and WC governance.

Why this matters: Bridges strict process and fast agentic execution.
metadata.expectedOutcome: Plan artifact linked to tasks with sections: assumptions; intended files; commands to run; risks; rollback strategy; required approvals. Commands: draft-plan, revise-plan, approve-plan, execute-plan. Cursor can render/edit; Workflow Cannon owns schema and approval semantics.
metadata.impact: Reduces unapproved large diffs; improves evidence for why changes occurred.
metadata.constraints:
Suggested architecture: Versioned plan JSON/Markdown hybrid; approval gate blocks execute-plan until signed; integrate with hooks and task transitions.

Likely dependencies: Policy approval patterns; planning module overlap—merge or extend deliberately.

Decomposition notes: Reuse planning module concepts where possible; avoid duplicate build-plan confusion—name and scope clearly.
metadata.successSignals: Approved plan required for sensitive execute path; revision history; execute-plan emits evidence bundle; docs show end-to-end flow.
metadata.evidenceRef: docs/maintainers/runbooks/wishlist-workflow.md; maintainer wishlist batch 2026-04-02

tasks.title: Add asynchronous remote execution with Cursor background-agent handoff

metadata.problemStatement:
Concept: Cursor background agents run async on isolated remotes, clone repos, run commands, accept follow-ups. Workflow Cannon should add a control plane treating them as managed workers, not standalone UI.

Why this matters: Scale and long-running autonomy without rebuilding remote compute.
metadata.expectedOutcome: Remote-run adapter: launch background agent from a task; persist remote run metadata; sync results/evidence back; support clean take-over-in-Cursor handoff. Kit owns launch criteria, approval gates, task linkage, evidence expectations, completion semantics. Cursor owns remote machine and editor environment.
metadata.impact: Long-running work stays tied to task engine state and approvals.
metadata.constraints:
Suggested architecture: Remote run records in SQLite; state machine (queued/running/needs-input/completed/failed); idempotent launch.

Likely dependencies: Cursor APIs or manual bridge documented; team orchestration may share assignment model.

Decomposition notes: MVP = metadata + human sync; automate sync when APIs exist; explicit failure paths when remote diverges.
metadata.successSignals: list-remote-runs (or equivalent) shows linkage to T###; evidence artifacts attached on completion; runbook for handoff.
metadata.evidenceRef: docs/maintainers/runbooks/wishlist-workflow.md; maintainer wishlist batch 2026-04-02

tasks.title: Add a first-class project memory layer for agent recall and continuity

metadata.problemStatement:
Concept: Strong documentation canon exists, but an explicit memory layer is needed for recurring operational knowledge, preferred patterns, environment assumptions, and learned behaviors. Claude Code uses CLAUDE.md plus auto memory; Workflow Cannon should offer a governed equivalent.

Why this matters: Docs are not memory—memory should optimize for recall and operational reuse.
metadata.expectedOutcome: Separate memory surface (e.g. CANNON.md plus structured memory records in persistence). Categories: build/runtime commands; architecture conventions; debugging discoveries; repo-specific gotchas; review preferences. Commands: list-memory, write-memory, approve-memory, prune-memory, explain-memory-precedence. Explicitly not mixed with maintainer canon or generated docs.
metadata.impact: Improves session continuity and reduces repeated rediscovery without polluting canonical docs.
metadata.constraints:
Suggested architecture: Distinct store or namespaced rows; approval workflow for promoted memory; precedence explainer ties config + memory + canon.

Likely dependencies: Policy for write-memory; task linkage optional; migration story from ad-hoc notes.

Decomposition notes: Start with human-curated memory only; defer fuzzy retrieval/ML; document boundary vs .ai/ and docs/maintainers/.
metadata.successSignals: Precedence command returns deterministic merge story; approved vs draft memory states; prune is audited; no collision with document-project outputs.
metadata.evidenceRef: docs/maintainers/runbooks/wishlist-workflow.md; maintainer wishlist batch 2026-04-02

tasks.title: Dashboard: Accept All for proposed tasks grouped by phase
tasks.approach: In Proposed · Improvements and Proposed · Execution, group rows by phase (phaseKey or phase label) and render an inline Accept All control next to each phase heading that runs accept transitions for every proposed task in that band for the active category only (improvements vs execution), reusing the same policy rationale and planning-generation hygiene as single-row Accept.
technicalScope: extensions/cursor-workflow-cannon: render-dashboard.ts — phase-grouped subsections under each proposed category with heading + Accept All; extend or consume dashboard-summary proposedImprovementsSummary / proposedExecutionSummary shapes (add grouped payload in workspace-kit dashboard-summary command if flat top[] cannot infer phase groups reliably).

DashboardViewProvider.ts — new data-wc-action(s) (e.g. proposed-imp-accept-phase, proposed-exe-accept-phase) delegating to confirmAndRunTransition / run-transition-with-approval in sequence with refreshed expectedPlanningGeneration between mutating calls when policy require.
Edge cases: empty phase bucket; mixed legacy ids (imp-*); partial failures — surface progress and which taskIds failed without silent no-op.
acceptanceCriteria: Each proposed category shows phase groups when multiple phases exist; Accept All only affects proposed tasks in that category and that phase.
Operator gets one policy rationale prompt for the batch (or documented per-task prompts if policy requires); behavior matches or exceeds single Accept safety.
After batch, dashboard refresh shows updated statuses; README or inline muted help mentions Accept All.

tasks.title: Planning: multi-task decomposition from build-plan

metadata.problemStatement: Primary durable handoff is wishlist intake; outputMode tasks persists a single proposed T###. Teams still manually decompose via convert-wishlist or follow-up work. There is no first-class path from one interview to multiple execution tasks with provenance.
metadata.expectedOutcome: After finalize, operators can generate a vetted set of execution tasks (or a structured decomposition payload) from the planning artifact in one kit-supported flow, with stable metadata.planningProvenance-style links.
metadata.impact: Closes the gap between rich planning answers and actionable queue items without repetitive manual splitting.
metadata.constraints: Must align with convert-wishlist decomposition schema and strict task validation; respect planning-generation policy on bulk creates; optional feature flag or outputMode extension.
metadata.successSignals: Instruction + tests for new path; example JSON in AGENT-CLI-MAP or planning instructions; at least one integration test.
metadata.evidenceRef: chat:planning-module-ux-gaps 2026-04-07

wishlistConversionDecomposition.rationale: Separate planning finalize contract (multi-task or decomposition envelope) from task-engine bulk persistence under planningGeneration, then ship operator docs and an integration test so the path is discoverable and regression-safe.
wishlistConversionDecomposition.boundaries: Does not implement the Cursor extension interview UI (T800/T801); does not change Tier A/B policy lanes; optional feature flag only if needed to stage behavior.
wishlistConversionDecomposition.dependencyIntent: T810 applies and validates payloads produced or stabilized by T809; T811 documents and tests against the shipped CLI/planning behavior.

tasks.title: Planning: first-class interview UI in Cursor extension

metadata.problemStatement: Operators run planning as JSON in a terminal or via agent chat. The dashboard Planning card only shows progress percent and a resume CLI line—no in-question UI, no structured form for build-plan answers.
metadata.expectedOutcome: Users can complete a guided planning interview from the Workflow Cannon dashboard (or dedicated webview) with one question at a time, validated fields, and a clear path to finalize without hand-assembling JSON.
metadata.impact: Lowers friction for humans who do not want to drive workspace-kit JSON manually; reduces agent errors on build-plan payloads.
metadata.constraints: Must stay consistent with dashboard-summary planningSession contract; mutations still go through workspace-kit run (extension does not write SQLite directly); obey tasks.planningGenerationPolicy on any new mutators.
metadata.successSignals: Extension ships a planning flow that invokes or mirrors build-plan semantics; E2E or integration test covers at least one planningType end-to-end from UI.
metadata.evidenceRef: chat:planning-module-ux-gaps 2026-04-07

wishlistConversionDecomposition.rationale: Ship the guided interview as implementable UI work first, then lock it with an automated end-to-end proof so planningSession and build-plan semantics do not regress.
wishlistConversionDecomposition.boundaries: Extension and test harness only; no direct task-engine SQLite writes from the UI; planning question definitions stay in the planning module unless an adapter shim is unavoidable.

tasks.title: Dashboard New Plan: guided planning interview

metadata.problemStatement: Operators need a first-class dashboard path into the planning module so they do not have to memorize CLI JSON; the interview should mirror kit semantics and produce the same artifacts.
metadata.expectedOutcome: One-click New Plan runs list-planning-types / build-plan iteratively; finalize can persist W### for wishlist triage.
metadata.impact: Improves discoverability of planning workflows and reduces friction between extension UI and workspace-kit state.
metadata.constraints: Must stay aligned with AGENT-CLI-MAP and policy gates (expectedPlanningGeneration when required); no duplicate planning logic in the extension.
metadata.successSignals: Operator completes interview from dashboard; wishlist shows new W### with coherent title and problem statement; CLI replay yields same shape.
metadata.evidenceRef: planning:new-feature:2026-04-06T18:38:11.102Z

goals: Let operators start and finish guided planning interviews from the Workflow Cannon dashboard, with visible next questions and a clean handoff to a persisted wishlist artifact when they choose.
artifact.approach: Context-driven workflow guided by planning rules.
majorTechnicalDecisions: Dashboard triggers the same CLI-shaped interview the kit already implements so behavior stays single-sourced; extension only orchestrates prompts and displays kit output.
risksAndConstraints: Constraint: Must stay aligned with AGENT-CLI-MAP and policy gates (expectedPlanningGeneration when required); no duplicate planning logic in the extension.

sourceAnswers.featureGoal: Let operators start and finish guided planning interviews from the Workflow Cannon dashboard, with visible next questions and a clean handoff to a persisted wishlist artifact when they choose.
sourceAnswers.placement: Cursor / VS Code extension dashboard panel (Workflow Cannon webview)
sourceAnswers.technology: TypeScript in the extension host; reuse existing workspace-kit run JSON contracts (build-plan, list-planning-types); no new native dependencies.
sourceAnswers.targetAudience: Maintainers and AI agent operators who drive workspace-kit from the editor.
sourceAnswers.decisionRationale: Dashboard triggers the same CLI-shaped interview the kit already implements so behavior stays single-sourced; extension only orchestrates prompts and displays kit output.

wishlistConversionDecomposition.rationale: Vertical slice: dashboard-driven guided interview that drives the same planning module flows as CLI (list-planning-types, build-plan iterations) and can finalize into wishlist intake without reimplementing kit policy in the extension.
wishlistConversionDecomposition.boundaries: No duplicate planning business logic in extension—call workspace-kit / shared contracts; respect expectedPlanningGeneration and AGENT-CLI-MAP; MVP may use subprocess or extension bridge already used elsewhere.
wishlistConversionDecomposition.dependencyIntent: Standalone from T780; optional ordering with extension work but no hard dependsOn.

tasks.title: Collaboration profiles: slash + CLI discoverability

metadata.problemStatement: Agent-behavior and chat playbooks are easy to miss next to task-engine and dashboard flows.
metadata.expectedOutcome: Users discover Cursor slash /collaboration-profiles (hub), /onboarding, /behavior-interview, and matching wk run agent-behavior commands from one mental model.
metadata.impact: Better default agent sessions without implying chat overrides policyApproval JSON.
metadata.constraints: Advisory profiles only; document that chat cannot substitute policy gates.
metadata.successSignals: Dashboard or README surfaces /collaboration-profiles; fewer "how do I set temperament?" questions.
metadata.evidenceRef: Feature breakdown item 12; .cursor/commands/collaboration-profiles.md

wishlistConversionDecomposition.rationale: Single slice: surface existing collaboration-profile slash commands and agent-behavior CLI from dashboard/README so operators find them without spelunking.
wishlistConversionDecomposition.boundaries: No policy tier or policyApproval semantics changes; advisory profiles only; no new agent-behavior engines beyond discoverability.
wishlistConversionDecomposition.dependencyIntent: Standalone; no task dependencies.

tasks.title: Add automatic checkpoints and task-linked rewind for safe autonomous execution

metadata.problemStatement:
Concept: Capture cheap rollback points before major mutating steps. Claude Code checkpointing lowers the trust barrier for autonomous edits.

Why this matters: More autonomy requires more reversibility.
metadata.expectedOutcome: Checkpoint subsystem recording: git commit or stash refs; changed files; task ID; agent ID; action type; timestamp. Commands: create-checkpoint, list-checkpoints, rewind-to-checkpoint, compare-checkpoint. Auto checkpoints before: multi-file writes, dependency upgrades, generated refactors, PR creation, risky task transitions.
metadata.impact: Operators can experiment with agentic batches with a governed safety net.
metadata.constraints:
Suggested architecture: Store pointers + file manifest; integrate with git workspace assumptions; never destroy reflog silently.

Likely dependencies: Hooks (before-write/before-pr); policy for rewind; clear docs when submodules/vendor dirs involved.

Decomposition notes: Opt-in per workspace first; default auto-checkpoint for high-risk manifest ops.
metadata.successSignals: Rewind restores working tree predictably; checkpoints listed per task; compare shows diff summary; tests cover stash and commit modes.
metadata.evidenceRef: docs/maintainers/runbooks/wishlist-workflow.md; maintainer wishlist batch 2026-04-02

wishlistConversionDecomposition.rationale: Split design and persistence from read-only CLI, then rewind with explicit safety rails, then optional auto-checkpoints so each slice is shippable and testable without one mega-diff.
wishlistConversionDecomposition.boundaries: In scope: git working-tree rollback points via stash or named commit pointers, per-task listing and compare, rewind that restores trees predictably, hooks for high-risk automation. Out of scope: cloud or VM snapshots, IDE-native checkpoint UX, destructive reflog rewriting.
wishlistConversionDecomposition.dependencyIntent: T748 locks the record model and store; T749 implements create/list/compare; T750 adds rewind and automated tests; T751 layers auto-checkpoint triggers after the manual commands exist.

tasks.title: Cursor 3 subagent for task-flow execution

metadata.problemStatement: Task delivery playbooks, maintainer-delivery-loop rules, and workspace-kit CLI steps are spread across many Cursor rules and docs. General-purpose agents load a large, overlapping rule surface, which increases context cost and makes it easy to skip or contradict task-engine policy (e.g. transitions vs chat-only claims).
metadata.expectedOutcome: A dedicated Cursor 3 subagent (or equivalent packaged agent profile) owns end-to-end task flow: queue inspection, run-transition discipline, branch/PR loop, and linked rules/skills co-located with that agent so the main session stays thinner. Where feasible, task-flow guidance is isolated from unrelated repo rules.
metadata.impact: Lower token load for non-task work, higher reliability on policy-sensitive kit commands, clearer handoff when someone explicitly wants task execution mode.
metadata.constraints: Must stay aligned with workspace-kit as source of truth; cannot replace policyApproval or CLI evidence; Cursor product capabilities (subagent packaging, rule scoping) may limit true isolation; avoid duplicating canonical docs in ways that drift.
metadata.successSignals: Documented subagent invocation pattern; scoped rule/skill bundle used in pilot sessions; fewer missed run-transition steps in maintainer delivery; optional metrics from team dogfooding.
metadata.evidenceRef: User request 2026-04-05 — abstract task flows into Cursor 3 subagent with task work and rules

wishlistConversionDecomposition.rationale: Wishlist bundles Cursor packaging research, concrete scoped artifacts (rules/skills/playbook pointers), and pilot evidence. Linear chain keeps design constraints frozen before implementation and avoids shipping dead config.
wishlistConversionDecomposition.boundaries: Does not change workspace-kit policy or replace policyApproval; Cursor product limits are documented rather than papered over; no wholesale duplication of .ai canon—only thin entry paths and scoped bundles.
wishlistConversionDecomposition.dependencyIntent: T745 blocks T746; T746 blocks T747.

tasks.title: Doctor + CLI: bridge native SQLite errors to consumer recovery runbook

acceptanceCriteria: Merged change ties real failure output to **`native-sqlite-consumer-install.md`** without asking readers to grep transcripts.

planning-generation-required and idempotency behavior unchanged for existing mutators (regression tests).

tasks.summary: Replace fragmented validation (pilot-only AJV, separate run-contracts checks, manifest, instructions) with one coherent pipeline for policy-sensitive and high-traffic commands.
tasks.description: Strategic follow-up from architecture review. Persisted-artifacts inventory explicitly calls out broader run validation beyond Phase 50 pilot and T602. Today only run-transition, dashboard-summary, create-task, update-task get early AJV (run-args-pilot-validation.ts). Tier B mutators fail later or with weaker structure. Goal: fewer agent/CI round-trips, single maintainer workflow when adding commands.
tasks.risk: Medium: stricter validation may surface legacy callers; document migration in CHANGELOG.

tasks.title: Improvement pipeline: optional heuristic v2 scoring (deterministic)
tasks.approach: Introduce separate heuristic_2 path in confidence.ts (or sibling module): weighted value score, novelty multiplier, evidence floor for admission. Keep heuristic_1 default until explicitly enabled via config; golden tests to prevent silent admission drift.
technicalScope: Config key under improvement.* for heuristic version.
ingest.ts branches on version; document in CHANGELOG + FEATURE-MATRIX.
Do not remove max-signal heuristic without migration note.
acceptanceCriteria: heuristic_1 unchanged when config absent or default.
heuristic_2 covered by unit tests with fixed inputs.
Instructions mention when to enable v2.

tasks.title: Improvement Scout: read-only or dry-run JSON scout report command
tasks.approach: Add improvement module command or extend generate-recommendations dryRun to emit structured candidate findings (no task persistence): lens, zone, stem, evidence refs, classification. Separate explicit proposal-open step remains policy-gated.
technicalScope: Router + instruction surface + AGENT-CLI-MAP tier row.
Contracts schema if response is part of run contracts.
Reuse existing policy rehearsal patterns where applicable.
acceptanceCriteria: JSON output validates against documented shape.
Default path does not create tasks or mutate planning without explicit flags.
Tests cover response codes and no-op persistence.

tasks.title: Improvement Scout: rotation memory in improvement state
tasks.approach: Persist bounded history of last scout runs (primaryLens, adversarialLens, targetZone, questionStem, runAt) in improvement state or sibling JSON under .workspace-kit/improvement/. Provide pure helper to compute next weighted choice (40/30/20/10) with deterministic tests; optional seed for CI.
technicalScope: Schema bump + migration if needed in improvement-state.ts.
Wire read/write only from improvement module or a small script; no task-engine hand-edits.
Document operator expectations in config.md or playbook.
acceptanceCriteria: State shape versioned and backward compatible.
Unit tests for weighting and cap on history length.
Doctor or config docs mention new fields if user-visible.

tasks.title: Improvement Scout: structured proposal metadata for improvement tasks
tasks.approach: Standardize optional metadata keys for scout-origin proposals (primaryLens, adversarialLens, findingType, evidenceAnchors, riskNotes, noveltyHint). Align manual create-task and any future machine path with improvement-task-payload / generate-recommendations metadata where appropriate.
technicalScope: Document field semantics in playbook or TERMS-adjacent note.
Extend buildImprovementTaskPayload and/or ingest metadata only when emitting automated tasks.
Keep heuristic_1 admission unchanged unless paired with heuristic v2 task.
acceptanceCriteria: Documented metadata contract for scout proposals.
At least one example improvement task or template uses the shape.
No regression to existing improvement rows / list-tasks filters.

tasks.title: Improvement Scout: playbook (lenses, target zones, question stems, adversarial pass)
tasks.approach: Author a dedicated playbook (or scoped section under improvement discovery) in .ai/playbooks with stable id; regenerate docs/maintainers mirror. Encode primary+adversarial lens roulette, target zone list aligned to kit surfaces, question-stem roulette, evidence floor (≥2 anchors, one repo-concrete), classification rubric, emit cap 1–3 per interval, and scout-vs-open two-step procedure.
technicalScope: Define lens catalog (determinism, operator friction, policy confusion, doc drift, config surprise, persistence integrity, module boundary, extension contract, release gates, utility expansion) and adversarial prompts.
Link AGENT-CLI-MAP, ARCHITECTURE, improvement-task-discovery; avoid duplicating Tier B policy prose.
Run pnpm run generate-maintainer-docs-from-ai after .ai edits per pipeline ADR.
acceptanceCriteria: Playbook exists with id and is linked from playbooks README / MACHINE-PLAYBOOKS / optional requestable .mdc if desired.
Maintainer render under docs/maintainers/playbooks/ matches .ai source after generation.
Procedure is executable by an agent without opening tasks until proposal pass criteria met.

tasks.title: Operator footguns: clean JSON from CLI, concurrent task writers, relational `approach` column shape
tasks.approach:
Transcript **agent-transcripts/58bf4b13-f34c-40ce-a58e-f9df524876d0/** captures three maintainability problems:

(1) **pnpm** / npm wrappers can prepend noise to stdout and break JSON consumers—document **node dist/cli.js run …** (or noise-free invocation) in **AGENT-CLI-MAP**.

(2) Parallel **mutating** `workspace-kit run` calls can **lose updates** (read–modify–write). **planningGeneration** + **expectedPlanningGeneration** (Phase 44+) mitigates when policy is **require**—document the expectation for agents and humans.

(3) Relational SQLite persists **approach** as a single **TEXT** column; passing **string[]** through **update-task** caused **better-sqlite3** “Too many parameter values”—document scalar-vs-array rules; optional follow-up **T###** for stricter validation.

**Expected outcome:** maintainer docs close each footgun with copy-paste-safe examples; optional code validation is a separate execution task if needed.
technicalScope: **AGENT-CLI-MAP.md** — JSON stdout, pnpm banner hazard, canonical invocation examples.
**task-persistence-operator.md** (or adjacent) — concurrent writers + generation / retry story.
Relational field guide: **approach**/**summary**/**description** are strings; use **technicalScope** arrays or **description** for bullet lists.
acceptanceCriteria: Each of the three themes has an anchor heading + at least one copy-paste command block.
Transcript path cited for provenance.
Improvement **completed** with merged docs or split to a **T###** for validation code with this id **cancelled** + link.

tasks.title: Document safe patterns for parsing workspace-kit JSON output in shell scripts
tasks.approach: Problem: Wrapping **`pnpm exec wk run …`** in scripts often breaks because stdout is **pretty-printed, multi-line JSON** (and stderr can interleave). Operators see exit failures even after the kit **already applied** mutations—especially when batching **create-task**, **update-wishlist**, or **run-transition**. Goal: maintainer-facing guidance and optional contract tests so script authors parse **one JSON value from stdout** reliably.
technicalScope: Add a short subsection to **docs/maintainers/AGENT-CLI-MAP.md** or a linked runbook: parse **full stdout** (trim), avoid line-splitting; mention **stderr**; reference **clientMutationId** for idempotent retries.
Cross-link from **README.md** “Developing” or contributor path if scripts are common.
Optional: a **test/** or contract check that selected commands emit a single JSON document on success stdout.
acceptanceCriteria: Following the new doc, a maintainer can wrap **wk run** without newline-based JSON hacks.
Doc calls out multiline JSON and distinguishes **parse failure** from **server-side partial apply** scenarios.
**pnpm run check** passes.

Implementation strategy: Improve test harness and assertion messages in existing `test/` (e.g. `.mjs` tests) without changing production behavior unless a test exposes a real bug (separate task). Keep changes limited to `test/**` and test helpers unless a bug is confirmed and user approves product fix.
acceptanceCriteria: `pnpm test` passes; any remaining failure prints enough context to fix in one iteration without reading unrelated files.

tasks.title: Add a plugin platform for installable capability bundles (Claude Code–compatible)

metadata.problemStatement:
Concept: Beyond core modules and skills, support installable plugins packaging commands, subagents, hooks, skills, and integration adapters—like Claude Code plugins across the coding loop. Turn modularity into an ecosystem.

Why this matters: Without this, new capability lands in core or bespoke customization—does not scale.

Claude Code interoperability (binding): The plugin platform must use the same plugin model Claude Code uses: on-disk layout, manifest/schema, and packaging conventions published by Anthropic for Claude Code plugins. A plugin that installs in Claude Code must generally install and run in Workflow Cannon on a configured plugin root without maintaining a separate WC-only plugin format for the same artifact. Any unsupported Claude-only runtime features must be explicitly documented as non-goals or no-ops.
metadata.expectedOutcome:
Plugin manifest: identity/version; provided commands, skills, subagents, hooks; required permissions; compatibility constraints—aligned with Claude Code plugin manifest and directory conventions (cite current official Anthropic docs in ADR). Commands: list-plugins, install-plugin, enable-plugin, disable-plugin, inspect-plugin. Sequencing: after hooks, skills, and subagents exist so the platform is not an empty shell.

Acceptance bar: a reference plugin that matches Claude Code's published plugin layout loads in CI; Workflow Cannon enumerates and inspects it the same way Claude Code would recognize the package (modulo documented gaps).
metadata.impact: Third parties and orgs extend WC without core forks; reuse Claude Code plugin artifacts where possible.
metadata.constraints:
Suggested architecture: Node package or folder-based plugins; semver compatibility with workspace-kit; sandbox permissions model.

Likely dependencies: Hooks engine; skill packs (incl. Claude-shaped SKILL.md trees); subagent registry; security review for install path.

Decomposition notes: Phase rollout—read-only plugins first; mutating plugins behind policy; document vs @workflow-cannon scope.

tasks.title: Add supervisor-led multi-agent team execution over the task engine

metadata.problemStatement:
Concept: A lead agent should decompose a task, assign subtasks to worker agents, collect results, and reconcile them into task transitions. Claude Code supports agent teams; Cursor supports async remote agents—but Workflow Cannon should own orchestration so the system stays deterministic and auditable.

Why this matters: This turns Workflow Cannon from a disciplined single-agent workflow into a coordinated development system.
metadata.expectedOutcome: Team-run orchestration layer with: supervisor agent; one or more named workers; assignment records linked to task IDs; result handoff contracts; merge/review checkpoints. Cursor background agents may be one execution target; Workflow Cannon remains conductor. Each worker emits structured evidence before any task transition is accepted.
metadata.impact: Enables scaled, multi-actor delivery without sacrificing task-engine authority or policy gates.
metadata.constraints:
Suggested architecture: Supervisor/worker model with persisted assignments and handoff schemas; explicit checkpoints before transitions; evidence bundle required on reconcile.

Likely dependencies: Subagent or worker registry; task metadata extensions; possibly remote-run adapter (separate wishlist). Policy sensitivity for multi-actor transitions.

Decomposition notes: Model assignments as first-class records; define minimal handoff contract v1; integrate with get-next-actions only where appropriate (supervisor-owned queue).
metadata.successSignals: Assignment records queryable via CLI; supervisor can close loop with evidence; blocked/invalid handoffs fail closed; docs describe team-run vs single-agent paths.
metadata.evidenceRef: docs/maintainers/runbooks/wishlist-workflow.md; maintainer wishlist batch 2026-04-02

wishlistConversionDecomposition.rationale: Decompose wishlist T562 into three execution slices: (1) ADR plus SQLite persistence for supervisor/worker assignments and handoff contract v1; (2) CLI to create/list/complete assignments with validation; (3) supervisor-queue documentation and bounded get-next-actions integration or explicit deferral, plus maintainer runbook. All build on Phase 57 subagent spawn foundation (T664).
wishlistConversionDecomposition.boundaries: No remote-run adapter (separate wishlist T567); no plugin platform (T570); no automatic launch of Cursor/Claude workers—kit records evidence and contracts only. Multi-actor run-transition policy changes are out of scope unless already aligned with existing policy lanes.
wishlistConversionDecomposition.dependencyIntent: Linear chain T665 → T666 → T667. Each task dependsOn T664 (subagent spawn/message v1) so team orchestration starts after registry+spawn MVP.

tasks.title: Add native subagents with isolated context and scoped permissions

metadata.problemStatement:
Concept: Workflow Cannon should spawn specialist agents for planning, implementation, testing, debugging, review, and docs—each with its own instructions, tool permissions, and bounded context. Claude Code treats custom subagents as first-class; that is a clear leverage point without abandoning Workflow Cannon governance.

Why this matters: Workflow Cannon is strong at task governance and command discipline but is still more of a controlled workflow engine than an agent runtime. Subagents would let it become both.
metadata.expectedOutcome: Workflow Cannon exposes a subagent registry and lifecycle: register-subagent, list-subagents, spawn-subagent, message-subagent, retire-subagent. Subagent definitions include role prompts, allowed commands/tools, evidence requirements, and lifecycle state. Every spawned worker has traceable provenance in the existing task/persistence model. Cursor remains the interface and execution surface; Workflow Cannon owns definitions, permissions, evidence, and state.
metadata.impact: Closes the gap between disciplined single-pipeline workflows and a real multi-role agent runtime while keeping policy and audit in kit-owned stores.
metadata.constraints:
Suggested architecture: Build the subagent registry in Workflow Cannon, not in Cursor. Cursor = interface/execution; kit = definitions, role prompts, allowed tools/commands, evidence requirements, lifecycle.

Likely dependencies: Task engine persistence extensions; module command surface; policy/manifest alignment for scoped permissions; documentation in maintainer canon.

Decomposition notes: Start with registry schema + list/get; add spawn/message as thin adapters to host (Cursor) execution; require task linkage and mutation evidence for each spawn; consider parity with existing agent-behavior module boundaries.

---

Maintainer dedupe (2026-04-04): Intake T561 was an identical duplicate of this row (same title and all wishlist fields). T561 was rejected (cancelled). No additional scope was merged.
metadata.successSignals: CLI documents and implements the five commands; spawned subagents appear in persistence with task IDs and evidence; strict validation rejects over-permissioned definitions; maintainer runbook describes Cursor handoff.
metadata.evidenceRef: docs/maintainers/runbooks/wishlist-workflow.md; maintainer wishlist batch 2026-04-02

wishlistConversionDecomposition.rationale: Deliver native subagents in three slices: durable registry model first, then definition CRUD and list/inspect commands with strict validation, then spawn/message lifecycle with task-linked provenance and maintainer runbook for Cursor handoff.
wishlistConversionDecomposition.boundaries: Excludes Cursor extension UI, async remote workers (separate wishlist), and full plugin platform. Claude Code patterns are reference material, not a full interoperability gate in this bundle.
wishlistConversionDecomposition.dependencyIntent: T662 blocks T663; T663 blocks T664. Follow-up tasks may split spawn vs message adapters if implementation demands.

tasks.title: Add GitHub-native invocation for issues, PR comments, and review loops

metadata.problemStatement:
Concept: Workflow Cannon should be invokable from GitHub comments and issue states, not only local shell or Cursor. Claude Code exposes GitHub Actions; Cursor Bugbot reviews PRs—WC needs an opinionated execution layer on top.

Why this matters: WC feels like a repo teammate, not only a local tool.
metadata.expectedOutcome: GitHub integration adapter: open task from issue comment; launch implementation from labeled issue; request review/fix cycles from PR comments; map GitHub events to playbooks. First slash/trigger commands: /cannon-plan, /cannon-implement, /cannon-review, /cannon-fix-review. All GitHub-triggered work routes through policy and task transitions.
metadata.impact: Bridges async collaboration on GitHub with kit governance and evidence.
metadata.constraints:
Suggested architecture: GitHub App or Actions workflow calling a thin runner that invokes workspace-kit with locked-down tokens; event→playbook map in config.

Likely dependencies: Secure credential story; webhook validation; rate limits; maintainer RELEASING alignment.

Decomposition notes: Start with comment-triggered plan-only path; add implement after policy review; never bypass run-transition.
metadata.successSignals: Documented triggers; sample workflow; end-to-end test or dry-run mode; audit log links GitHub event to task ids.
metadata.evidenceRef: docs/maintainers/runbooks/wishlist-workflow.md; maintainer wishlist batch 2026-04-02

wishlistConversionDecomposition.rationale: GitHub integration spans security/ADR, config contracts, webhook+runner MVP, expanded slash flows with policy+audit, and shippable sample+docs; split so plan-only path can ship before implement/review commands pass policy review per wishlist constraints.
wishlistConversionDecomposition.boundaries: Runner stays out of core package default install unless ADR says otherwise; no bypass of run-transition; secrets never logged; v1 may be Actions-based sample before full GitHub App.
wishlistConversionDecomposition.dependencyIntent: Linear T649 → T650 → T651 → T652 → T653; optional cross-phase deps to credential hardening tasks added by maintainer later.

tasks.title: Add deterministic hooks around agent and task lifecycle events

metadata.problemStatement:
Concept: Workflow Cannon should support hook points before/after key events: prompt submission, command execution, file mutation, test execution, task transition, PR creation. Claude Code hooks let teams enforce behavior in code instead of prompt obedience.

Why this matters: Fits WC architecture: strengthens policy, evidence, and consistency.
metadata.expectedOutcome: Hook engine with: named hook events; ordered handlers; allow/deny/modify outcomes; structured hook logs. Initial events: before-task-transition, after-task-transition, before-command, after-command, before-write, after-write, before-pr, after-pr. Shell hooks and Workflow Cannon-native hooks first; HTTP hooks later.
metadata.impact: Organizations can codify guardrails and automation at lifecycle edges without forking core.
metadata.constraints:
Suggested architecture: Ordered handler chain with explicit outcomes; persist hook traces for audit; fail-closed vs warn modes configurable.

Likely dependencies: Command router integration; task transition pipeline; config schema for hook registration; security review for shell hooks.

Decomposition notes: Ship read-only observation hooks first; then mutating/modify path; document performance budgets.
metadata.successSignals: Handlers run in deterministic order; logs appear in machine-readable evidence; integration tests cover deny and modify; maintainer docs list supported events.
metadata.evidenceRef: docs/maintainers/runbooks/wishlist-workflow.md; maintainer wishlist batch 2026-04-02

wishlistConversionDecomposition.rationale: T563 spans architecture, read-only telemetry, mutating guardrails, and maintainer-facing documentation—sequenced so observation and trace semantics land before deny/modify, matching the intake decomposition notes.
wishlistConversionDecomposition.boundaries: HTTP webhook delivery and deep third-party CI integrations are out of scope except as explicit deferrals in maintainer docs. Host/editor adapters stay thin; core semantics and persistence live in workspace-kit.
wishlistConversionDecomposition.dependencyIntent: T645 (ADR + config) blocks T646 (read-only runtime); T646 blocks T647 (mutate + shell hardening); T647 blocks T648 (PR events + docs + budgets).

tasks.title: Add reusable skill packs for domain and workflow capabilities

metadata.problemStatement:
Concept: Claude Code Skills package reusable capability bundles. Workflow Cannon should have an equivalent so teams install and invoke domain abilities (e.g. React delivery, MarkLogic debugging, release prep, test repair).

Why this matters: Reuse without hardcoding behavior into core.
metadata.expectedOutcome: Skill contract = folder with metadata, instructions, optional commands, optional hooks, optional templates, optional policy requirements. Commands: list-skills, inspect-skill, apply-skill, recommend-skills. Skills attachable to tasks, subagents, and playbooks.
metadata.impact: Ecosystem-friendly reuse layer between core modules and one-off prompts.
metadata.constraints:
Suggested architecture: Versioned skill manifest; discovery from workspace + optional package paths; apply-skill writes evidence and respects policy.

Likely dependencies: Hooks (optional); module command registration; documentation generation for skill index.

Decomposition notes: Align with Cursor/Codex skill folders where practical; keep kit authoritative for policy attachment.
metadata.successSignals: Sample skill pack in repo or fixture; CLI lists and inspects; apply-skill records provenance; TERMS/AGENT-CLI-MAP updated when stable.
metadata.evidenceRef: docs/maintainers/runbooks/wishlist-workflow.md; maintainer wishlist batch 2026-04-02

wishlistConversionDecomposition.rationale: Wishlist T564 spans manifest design, read-only discovery, mutating apply with policy, attachment surfaces, and recommendation/docs; split so each slice ships with tests and maintainer contracts without blocking on a future hooks platform.
wishlistConversionDecomposition.boundaries: v1 does not require the subagent registry or full hook engine; apply-skill may shell out to documented workspace-kit commands only; recommend-skills v1 is deterministic (manifest metadata/tags), not ML.
wishlistConversionDecomposition.dependencyIntent: T640 → T641 → T642 → T643 → T644 linear; no cross-links to T630–T639 unless maintainer adds later.

tasks.title: Add native subagents with isolated context and scoped permissions

metadata.problemStatement:
Concept: Workflow Cannon should spawn specialist agents for planning, implementation, testing, debugging, review, and docs—each with its own instructions, tool permissions, and bounded context. Claude Code treats custom subagents as first-class; that is a clear leverage point without abandoning Workflow Cannon governance.

Why this matters: Workflow Cannon is strong at task governance and command discipline but is still more of a controlled workflow engine than an agent runtime. Subagents would let it become both.
metadata.expectedOutcome: Workflow Cannon exposes a subagent registry and lifecycle: register-subagent, list-subagents, spawn-subagent, message-subagent, retire-subagent. Subagent definitions include role prompts, allowed commands/tools, evidence requirements, and lifecycle state. Every spawned worker has traceable provenance in the existing task/persistence model. Cursor remains the interface and execution surface; Workflow Cannon owns definitions, permissions, evidence, and state.
metadata.impact: Closes the gap between disciplined single-pipeline workflows and a real multi-role agent runtime while keeping policy and audit in kit-owned stores.
metadata.constraints:
Suggested architecture: Build the subagent registry in Workflow Cannon, not in Cursor. Cursor = interface/execution; kit = definitions, role prompts, allowed tools/commands, evidence requirements, lifecycle.

Likely dependencies: Task engine persistence extensions; module command surface; policy/manifest alignment for scoped permissions; documentation in maintainer canon.

Decomposition notes: Start with registry schema + list/get; add spawn/message as thin adapters to host (Cursor) execution; require task linkage and mutation evidence for each spawn; consider parity with existing agent-behavior module boundaries.
metadata.successSignals: CLI documents and implements the five commands; spawned subagents appear in persistence with task IDs and evidence; strict validation rejects over-permissioned definitions; maintainer runbook describes Cursor handoff.

Document in README maintainer note + CLI-VISUAL-GUIDE.
metadata.impact: Lowers terror for casual humans; keeps agent-oriented stdout JSON contract intact.
metadata.constraints: No mandatory interactive flow for CI; do not remove JSON mode; accessibility and VS Code marketplace constraints for extension.
metadata.successSignals: Shipped UX improvement with screenshot or ASCIInema in docs; user-tested path from open repo to first successful mutating command; CHANGELOG consumer note if behavior visible.

wishlistConversionDecomposition.rationale: Arch E spans UX discovery, CLI affordances, and extension/maintainer documentation. A short design pass picks 1–2 shippable affordances so implementation stays bounded; CLI work lands before extension and visual-guide updates reference real behavior.
wishlistConversionDecomposition.boundaries: Stdout remains JSON-first for agents; no mandatory interactive flows required for CI; additive UX only; respect extension marketplace and accessibility constraints; do not remove or gate JSON mode.

tasks.title: [Arch] D — Agent ergonomics: stable errors + instruction linkage

Optional: doctor --agent-instruction-surface enrichment with error catalog links. Update AGENT-CLI-MAP pattern for copy-paste recovery.
metadata.impact: Shorter agent loops; fewer mistaken retries on planning-generation-mismatch and policy tiers.
metadata.constraints: Do not leak sensitive paths in consumer installs; keep stdout JSON shape backward compatible (additive fields ok); align POLICY-APPROVAL wording.
metadata.successSignals: Documented error code table or generated snippet; spot-check run-transition, create-wishlist, generate-recommendations; tests assert codes for invalid payloads where pilot B lands.
metadata.evidenceRef: docs/maintainers/AGENT-CLI-MAP.md; src/contracts/builtin-run-command-manifest.json

wishlistConversionDecomposition.rationale: T602 spans documentation contract, router/task-engine failure paths, and doctor surfaces. Split design (inventory + remediation contract) from implementation, then maintainer/doctor docs, with a linear dependency chain so codes stabilize before catalog expansion.
wishlistConversionDecomposition.boundaries: Additive JSON response fields only; no secrets in consumer-facing remediation strings; align with POLICY-APPROVAL and existing run-args pilot where commands overlap; no command renames or module id changes.

tasks.title: Parent — Installable skill packs (reusable capability bundles)
tasks.approach: Define skill manifest schema and directory layout; implement registry/loader; add CLI surfaces (list-skills, inspect-skill, apply-skill, recommend-skills per phased rollout); allow attachment to task context / plans / future subagents; log skill application to workspace evidence. v1 avoids remote distribution services, dynamic untrusted code execution, and complex skill dependency graphs.
technicalScope: skill.json (or equivalent) manifest: identity, version, description, required inputs, optional instructions path, command templates, optional hook references, optional policy requirements, optional output templates.
Registry resolution order: bundled / workspace / user layers — aligned with config precedence patterns where appropriate.
CLI: list + inspect + apply + (later) recommend; structured JSON for agents.
Task integration: store or reference applied skill ids on task metadata or execution evidence without hidden behavior.
Candidate first skills (deferred to child tasks): react-feature-delivery, test-failure-repair, release-closeout, docs-update, marklogic-debugging, policy-sensitive-change-review.
Out of scope for initial slice: marketplace, remote skill CDN, arbitrary hidden execution, rich cross-skill dependency resolution.
Child work likely: schema, registry, CLI commands, task attach + evidence, template actions, hook-linked behavior, maintainer docs + examples, built-ins, tests.
acceptanceCriteria: Skills are discoverable and inspectable from the CLI with declared boundaries visible in JSON output.
A skill can be attached to a task or workflow context in a documented, logged way.
Skill application affects execution only through declared manifest contents (instructions, templates, declared commands) — no undocumented side channels.
Skill usage produces inspectable evidence suitable for audit and debugging.
Project-local skills can be added without patching core; compatibility with policy approval model is preserved (skills do not bypass policyApproval).
Decomposition into child T### tasks is recorded before implementation execution (this item stays parent/planning until split).
tasks.summary: First-class skill pack artifact: manifest, discovery, inspect/apply CLI, optional templates and hook references; task/workflow attachment + evidence — governance-friendly, not a shadow module system.
tasks.description: Workflow Cannon is modular and documentation-driven but lacks a lightweight way to package repeatable expertise (debugging flows, release prep, framework-specific delivery). Adapt the *concept* of Claude Code Skills into a CLI-native, task-aware, policy-aligned form: explicit manifests, inspectable contents, optional command templates and hook links, durable application evidence. Skills sit above core primitives and below full plugins.

tasks.title: Parent — Deterministic hook framework (command, task, write, session lifecycle)
tasks.approach: Treat hooks as a core subsystem (not Cursor-only). Land hook registry, explicit event contracts, handler result types, hook execution engine, integration at command-router and task-engine transition boundaries, and structured hook evidence in persistence/logging. v1 handler kinds: internal modules first; shell handlers next; defer arbitrary scripting and HTTP. Preserve existing policy guarantees and reproducibility of transitions.
technicalScope: Event families (initial): before-command, after-command, before-task-transition, after-task-transition, before-write, after-write, session-start, session-end — with explicit JSON schemas per payload shape.
Hook registry + loader (manifest or config-driven); deterministic ordering; warn vs block semantics; bounded timeout/failure behavior documented.
Integrate invocation into ModuleCommandRouter / run path and TransitionService (or equivalent) without weakening policyOperationId / policyApproval flows.
Emit hook-run records traceable to command id / task id / transition id; no hidden mutation — deny/modify paths must leave policy-grade traces.
Out of scope for this parent task slice: distributed remote hooks, marketplace, complex multi-agent async orchestration, unguarded user scripting.

Child work likely: hook manifest schema, registry/loader, engine, router integration, transition integration, logging/evidence model, CLI list/inspect/test, docs + policy guidance, shell handler support, harness tests.
acceptanceCriteria: Hook definitions are listable/inspectable via documented CLI or introspection path.
Hooks run at declared lifecycle boundaries with deterministic ordering.
Blocking hooks can deny operations with structured, logged reasons; non-blocking hooks can append warnings/evidence only within declared contracts.
Hook runs are durable and traceable to command/task/transition context.
Core task and command flows remain deterministic when hooks are enabled; policy guarantees are not weakened.
Decomposition into child T### tasks is recorded before implementation execution (this item stays parent/planning until split).
tasks.summary: Governed hook registry + execution engine: named lifecycle events, ordered handlers, structured allow/deny/modify/evidence results, durable logging; integrates router + task-engine boundaries.
tasks.description: Workflow Cannon has strong task governance and policy but no native event-interception layer for before/after important actions. Borrow the *idea* of Claude Code-style lifecycle hooks (named events, predictable handler runs) while centering Workflow Cannon on CLI command execution, task transitions, policy-sensitive ops, and evidence — not porting their implementation. Goal: deterministic, inspectable, policy-aware hooks so behavior can be enforced in code with bounded failure modes and no silent task mutation.

Target phase default: (1) If docs/maintainers/data/workspace-kit-status.yaml next_kit_phase has no tasks in non-terminal execution statuses (proposed, ready, in_progress, blocked, paused), use that phaseKey and a matching human phase label. (2) Else use the smallest integer phaseKey strictly greater than the maximum phaseKey in use such that no tasks exist in those non-terminal statuses for that key. (3) If still ambiguous or conflicting with roadmap intent, soft-gate and ask the operator.
Persistence: convert-wishlist only via workspace-kit; decomposition plus tasks with phase and phaseKey aligned to the chosen bucket
When tasks.planningGenerationPolicy is require: pass expectedPlanningGeneration from the last list/get response; on planning-generation-mismatch, re-read and retry

tasks.title: Dashboard tasks: drag-and-drop phase and status

metadata.problemStatement: In the VS Code dashboard plugin tasks section, tasks cannot be reorganized by dragging. Phase assignment and status changes require leaving the tree UI or using other flows, which slows triage.
metadata.expectedOutcome: Users can drag tasks within the tasks section. Dropping a task onto a phase folder assigns that task to the corresponding phase. Dropping onto a different top-level (status) folder updates the task status to match that folder, with changes persisted consistently with task-engine rules.
metadata.impact: Faster, more intuitive backlog grooming for maintainers who live in the dashboard; reduces friction between visual organization and actual task state.
metadata.constraints: Must use supported task-engine mutations and valid transitions; no hand-editing the task store; respect policy-sensitive transitions where applicable; extension UX must remain clear (drop targets, errors on invalid drops).
metadata.successSignals: DnD works for phase and status targets; persisted state matches get-task / list-tasks; invalid drops are rejected with understandable feedback; documented or automated verification for the plugin surface.
metadata.evidenceRef: Dashboard extension tasks tree UI (plugin)

wishlistConversionDecomposition.rationale: Separate transition rules and UX contract from implementation and from verification so each slice can ship with focused review.
wishlistConversionDecomposition.boundaries: In-repo Cursor/VS Code extension only; persist via existing `workspace-kit run` commands (`assign-task-phase`, `run-transition`). No hand-editing the task store. New task-engine commands only if T557 proves unavoidable.
wishlistConversionDecomposition.dependencyIntent: T557 completes first; then T558; then T559.

metadata.originalObservation: "Chat approval doesn't count" repeated everywhere—correct for safety, exhausting as ritual. The system trusts JSON flags more than the human in the thread, which is logically consistent and emotionally weird.

tasks.title: [workspace-kit] Clarify and harden two approval lanes (run JSON vs env)
tasks.approach: Overall plan (and similar issues): Theme: wrong-lane errors and teachable denials. Improve error text, add doctor subcheck, optional CLI hint when env set during `run`, and AGENT-CLI-MAP decision tree updates; same class: any future third lane.
technicalScope: Trace denial paths in `run-command.ts` and config/init entrypoints; catalog confusion cases.
Prototype clearer errors: "you used env approval but this command requires JSON policyApproval" (wording policy-safe).
Update CLI-VISUAL-GUIDE / AGENT-CLI-MAP with a single flowchart entry.
acceptanceCriteria: Representative misuse scenarios get explicit next-step text (no generic failure).
AGENT-CLI-MAP tier table cross-links the two lanes in one subsection.
Tests cover at least one wrong-lane message.

metadata.originalObservation: Two approval lanes that humans and agents will swap — JSON `policyApproval` on `workspace-kit run` vs env `WORKSPACE_KIT_POLICY_APPROVAL` for `config` / `init` / `upgrade`. The design is defensible; the UX is a trap.
metadata.note: Architect review item — Phase 29 execution backlog

tasks.title: [workspace-kit] Centralize policy sensitivity registry for extensions
tasks.approach: Overall plan (and similar issues): Theme: single source of truth + CI enforcement. Extend manifest or codegen; add check script that manifest, policy aggregates, and instruction metadata agree; document extension pattern for module authors.
technicalScope: Map every path that feeds `isSensitiveModuleCommand` / `resolvePolicyOperationIdForCommand`.
Design additive manifest fields or build step to emit policy map.
Extend existing check scripts (`check-builtin-command-manifest`, coverage) for drift detection.
acceptanceCriteria: New module command cannot ship without policy classification in CI (or explicit exempt with reason).
Maintainer doc: "how to mark sensitive" in one page.
No silent downgrade: tests for dynamic + builtin + dry-run matrix.

metadata.originalObservation: Policy sensitivity is a scavenger hunt — Builtins in `builtin-run-command-manifest`, plus `extraSensitiveModuleCommands` from effective config, plus command-specific dry-run exceptions in `policy.ts`. Extend the kit without updating all three and you've invented silent "not sensitive" behavior.
metadata.note: Architect review item — Phase 29 execution backlog

tasks.title: [workspace-kit] Plan task store schemaVersion evolution beyond hard-stop
tasks.phase: Phase 30 — persistence, packaging, and task-store evolution
tasks.approach: Overall plan (and similar issues): Theme: forward-compatible migrations. Version bump playbook, automatic migrate on load for additive changes, doctor reporting; same class: SQLite blob schema inside JSON.
technicalScope: Document current schemaVersion semantics in task-engine workbook.
Design migration hook: load → migrate → save, with backup advice.
Spike one noop v2 or additive field migration.
acceptanceCriteria: Documented migration policy for maintainers.
Either implemented migration path for one safe change OR explicit "no bump until X" decision in ADR.
Tests cover migrate + idempotency.

metadata.originalObservation: Task store schema version `1` hard-stop in JSON path—simple, until you need a migration story that isn't "throw."
metadata.note: Architect review item — Phase 29 execution backlog

tasks.title: [workspace-kit] Unify dual persistence (SQLite default + JSON opt-out) operator story
tasks.phase: Phase 30 — persistence, packaging, and task-store evolution
tasks.approach: Overall plan (and similar issues): Theme: single mental model for operators and agents. Consolidate troubleshooting (doctor output, AGENT-CLI-MAP), consider auto-detect banner, shared invariant tests across backends, and explicit `explain-config` for persistence.
technicalScope: Inventory all user-facing mentions of JSON vs SQLite; classify into canonical vs duplicate.
Add or extend doctor / `resolve-config` output: effective persistence backend + paths.
Ensure parity tests or checklist explicitly runs both paths where feasible.
acceptanceCriteria: One maintainer runbook section: "determine backend + paths + recovery".
CLI surfaces effective backend without reading three docs.
Tests or scripts document parity expectations for both backends.

metadata.originalObservation: Two persistence backends for the same conceptual store — Default SQLite vs JSON opt-out means two code paths, two mental models, and docs that constantly say "unless you're on the other one." Every bug report starts with "which backend?"
metadata.note: Architect review item — Phase 29 execution backlog

tasks.title: [workspace-kit] Rethink JSON-document-in-SQLite task persistence model
tasks.phase: Phase 30 — persistence, packaging, and task-store evolution
tasks.approach: Overall plan (and similar issues): Theme: persistence shape vs operational benefit. Either lean into documents (simplify SQLite to one blob, fewer shapes) or incrementally normalize hot paths (indexes, query by status/id without full parse). Same class: migrations, dual table shapes, load/save costs.
technicalScope: Document current read/write hot paths and migration story (`SqliteDualPlanningStore`, `TaskStore.forSqliteDual`).
Spike minimal schema option (single row versioned blob vs keyed columns) with benchmark or complexity note.
If normalized: define migration from existing files and JSON opt-out parity.
acceptanceCriteria: ADR or design note commits to direction (document-first vs hybrid vs normalized) with rationale.
Migration risk called out with rollback story.
No increase in undocumented table shapes without doctor detection.

metadata.originalObservation: SQLite used as a fancy JSON file — Task state is a `TaskStoreDocument` blob in `TEXT`, not normalized rows. You pay native SQLite operational cost and migration complexity (`legacy-dual` vs `task-only` in `sqlite-dual-planning.ts`) without getting relational querying or clean concurrency story.
metadata.note: Architect review item — Phase 29 execution backlog

tasks.title: [workspace-kit] Reduce native SQLite consumer pain for workspace-kit
tasks.phase: Phase 30 — persistence, packaging, and task-store evolution
tasks.approach: Overall plan (and similar issues): Theme: distribution and install predictability for the same class of issues (native addons, cross-platform CI, consumer docs). Audit alternatives (optional wasm/sql.js, pure JS fallback, documented optional dependency) against determinism and R002; produce ADR + migration path; tighten postinstall/doctor signals; document consumer runbooks in one place.
technicalScope: Survey install failure modes (ABI, corporate proxies, air-gapped) and current mitigations (`ensure-native-sqlite`, AGENTS.md).
Compare trade-offs: keep native default vs optional backend vs bundled prebuilds; align with task-engine persistence ADRs.
Define acceptance tests / parity expectations if adding a fallback path.
acceptanceCriteria: Written decision (ADR or ROADMAP slice) with explicit compatibility stance.
Doctor or install path surfaces actionable recovery in one screen of output.
Maintainer doc links consumer expectations without scattering warnings across 5 files.

metadata.originalObservation: Native `better-sqlite3` on a published workflow CLI — You ship a compiled addon to arbitrary Node versions and machines. The repo itself warns about ABI mismatch and `pnpm rebuild`. That's a support vector shaped like a middle finger to anyone who thought "npm install" meant "it works."

tasks.title: [workspace-kit] Command-scoped first-use policy approval UX (Deny / Allow / Allow for session)
tasks.phase: Phase 9 — Interactive policy UX and template enforcement
tasks.approach: Purpose: Today, sensitive `workspace-kit run` flows require `policyApproval` JSON or pre-recorded session grants; maintainers want an interactive, command-scoped first prompt (Deny / Allow once / Allow for session) where the host environment supports it—without weakening default fail-closed behavior in CI or non-interactive shells. Implementation strategy: Add an opt-in interactive path in the CLI run (and optionally init/upgrade/config) flow that maps user choices to existing constructs (`policyApproval` with `scope`, `recordSessionGrant`, env-based approval where already valid). Prefer stdin/TTY detection; integrate Cursor Question UI or equivalent only behind capability flags so headless and scripts stay deterministic. Align with `.ai/ROADMAP.md` decision D007.
technicalScope: Define UX contract: which operations prompt, exact button/choice mapping to `once` vs `session`, and how `WORKSPACE_KIT_SESSION_ID` interacts with stored grants.

Implement TTY-gated interactive resolver in `src/cli/run-command.ts` (or shared policy helper) that runs only when stdout is TTY and env like `WORKSPACE_KIT_INTERACTIVE_APPROVAL=on` (name TBD) is set; default remains current deny-with-JSON behavior.
Ensure CI/non-interactive runs never hang waiting for input; add tests that simulate non-TTY and assert no prompt.
Where Cursor provides a structured question API, add an optional adapter or document extension pattern without hard-depending on Cursor in core package API surface.
Update `docs/maintainers/POLICY-APPROVAL.md`, runbooks, and `.cursor/rules` mirrors to describe interactive vs JSON vs env paths.
Append policy traces on allow/deny with deterministic messages; deny must not leak secrets.
Extend tests (`test/`): happy path session grant from mock stdin; regression that JSON `policyApproval` still overrides/skips prompt when provided.
acceptanceCriteria: With interactive approval disabled or non-TTY, behavior is identical to pre-task baselines (no prompt, same JSON denial shapes).
With interactive approval enabled and TTY, an operator can approve a sensitive `run` once or for session without hand-authoring JSON, and subsequent same-session command obeys session grant rules.
Documentation lists env vars, limitations (CI, automation), and security rationale (no silent bypass).
`pnpm run build`, `check`, `test`, and `parity` pass; new tests cover interactive and non-interactive branches.

tasks.title: [workspace-kit] Configurable response-template enforcement (advisory default + explicit strict opt-in)
tasks.phase: Phase 9 — Interactive policy UX and template enforcement
tasks.approach: Purpose: `responseTemplates.enforcementMode` exists with `advisory` default; operators need a clear, tested strict mode that fails `workspace-kit run` when template selection is invalid or directives conflict—without breaking existing advisory consumers. Implementation strategy: Audit `src/core/response-template-shaping.ts` and config metadata; ensure strict mode semantics are total (which fields trigger failure codes), documented, and covered by tests; improve error messages for strict violations. Align with `.ai/ROADMAP.md` decision D008.
technicalScope: Inventory all code paths that read `responseTemplates.*` and list behaviors in advisory vs strict per command.
Define strict contract: unknown template id, mismatched template, missing directive when required, warning→error promotion rules.
Implement or tighten strict checks so `enforcementMode` strict produces non-zero exit and stable `code` in JSON output when violations occur.
Ensure default remains `advisory`; upgrading consumers see no new failures until they set `strict`.
Update `docs/maintainers/runbooks/response-templates.md`, `CONFIG` reference (via `config generate-docs` if descriptions change), and FEATURE-MATRIX row when capability is finalized.
Add/extend tests in `test/phase6b-response-templates.test.mjs` (or equivalent) for strict failures and advisory control baseline.
acceptanceCriteria: With default config, existing `workspace-kit run` outcomes match prior release behavior within intentional scope (advisory warnings only).
With `responseTemplates.enforcementMode` set to `strict`, invalid or inconsistent template usage fails the command with actionable JSON and documented `code` values.
Maintainer runbook explains when to use strict (CI gates) vs advisory (local iteration).
`pnpm run build`, `check`, `test`, and `parity` pass.

tasks.title: [workspace-kit] Implement planning-to-task bridge via create-task-from-plan
tasks.approach: Add a controlled bridge from the planning surface into execution so vetted ideas/plans can become canonical tasks without manual file editing.
technicalScope: Implement create-task-from-plan; input shape from planning module or markdown frontmatter—locked in T311.
Preserve provenance: `metadata.planRef` or equivalent.
Tests: valid plan, invalid plan, id collision.
acceptanceCriteria: Planning outputs can be promoted into canonical task records through the engine.
Source-to-task provenance is preserved.
Invalid or incomplete planning inputs are rejected cleanly.
Behavior is test-covered.

tasks.title: [workspace-kit] Implement task summary and blocked-summary query commands
tasks.approach: Add explicit dashboard-grade summary commands instead of overloading get-next-actions for all aggregate task insight.
technicalScope: Implement get-task-summary and get-blocked-summary; align with `dashboard-summary` to avoid duplicate math—factor shared pure functions in task-engine module.
Explicit archived exclusion per T314.
Stable JSON schema documented in instruction files.
acceptanceCriteria: Task summary and blocked-summary are available as dedicated commands.
Output is stable and dashboard-friendly.
Summaries reflect archived-task exclusion rules correctly.
Behavior is test-covered.

tasks.title: [workspace-kit] Implement task history retrieval and recent activity queries
tasks.approach: Expose task-level evidence/history so maintainers and UI surfaces can inspect what happened, not just current state.
technicalScope: Implement get-task-history and get-recent-task-activity; unify with transition + CRUD/dependency evidence streams defined in T311.
Normalize sort order (newest first) and caps for large repos.
Consider deprecating redundant fields on `get-task` if dedicated commands supersede—document migration for extension.
acceptanceCriteria: Task history can be retrieved through a stable command surface.
Recent activity output is deterministic and useful for dashboards/debugging.
CRUD and transition evidence are both visible through history queries.
Behavior is test-covered.

tasks.title: [workspace-kit] Implement dependency mutation commands and graph retrieval
tasks.approach: Make task dependency management a first-class engine operation rather than an indirect data edit.
technicalScope: Implement add-dependency, remove-dependency, get-dependency-graph per T311.
Cycle detection policy: document whether cycles are forbidden or only warned.
Re-run dependency guards / unblock logic after mutation.
Tests: self-edge, missing task id, duplicate edge, cycle.
acceptanceCriteria: Dependencies can be mutated through explicit engine commands.
Invalid dependency mutations fail deterministically.
Readiness and blocked-state implications are handled correctly.
Dependency graph retrieval is machine-usable and test-covered.

tasks.title: [workspace-kit] Implement task archival command and retention behavior
tasks.approach: Add archival instead of hard delete so the engine preserves traceability and avoids destructive queue mutations.
technicalScope: Implement archive-task command.
Schema: add `archived` / `archivedAt` or dedicated `status` value—decided in T311; migrate store version if needed.
Ensure archived tasks excluded from `list-tasks` default and from `get-next-actions` / ready queue.
Preserve history and allow `list-tasks` with `{ includeArchived: true }` if specified in contract.
Add instruction file + tests for queue exclusion and retrieval.
acceptanceCriteria: Tasks can be archived without destructive deletion.
Archived tasks remain retrievable for history/debugging.
Active queue commands exclude archived tasks by default.
Archive behavior is deterministic and test-covered.

tasks.title: [workspace-kit] Implement task update command for mutable task fields
tasks.approach: Add controlled metadata updates for existing tasks without bypassing lifecycle rules or store validation.
technicalScope: Implement update-task command.
Support updates for allowed mutable fields per T311 workbook.
Reject mutation of immutable fields (id, createdAt, status except via run-transition).
Emit structured update evidence.
Add instruction entry and instruction file for update-task.
Add tests for valid updates, immutable-field rejection, and schema validation.
acceptanceCriteria: Existing tasks can be updated safely through the engine.
Immutable-field mutations are rejected explicitly.
Updates are persisted deterministically and emit evidence.
Behavior is fully test-covered for supported fields.

tasks.title: [workspace-kit] Implement task creation command and persistence path

Persist newly created tasks through the canonical task store.
Reject duplicate IDs and invalid schemas.

tasks.title: [workspace-kit] Design Task Engine CRUD and query extension workbook
tasks.approach: Extend the existing Task Engine design workbook so record lifecycle, dependency mutation, history retrieval, and summary/query behavior are specified before implementation.

Define validation rules for create vs update operations.

Define retrieval contracts for get-task-history, get-task-summary, and get-blocked-summary.
Define the bridge contract for create-task-from-plan.
Define evidence requirements for non-transition mutations.
Define error taxonomy additions for CRUD and dependency operations.
acceptanceCriteria: CRUD, dependency, history, and summary functions are specified at implementation-ready detail.

tasks.title: [Cursor extension] Manual E2E checklist and fixture documentation for operators

tasks.title: [Cursor extension] Security and trust pass: sanitization, policy UX, no silent mutation

tasks.title: [Agent/CLI Parity] Add always-applied Cursor rule for `workspace-kit` execution path
