Rule text is under ~80 lines or splits into scoped rule + link to avoid drift.
Maintainer doc notes rule as enforcement mirror of `docs/maintainers/AGENTS.md` CLI section.
Rule explicitly forbids hand-editing `.workspace-kit/tasks/state.json` for lifecycle transitions except documented recovery.

tasks.title: [Agent/CLI Parity] Encode CLI-first execution in `.ai/AGENTS.md` and maintainer `AGENTS.md`
tasks.approach: Update canonical agent guidance so execution parity is a MUST-level rule where the kit provides a command; keep autonomy for normal code work. Deliver explicit copy-paste examples for: task transitions, policy-gated `run`, and config mutation env approval.
technicalScope: Extend `.ai/AGENTS.md` with CLI-first rules, link to Agent CLI map from T285.
Mirror or summarize in `docs/maintainers/AGENTS.md` per documentation contract.
Call out `policyApproval` vs `WORKSPACE_KIT_POLICY_APPROVAL` vs interactive TTY path; forbid relying on chat-only approval for gated ops.
Add a short subsection: 'When the agent must run terminal commands' with 2–3 concrete examples (task transition, `run` with policy JSON, `config` with env approval).
Cross-link to `docs/maintainers/POLICY-APPROVAL.md` and `docs/maintainers/TERMS.md` for terminology alignment.
acceptanceCriteria: Both AGENTS files state: before changing kit-owned state, run the matching `workspace-kit` command.
Source-of-truth ordering still matches project contract; no new conflicting policy vs `.cursor/rules/`.
If `.ai/` and `docs/maintainers/` are intentionally paired, they stay aligned after the change.
At least three copy-paste CLI examples exist across the two files (task + policy + config).

tasks.title: [Agent/CLI Parity] Define CLI-mandatory scope and publish the Agent CLI map
tasks.approach: Treat `workspace-kit` as the execution source for kit-owned mutations; document what may still be edited directly (application code only). Produce one canonical table: intent → command → approval surface → evidence artifact.
technicalScope: Inventory policy-sensitive `run` commands, task-engine transitions, config/init/upgrade mutations, and documentation module entrypoints.
Classify Tier A (task state transitions), Tier B (other `run` module commands), Tier C (non-kit file edits).
Add Maintainer doc section or small `docs/maintainers/` page linked from `AGENTS.md` and `POLICY-APPROVAL.md` (single source for the map).
Cross-check against `docs/maintainers/POLICY-APPROVAL.md` and module `instructions/*.md` so examples stay copy-paste accurate.
acceptanceCriteria: Published map lists every Tier A/B operation with exact `workspace-kit` invocation pattern and approval shape.
Explicit statement that hand-editing `.workspace-kit/tasks/state.json` for lifecycle changes is out of contract except documented recovery.
No contradiction with existing policy and runbook docs; links resolve.

tasks.title: [workspace-kit] Implement parser-backed documentation schema validator in runtime
tasks.phase: Phase 6 automation hardening and response templates
tasks.approach: Replace the current meta-line-only check with a deterministic parser/validator that enforces schema structure, enums, ids, and doc-type-specific required records.
technicalScope: Build parser for AI record lines (`type|k=v|...`) with stable error reporting for malformed lines.
Validate `meta` correctness, supported doc types, status/truth enums, and required-first-record behavior.
Validate schema profile selection and allowed/required record sets per `meta.doc` profile (including `rules`, `runbook`, and `workbook` families).
Add schema/version evolution handling: support `meta.schemaVersion` (or equivalent) and enforce backward-compatible parsing rules with explicit deprecation guidance.
Validate path-policy constraints (output location => expected `meta.doc` profile) and ensure nested templates generate compatible metadata.
Validate `id` namespaces/format + uniqueness (doc-family scoped where appropriate) and detect collisions; add cross-field consistency guards that prevent silent contradictions.
Validate enums and record-level required fields (`risk`, `ap`, `st`, etc.) including unknown-field policy (fail/warn on typos based on strictness tier).
Validate `ref.path` existence and reference integrity by strictness tier (`must_exist` vs advisory), and emit structured evidence including missing paths and suggested fixes.
Enforce deterministic ordering normalization policy for record groups (or report ordering issues when strict) to reduce doc churn.
Integrate validator output into existing generation evidence (`validationIssues`) with stable `check` codes, line/record location, severity, and a short suggested fix when possible.
acceptanceCriteria: Runtime rejects structurally invalid AI docs that previously passed the meta-only check.
Validator enforces per-profile required record sets and disallows unsupported record types for each doc family.
Strict mode fail behavior and advisory mode warning behavior are deterministic and test-covered.
Validator emits structured evidence enabling maintainers to fix failures without re-running excessive generation cycles.
Reference integrity and unknown-field policy are implemented exactly as defined by schema strictness tiers.

tasks.title: [workspace-kit] Add documentation schema compliance test matrix across AI doc families
tasks.phase: Phase 6 automation hardening and response templates
tasks.approach: Prevent regression by adding positive/negative tests for schema enforcement across rules, runbooks, and workbooks plus strict/advisory behavior expectations.
technicalScope: Add fixture-backed tests for valid AI docs by doc family (`rules`, `runbook`, `workbook`) including nested runbooks/workbooks outputs.
Add failure tests for malformed records, invalid enums/ids, duplicate-id collisions, missing required records, unsupported record types per profile, and unknown-field typos.
Add path-policy tests asserting that `.ai/runbooks/*` and `.ai/workbooks/*` emit correct/compatible `meta.doc` profiles and are rejected when mismatched.
Add schema/version evolution tests verifying backward-compatible parsing and explicit deprecation handling for older doc shapes.
Add reference integrity tests verifying `ref.path` existence behavior by strictness tier (advisory vs fail) with structured evidence assertions.
Add deterministic ordering normalization tests (either stable ordering or explicit reporting depending on tier).
Cover `document-project` batch behavior with nested templates and schema validation outcomes, ensuring batch continues/fails as designed by strictness.
acceptanceCriteria: Test suite fails on schema regressions that could produce low-quality/invalid AI docs.
Strict and advisory validation paths are explicitly covered for: unknown fields, missing refs, missing required records, invalid enums/ids, and path-policy violations.
Version evolution/deprecation tests are included to prevent schema upgrade regressions.
Nested doc generation and schema validation behavior stay green in phase gate runs.

tasks.title: [workspace-kit] Extend documentation schema for runbook/workbook coverage and doc-type contracts
tasks.phase: Phase 6 automation hardening and response templates
tasks.approach: Upgrade `documentation-schema.md` from rules-only assumptions to a typed schema contract that explicitly supports `.ai` top-level docs plus nested `runbooks/` and `workbooks/` surfaces.
technicalScope: Add/normalize allowed `meta.doc` values to include `runbook` and `workbook` alongside existing `.ai` doc types.
Define minimal required records per doc class (for example: runbook/workbook/rules/manifest) and record-level field requirements.
Define or normalize record types used by current AI docs (such as `runbook|`, `workbook|`, `chain|`, `state|`, `transition|`) so schema and emitted docs are consistent.
Add explicit schema profile model with per-profile allowed record types, required record sets, and field-level constraints.
Add schema/version evolution contract (`meta.schemaVersion` or equivalent) with backward-compatibility and deprecation guidance.
Define path-policy constraints mapping output location to allowed `meta.doc` profile (top-level docs vs `runbooks/*` vs `workbooks/*`).
Define reference integrity policy (`must_exist` vs advisory) and behavior by validation strictness tier.
Define id namespace/uniqueness constraints and cross-field consistency guards (for example critical risk cannot auto-override approval).
Define strictness tiers (`advisory`, `strict`, `release-gate`) and required validator evidence fields (code, severity, location, suggested fix).
Document deterministic ordering and unknown-field handling policy (including extension namespace guidance).
acceptanceCriteria: Schema contract explicitly supports current `.ai` doc families, including nested runbook/workbook docs.
Per-doc required record expectations are documented and testable.
No active AI doc uses record types that are undefined or ambiguous in schema guidance.
Schema contract defines compatibility and deprecation posture so future record/profile changes are upgrade-safe.
Validation strictness tiers and reference-integrity policies are explicit enough to implement without interpretation gaps.

tasks.title: [workspace-kit] Fix architecture-review doc drift and broken index references
tasks.approach: Purpose: Restore trust in maintainer navigation before deeper refactors—broken links and stale architectural review bullets waste time and invite wrong decisions. Implementation strategy (as shipped): Fix README and `docs/maintainers/*` index pointers to real files; align `ARCHITECTURAL-REVIEW-FINDINGS.md` with post-split reality; add explicit canonical-vs-derived notes where policy appears in multiple surfaces (`README` vs `.cursor/rules/` vs `docs/`). Doc-only scope; no behavior change to the kit runtime.
technicalScope: Audit high-traffic entrypoints (`README.md`, `docs/maintainers/INDEX` or equivalent tables, `ARCHITECTURAL-REVIEW-FINDINGS.md`) for 404s and renamed paths.
Either restore missing targets (e.g. contributor doc) or retarget links to the correct canonical file.
Reconcile duplicate bullets between architectural review and ROADMAP so one defers to the other.
Record canonical source for planning vs narrative docs per `.ai/PRINCIPLES.md` truth order.
acceptanceCriteria: No broken maintainer-doc navigation links from README or primary maintainer index to missing files.
Architecture review and roadmap agree on what is shipped vs historical extraction provenance.
Spot-check: `grep` or link check for stale `CONTRIBUTING` / legacy repo paths returns clean for listed entrypoints.

tasks.title: [workspace-kit] Reconcile package identity and changelog canon
tasks.approach: Purpose: Consumers and CI must see one package name and one changelog contract after the repo split—mixed `quicktask-*` vs `@workflow-cannon/workspace-kit` strings cause install and support churn. Implementation strategy (as shipped): Normalize user-facing strings in CLI, `package.json`, `.workspace-kit/manifest.json`, and maintainer docs; keep historical names only in provenance sections. Single canonical changelog under `docs/maintainers/CHANGELOG.md` (with root pointer pattern if used); align `RELEASING.md` and npm publish notes.
technicalScope: Replace or qualify legacy package id strings in runtime output and packaged metadata.
Ensure `CHANGELOG.md` at repo root, if present, is a stub pointing at maintainer changelog or is the single source—no conflicting histories.
Update release gate docs and version bump scripts to reference canonical paths.
Verify npm package `name` field and kit manifest identity match.
acceptanceCriteria: `npm view` / package consumers see consistent `@workflow-cannon/workspace-kit` naming in documented install paths.
Maintainer can answer “where is the changelog?” in one sentence with one path.
Grep of active docs (excluding historical notes) does not advertise wrong package id for install.

tasks.title: [workspace-kit] Decompose monolithic CLI orchestration into command handlers
tasks.approach: Purpose: A single oversized `cli.ts` couples parsing, policy, config, and output—every change risks unrelated regressions and blocks parallel work. Implementation strategy (as shipped): Introduce `src/cli/run-command.ts` (and related modules) so each subcommand owns its path; shared bootstrap handles argv, workspace resolution, and JSON vs human output. Preserve argv compatibility and exit codes; expand tests per command.
technicalScope: Extract handlers for `init`, `check`, `upgrade`, `drift-check`, `doctor`, `config`, `run`, and other shipped verbs from the former monolith.
Centralize shared concerns: logger, policy context creation, config load, error formatting.
Maintain deterministic JSON output contracts for automation.
Extend `test/cli.test.mjs` (or equivalent) to cover routing and regression cases per handler.
acceptanceCriteria: All previously supported CLI flags and exit codes behave as before (consumer parity tests pass).
New contributors can locate a command’s logic in one module without scrolling a mega-file.
`pnpm test` includes CLI coverage sufficient to catch handler wiring mistakes.

tasks.title: [workspace-kit] Consolidate layered config defaults to a single canonical source
tasks.approach: Purpose: Duplicate default tables in `workspace-kit-config` and modules cause explain output and docs to disagree with runtime. Implementation strategy (as shipped): Single authoritative default map in `src/core/workspace-kit-config.ts` (or successor), with modules registering keys without re-stating defaults; `config explain` and tests read the same source.
technicalScope: Remove redundant default literals from module contribution paths; wire them to the canonical table.
Preserve precedence order: workspace > project > defaults; document any exception.
Regenerate or hand-update maintainer config docs if key ownership strings change.
Run full config resolution tests and spot-check `workspace-kit config explain` output.
acceptanceCriteria: One definition per default key in code; no conflicting literals in separate files.
Existing precedence and explain tests pass unchanged in behavior.
Generated or published config reference matches runtime defaults.

tasks.title: [workspace-kit] Harden documentation runtime path resolution for package consumers
tasks.approach: Purpose: Documentation module previously assumed a git checkout layout (`docs/` next to `src/`); consumers installing from npm only have `dist/` and packaged assets—commands failed opaquely. Implementation strategy (as shipped): Resolve paths from package root via `import.meta.url` / `__dirname` patterns, honor `package.json` `files` so sources ship; config overrides for custom layouts; actionable errors when files are missing.
technicalScope: Audit `src/modules/documentation/runtime.ts` (and callers) for cwd-relative assumptions.
Implement fallback chain: explicit config → packaged module path → error with install hint.
Ensure packaged tarball contains required markdown or generated assets per `package.json` `files`.
Add tests simulating consumer `node_modules` layout where feasible.
acceptanceCriteria: `run` documentation-related commands work when invoked from a consumer project using the published package.
Failures state which path was tried and whether the package appears incomplete.
In-repo maintainer workflow remains supported.

tasks.title: [workspace-kit] Harden actor resolution and policy decision trace ergonomics
tasks.approach: Purpose: Policy traces need a stable `actor` for audit; synchronous git subprocess calls can hang or fail in CI/sandboxes. Implementation strategy (as shipped): Async, bounded actor resolution in `src/core/policy.ts` with env override first (`WORKSPACE_KIT_*` or documented vars), then git with timeout/fallback to `unknown` or explicit service actor; traces remain structured for JSON consumers.
technicalScope: Refactor actor lookup to non-blocking pattern compatible with Node worker/async model used by CLI.
Define precedence: explicit env → git config user → fallback token.
Document expectations for GitHub Actions / containers (no git identity).
Unit tests for timeout, missing git, and override success.
acceptanceCriteria: Policy JSON traces include actor fields consistently in local and simulated CI fixtures.
No indefinite hang when git is missing or slow; bounded failure path.
Tests cover at least: override, success, unknown actor.

tasks.title: [workspace-kit] Improve transcript hook observability and overlap control
tasks.approach: Purpose: Post-completion transcript hooks run asynchronously; without logs or locking, operators see silent skips or double syncs. Implementation strategy (as shipped): `src/core/transcript-completion-hook.ts` writes structured events (or appends to event log), implements busy/lock guard, remains opt-in and non-blocking for task completion.
technicalScope: Emit machine-readable status for hook start/skip/complete/fail paths.
Serialize overlapping invocations (mutex or pid file pattern) within process expectations.
Wire optional verbosity to env or config keys documented in Phase 6 runbooks.
Tests for concurrent transition calls and skip-when-busy.
acceptanceCriteria: Hook outcome is diagnosable from stdout/stderr or log file without debugger.
Rapid sequential task completions do not spawn unsafe overlapping child work.
Default remains opt-in; no hook run when disabled.

tasks.title: [workspace-kit] Canonicalize policy/workflow governance surfaces and ADR hygiene
tasks.approach: Purpose: Duplicated guidance between `.cursor/rules/`, `docs/maintainers/`, and `.ai/` causes agents to follow stale rules. Implementation strategy (as shipped): Document canonical ownership (which file is edited first), when to mirror vs generate, and ADR minimums; update `DECISIONS.md`, `docs/adr/README` or equivalent, and cross-links in README/ROADMAP.
technicalScope: Map each governance concern (release, PR, task completion) to a primary doc and mark derivatives.
Add short “do not duplicate long sections” rule for Cursor rules pointing to maintainer docs.
Align ADR folder expectations with actual usage (when to add ADR vs roadmap decision line).
Ensure `.ai/PRINCIPLES.md` and workflow contract references stay consistent.
acceptanceCriteria: Onboarding answers “which doc do I edit?” for policies vs agent rules vs AI canonical docs.
No contradictory release/task instructions between top-level maintainer docs and rules without an explicit mirror note.
ADR guidance matches how the repo is maintained (no mandatory fiction).

tasks.title: [workspace-kit] Add path-aware AI meta/doc defaults for nested documentation surfaces
tasks.phase: Phase 6 automation hardening and response templates
tasks.approach: Make AI output metadata path-aware so generated docs under `.ai/runbooks/` and `.ai/workbooks/` emit correct `meta.doc` values instead of defaulting to rules semantics.
technicalScope: Implement deterministic path-to-doc-type resolver for top-level docs, `runbooks/*`, and `workbooks/*` templates.
Apply resolver in AI output bootstrap path (`generateDocument`) and validate compatibility with schema validator.
Ensure nested template generation preserves relative documentType while emitting matching metadata contract.
Document mapping behavior in documentation module instructions and README.
acceptanceCriteria: Generated AI docs under nested surfaces emit correct `meta.doc` classification.
No generated nested doc defaults to incorrect rules metadata.
Path-aware mapping is deterministic and validated in tests.

tasks.title: [workspace-kit] Add frequent-run safety rails without suppressing repeated recommendation analysis
tasks.approach: Bound runtime overhead via scan/window/time caps and backoff policies while preserving ability to generate multiple recommendations from unchanged evidence sets.
technicalScope: Define hard caps for transcript scan count, bytes/time budget, and per-run candidate processing.
Define scheduling/backoff policy that does not gate recommendation generation solely on evidence freshness.
Document and test behavior under repeated runs against the same dataset.
acceptanceCriteria: Frequent runs remain operationally bounded and predictable.
System can produce additional recommendations from existing evidence when heuristics/rules permit.

tasks.title: [workspace-kit] Implement transcript source autodiscovery and path validation
tasks.approach: Reduce setup friction by discovering likely transcript sources and validating configured paths before sync.
technicalScope: Add source path discovery order and explicit precedence with config overrides.
Validate readability/access and emit actionable diagnostics for missing/inaccessible paths.
Keep discovery deterministic and safe for local-only archives.
acceptanceCriteria: Users can sync transcripts without manual path setup in common environments.
Path failures are explicit and recoverable.

tasks.title: [workspace-kit] Harden sync/ingest idempotency and dedupe diagnostics
tasks.approach: Guarantee deterministic reruns and expose exact skip reasons for files and recommendation candidates.
technicalScope: Add deterministic run identifiers and structured skip-reason taxonomy.
Ensure repeated sync/ingest runs are idempotent across process restarts.
Expose machine-readable dedupe metrics for observability.
acceptanceCriteria: Repeated runs do not create duplicate archives or recommendation tasks.
Operators can see why items were skipped.

tasks.title: [workspace-kit] Add operator status surface for transcript sync and recommendation runs
tasks.approach: Provide quick diagnostics for last run, cadence decisions, and current queue impact.
technicalScope: Expose last sync/generation timestamps, cadence decisions, and run outcomes via command output.
Include counts for scanned files, copied files, candidates, created, skipped, and failures.
Document fields for maintainers and automation consumers.
acceptanceCriteria: Maintainers can diagnose run behavior without reading raw evidence files.
Status output is stable JSON.

tasks.title: [workspace-kit] Reduce policy approval friction for recurring recommendation generation
tasks.approach: Configurable command-scoped approval UX with first-run prompt options and session memory for the active session.
technicalScope: Use Cursor question UI when available to present first-use approval choices for a sensitive command: Deny, Allow, Allow for this session.
Make approval behavior configurable by command with defaults that prompt on first use and record selection for session-scoped reuse when chosen.
Honor stored session choice for that specific command for remaining session duration; preserve fail-closed behavior for missing/invalid approval context.
Add tests for deny/allow/session pathways, per-command isolation, expiry/reset behavior, and policy trace evidence.
acceptanceCriteria: Default behavior prompts first use per sensitive command with Deny/Allow/Allow for this session.
Allow for this session reuses approval for the same command only for the rest of the session.
Behavior is configurable, deterministic, auditable, and remains fail-closed on invalid approval context.

tasks.title: [workspace-kit] Add scale and resilience tests for transcript ingestion
tasks.approach: Validate performance and correctness under realistic transcript volume and malformed data conditions.
technicalScope: Add integration tests for large transcript archives and repeated generation runs.
Add failure-path tests for malformed JSONL, partial writes, and unreadable files.
Assert deterministic outputs and bounded runtime behavior.
acceptanceCriteria: Ingestion remains reliable at expected scale envelopes.
Malformed input paths fail safely with clear diagnostics.

tasks.title: [workspace-kit] Add failed-file retry queue with bounded backoff for transcript sync
tasks.approach: Improve robustness by tracking failed transcript files and retrying deterministically.
technicalScope: Record failed sync attempts with reason and retry state.
Implement bounded retry strategy with explicit max attempts/backoff.
Surface retry queue state in status output.
acceptanceCriteria: Transient sync failures are retried without manual intervention.
Permanent failures remain visible with actionable details.

tasks.title: [workspace-kit] Publish transcript ingestion onboarding and operations runbook
tasks.approach: Provide one canonical manual-first guide plus advanced automation appendix for maintainers.
technicalScope: Document end-user quickstart for sync and one-shot ingest commands.
Document maintainer operations, troubleshooting, and policy approval workflows.
Document optional scheduler integration as advanced mode only.
acceptanceCriteria: Users can onboard transcript ingestion without external scheduler setup.
Maintainers have complete operations guidance for frequent runs.

tasks.title: [workspace-kit] Add transcript-derived metadata privacy and redaction guardrails
tasks.approach: Ensure recommendation metadata and evidence do not leak sensitive transcript content.
technicalScope: Define allowed transcript-derived metadata fields and redaction policy.
Apply redaction/sanitization in sync and recommendation generation outputs.
Add tests validating sensitive tokens/strings are not persisted in task metadata or evidence.
acceptanceCriteria: Transcript-derived recommendation artifacts comply with redaction policy.
Privacy guardrails are documented and test-covered.

tasks.title: [workspace-kit] Add optional pre-release transcript ingestion hook
tasks.approach: Optionally surface fresh recommendation signals during release readiness without blocking publish by default.
technicalScope: Define release workflow hook that can run transcript sync + generation in advisory mode.
Emit explicit summary artifact for release reviewers.
Document enable/disable controls and default non-blocking behavior.
acceptanceCriteria: Release workflow can include transcript-based recommendation refresh when configured.
Hook behavior and impact are explicit and observable.

tasks.title: [workspace-kit] Define response template contract and advisory warning policy
tasks.approach: Define configurable response-template contract and warning policy with advisory-only default and optional stricter modes.
technicalScope: Define canonical responseTemplate model (id, scope, expected sections/shape, advisory flags, versioning).
Define warning policy for template mismatches and fallback paths with concise messages and advisory-only default behavior.
Add configuration hooks for enforcement mode so advisory remains default and stricter validation can be enabled intentionally.
Define compatibility posture so template contract evolves safely with runtime/module outputs.
acceptanceCriteria: Response template contract is documented and versioned as a core runtime concept.
Default mode is advisory-only and configurable to stricter modes.
Warning copy rules require short, actionable messages.

tasks.title: [workspace-kit] Add response template registry and runtime lookup APIs
tasks.approach: Implement central registration/loading for response templates to avoid ad hoc template parsing in individual modules.
technicalScope: Add registry for response template definitions with validation and stable identifiers.
Expose runtime lookup helpers for command/workflow surfaces.
Add deterministic errors/warnings when requested template IDs are missing or invalid.
acceptanceCriteria: Runtime can resolve registered response template definitions by id.
Missing/invalid template ids produce short advisory warnings.

tasks.title: [workspace-kit] Integrate response templates into workspace-kit run output shaping (advisory mode)
tasks.approach: Apply response templates at the command output boundary without failing command execution when templates are missing/mismatched.
technicalScope: Hook template application into the `workspace-kit run` result path.
Attach concise advisory warnings when template application is partial/unavailable.
Preserve existing `ModuleCommandResult` compatibility for non-template flows.
acceptanceCriteria: Template-aware output shaping works for selected command flows in advisory mode.
Command success/failure semantics are unchanged by template advisory warnings.

tasks.title: [workspace-kit] Support plain-English instruction mapping to response templates
tasks.approach: Allow instruction phrases like 'Use the COMPLETED_TASK template' to resolve to response template IDs deterministically.
technicalScope: Define parser/mapping rules for plain-English template directives in workflow instructions.
Normalize template ID extraction and collision handling.
Emit short warnings when mapping is ambiguous or unknown.
acceptanceCriteria: Instruction phrases can request response templates using plain English.
Ambiguous/unknown directives produce concise advisory warnings.

tasks.title: [workspace-kit] Add response template configuration across module and workspace surfaces
tasks.approach: Configure template defaults and overrides through canonical precedence across module config, workspace config, and runtime arguments.
technicalScope: Define config keys for default template selection, workflow-specific overrides, and enforcement mode selection.
Wire keys through existing config metadata/explain surfaces.
Document precedence and fallback behavior with advisory warning examples and strict-mode examples.
acceptanceCriteria: Template behavior is configurable via canonical config surfaces.
Explain/resolve outputs include response template effective values and enforcement mode.

tasks.title: [workspace-kit] Add response template observability and concise warning telemetry
tasks.approach: Make template usage and warning outcomes debuggable while keeping warning copy intentionally brief.
technicalScope: Emit structured metadata for requested/applied template IDs and advisory warning outcomes.
Add status surfaces to inspect template application behavior over recent runs.
Standardize warning message length limits and wording patterns.
acceptanceCriteria: Operators can diagnose template application behavior from structured output.
Warnings remain short and consistent across workflows.

tasks.title: [workspace-kit] Add response template test matrix and maintainer runbook
tasks.approach: Prevent drift with targeted tests and clear maintainer guidance for authoring and operating response templates.
technicalScope: Add tests for valid/invalid template IDs, advisory fallback behavior, and plain-English directive mapping.
Add tests ensuring warning messages remain short within defined limits.
Publish maintainer runbook for creating, versioning, and rolling out response templates.
acceptanceCriteria: Response template behavior is comprehensively test-covered for advisory mode.
Maintainers have an operational guide for response template lifecycle management.

tasks.title: [workspace-kit] Add package scripts for transcript sync and ingest CLI entrypoints
tasks.approach: Provide stable, documented commands so editor automation and hooks do not embed fragile `node dist/cli.js` paths or ad-hoc args.
technicalScope: Add `pnpm` scripts (e.g. `transcript:sync`, `transcript:ingest`) that invoke `workspace-kit run sync-transcripts` and `ingest-transcripts` with `{}` JSON args as required by the CLI.
Define behavior when `dist/` is missing (fail fast with clear message, or build-first — pick one and document).
For `ingest-transcripts`, document how callers pass `policyApproval` (env and/or script wrapper) for non-interactive runs.
Keep scripts repo-local only; no change to published package contract unless explicitly required.
acceptanceCriteria: Maintainers can run transcript sync and ingest from `package.json` scripts without hand-rolling CLI paths.
Script behavior for missing build output is explicit and user-friendly.
Policy expectations for ingest are documented at the script boundary.

tasks.title: [workspace-kit] Add Cursor/VS Code workspace tasks for folder-open transcript sync
tasks.approach: Use native `runOn: folderOpen` tasks so opening the repo triggers copy-first sync without blocking the agent; keep full ingest as an explicit or opt-in task.
technicalScope: Add `.vscode/tasks.json` with at least one task that runs `transcript:sync` (or equivalent) with `runOptions.runOn: folderOpen`.
Mark long-running or noisy tasks appropriately (`isBackground`, empty `problemMatcher` where needed) so the UI does not treat transcript copy as a failing build.
Add a separate optional task for full `ingest-transcripts` (manual or opt-in) that documents required `WORKSPACE_KIT_POLICY_APPROVAL` / args for unattended use.
Do not enable full ingest on every folder open by default unless explicitly chosen — default path should be sync-only to avoid policy friction and load.
Verify behavior in Cursor (and VS Code compatibility note if differences exist).
acceptanceCriteria: Opening the workspace runs transcript file copy (sync) automatically per task configuration.
Full ingest is available but not silently policy-breaking on folder open.
Tasks are documented and reproducible for other maintainers.

tasks.title: [workspace-kit] Document Cursor-native transcript automation runbook for maintainers
tasks.approach: Make the editor automation discoverable and safe: what runs when, what requires approval, and how it relates to cadence and `ingest-transcripts`.
technicalScope: Add or extend a maintainer runbook under `docs/maintainers/runbooks/` covering folder-open sync, manual ingest, and policy approval patterns.
Clarify limitation: folder open fires on workspace load, not wall-clock intervals; point to OS schedulers or future hooks for true periodic runs.
Cross-link transcript automation baseline workbook and CONFIG keys (`improvement.transcripts`, `improvement.cadence`).
Note gitignore/privacy expectations for `agent-transcripts/` vs source paths.
acceptanceCriteria: A maintainer can follow the runbook to enable and troubleshoot Cursor tasks without reading source.
Sync vs ingest semantics and policy requirements are explicit.

tasks.title: [workspace-kit] Optional background transcript sync after task-engine transition to completed
tasks.approach: Complement Cursor folder-open automation with a repo-native hook when a task reaches `completed`: spawn a non-blocking subprocess so the transition returns immediately while transcripts refresh for later analysis.
technicalScope: Add config gate (e.g. `improvement.hooks.afterTaskCompleted`) defaulting off or sync-only.

On successful `run-transition` to `completed`, if enabled, `spawn` a detached child process running `transcript:sync` or `transcript:ingest` without awaiting completion; parent uses `unref()` or equivalent to avoid holding the process open.
Prevent overlapping runs via a simple lock file or skip-if-busy in the child script to align with T251 idempotency goals.
Document interaction with cadence: prefer sync-only in hook; full ingest optional and policy-aware.
Add tests for hook wiring with subprocess mocked or isolated temp workspace.
acceptanceCriteria: Task transitions to `completed` are not materially delayed by transcript I/O.
Hook is opt-in, safe with concurrent transitions, and covered by tests.
Behavior is documented alongside Cursor-native tasks (T273).

tasks.title: [workspace-kit] Add transcript sync command for end-user improvement ingestion
tasks.approach: Provide a manual first-class transcript-sync command that users can run directly instead of requiring cron/scheduler setup.
technicalScope: Add a command surface to sync new transcript JSONL files from a configured source path into local archive path (`agent-transcripts/` by default).
Keep sync deterministic and non-destructive (copy new files, avoid mutating source).
Return machine-readable sync summary (files discovered, copied, skipped, errors).
acceptanceCriteria: Users can run one command to sync transcript archives locally without external scheduler setup.
Sync command output is deterministic JSON suitable for agent workflows.

tasks.title: [workspace-kit] Define Task Engine core schema and lifecycle
tasks.approach: Implement typed schema, lifecycle states, transition validation, and guard hooks based on the T199 workbook.
technicalScope: Implement `TaskEntity` type with all required and optional fields from the workbook.
Implement `TaskStatus` enum: `proposed`, `ready`, `in_progress`, `blocked`, `completed`, `cancelled`.
Implement `TaskType` field (string, uniform lifecycle in Phase 1).
Implement the allowed-transition map as a typed constant.
Implement `TransitionGuard` interface and `GuardResult` type.
Implement `TransitionValidator` that checks state validity, runs registered guards in order, and returns typed errors on rejection.
Implement built-in guards: `dependency-check` and `state-validity`.
Register task-engine module with `WorkflowModule` contract (update existing stub with real capabilities, config, state, instruction entries).
Add unit tests: every valid transition, every invalid transition, guard registration and execution order, built-in guard behavior, error codes.
acceptanceCriteria: `TaskEntity`, `TaskStatus`, `TransitionGuard`, and related types are exported.
Transition validator rejects all disallowed state changes with specific error codes from the taxonomy.
Guard hooks run in registration order; first rejection stops the chain.
Built-in `dependency-check` guard prevents starting tasks with incomplete deps.
Module registration passes registry validation with updated instruction entries.

tasks.title: [workspace-kit] Implement Task Engine transition runtime and persistence
tasks.approach: Transition service with auto-unblock, evidence emission, and file-backed JSON persistence.
technicalScope: Implement `TaskStore` — file-backed JSON store at `.workspace-kit/tasks/state.json`. Schema-versioned. Load on init, save after each transition batch.
Implement `TransitionService` — accepts a task ID + target state, loads task from store, validates via `TransitionValidator`, applies state mutation, runs post-transition hooks, saves.

Implement **auto-unblock**: when a task transitions to `completed`, scan dependents; any task whose `dependsOn` list is now fully satisfied moves from `blocked → ready` automatically. Include unblocked task IDs in evidence.
Implement structured transition evidence: `transitionId`, `taskId`, `fromState`, `toState`, `guardResults`, `dependentsUnblocked`, `timestamp`, `actor`.
Implement `onCommand` handler for the task-engine module: `run-transition`, `get-task`, `list-tasks`, `get-ready-queue`.
Wire commands through the CLI `run` command (already built).
Add exhaustive test matrix: every valid transition, every invalid transition, guard failures, auto-unblock cascades, evidence completeness, persistence round-trip, concurrent-safe save behavior.
acceptanceCriteria: Transition service is deterministic under repeated runs with identical inputs.
Auto-unblock correctly cascades through dependency chains.
Evidence records are emitted for every transition including auto-unblocks.
File-backed store persists and reloads correctly.
`workspace-kit run list-tasks` and `workspace-kit run get-ready-queue` return structured JSON.
Typed errors map to specific caller-facing failure states.

tasks.title: [workspace-kit] Add task-type adapter contract and TASKS.md generation
tasks.approach: Adapter interface for task sources + generated TASKS.md as the human-readable view + one-time import from current TASKS.md format.
technicalScope: Define `TaskAdapter` interface: `load()` to hydrate tasks from external source, `save()` to persist back, `supports()` to declare capabilities (read, write, watch). Adapters are optional — the file-backed store is the canonical source.
Enforce adapter capability validation on registration.

Implement **TASKS.md generator**: reads task state from the store and produces a formatted `docs/maintainers/TASKS.md` matching the current section structure (status markers, dependency fields, phase groupings). This is a write-only output, not a round-trip.

Implement **import-tasks command**: one-time parser that reads the current TASKS.md markdown format and imports tasks into the engine's JSON state. Handles status markers (`[p]`, `[ ]`, `[~]`, `[!]`, `[x]`), dependency fields, priority, and phase groupings.
Add `generate-tasks-md` and `import-tasks` as instruction entries on the task-engine module.
Add tests: adapter registration, TASKS.md generation matches expected format, import round-trip (import then generate produces equivalent output), invalid adapter rejection.
acceptanceCriteria: Adapter interface is stable, versioned, and exported.
`workspace-kit run generate-tasks-md` produces a well-formatted `docs/maintainers/TASKS.md` from engine state.
`workspace-kit run import-tasks` successfully imports the current TASKS.md into `.workspace-kit/tasks/state.json`.
Invalid adapters fail registration with clear error codes.
Adapters cannot mutate task state outside the transition service.

acceptanceCriteria: Same layered inputs always yield the same effective config.

Optional: define a simple **local evidence JSON template** (path under `artifacts/` or `.workspace-kit/`) for one rehearsal — not a product API.

tasks.title: [workspace-kit] Implement recommendation intake and review queue
tasks.approach: Treat the Task Engine as the canonical “recommendation queue” by representing each recommendation as a Task Engine task and by implementing a deterministic decision lifecycle wired to the `approvals` module.
technicalScope: Implement the recommendation queue as **Task Engine tasks** (task `type` = `improvement`) using the Task Engine lifecycle states:
recommendation enqueued → `ready`
being worked/approved → `in_progress`
accepted/applied → `completed`
declined → `cancelled`
Implement decision actions (`accept`, `decline`, `accept edited`) as validated operations that:
ensure the target task exists and is the correct type/state for the requested decision
are idempotent (replaying the same decision does not create duplicate decision records)
record an immutable decision history that ties decisions back to evidence (`evidenceKey`) and actor.
Wire the human approval path through the `approvals` module:
implement the `approvals` module runtime instruction used for recording `review-item` decisions
ensure the approvals decision updates the linked recommendation task state and writes decision evidence.
Record audit-friendly references suitable for lineage reconstruction (see `T192`), including:
recommendation task id
`evidenceKey`
actor
decision verb (`accept|decline|accept edited`)
relevant policy/config trace references available in command context.
acceptanceCriteria: Recommendation queue supports the full decision lifecycle with immutable decision history.
Decision actions are idempotent and validated against task existence/type/state.
Decisions are recorded in a machine-readable format that can be linked to lineage and evidence.

tasks.title: [workspace-kit] Implement evidence-backed recommendation generation
tasks.approach: Evidence-backed generation + heuristic confidence + deterministic dedupe by `evidenceKey`, producing Task Engine tasks (task `type` = `improvement`) ready for review.
technicalScope: Implement an **on-demand** `generate-recommendations` pipeline (invoked explicitly, not scheduled):
reads new evidence since last run using an incremental cursor stored in improvement module state
creates/updates recommendation tasks in the Task Engine store as `ready` tasks.
Evidence ingestion (as configured by your Phase 3 choices):
agent transcripts: parse local `agent-transcripts/` JSONL and extract user friction + improvement targets
git diffs: compute diffs between **stable evidence tags/releases** (diff-between-tags strategy) and extract change-derived improvement opportunities
policy traces: parse `.workspace-kit/policy/traces.jsonl` and infer UX/policy gaps from denied sensitive operations (include operation id + timestamp)
config mutations: parse `.workspace-kit/config/mutations*.jsonl` and infer improvement opportunities from failed/safe-write rejections
task transitions evidence: use task-engine transition log evidence to identify recurring friction patterns
Compute a deterministic `evidenceKey` for each candidate recommendation (dedupe by evidence key):
include evidence type + relevant file paths/identifiers + owning command/op context
Implement heuristic confidence scoring (see `T202`) and admission thresholds:
only enqueue recommendations that meet the deterministic threshold rule for the current evidence.
Implement dedupe behavior:
if a recommendation task with the same `evidenceKey` already exists, skip task creation (or update only non-decision metadata deterministically).
Populate Task Engine task fields for each enqueued recommendation:
`type="improvement"`
`title` = concise recommendation summary
`priority` set by heuristic confidence tier (deterministic)
`metadata` includes `evidenceKey`, confidence, and provenance links/refs needed by `T192`.
acceptanceCriteria: Generated recommendations are represented as Task Engine `improvement` tasks in `ready` state.
Each recommendation includes deterministic provenance refs (including `evidenceKey`) and heuristic confidence.
Dedupe prevents equivalent recommendations by `evidenceKey` across repeated on-demand runs.
Incremental cursor prevents missing new evidence while keeping results deterministic.

Correlation id requirements:

include decision correlation with actor and decision verb from `approvals`
reference policy trace entries (operation id + timestamp) and config mutation record identity when applicable.
Persist lineage events immutably (append-only) in a deterministic format.
Expose a lineage query path for debugging and audit (at minimum: given a recommendation task id, reconstruct the full rec/dec/app chain).
acceptanceCriteria: End-to-end lineage can be reconstructed deterministically.
Lineage records are immutable after commit.

tasks.title: [workspace-kit] Design Task Engine schema workbook
tasks.approach: Produce a design workbook that documents the resolved decisions above and specifies implementation-ready details for schema, transitions, guards, persistence, and errors.
technicalScope: **State model**: Document the core lifecycle states: `proposed`, `ready`, `in_progress`, `blocked`, `completed`, `cancelled`. States are fixed in Phase 1; extensibility deferred.

**Transition graph**: Document every allowed transition with guard conditions. Include reversibility markers. Mandatory transitions: `proposed → ready` (accept), `proposed → cancelled` (reject), `ready → in_progress` (start), `ready → blocked` (block), `ready → cancelled` (cancel), `in_progress → completed` (complete), `in_progress → blocked` (block), `in_progress → ready` (pause), `blocked → ready` (unblock, all deps met), `blocked → cancelled` (cancel).

**Entity schema**: Document task entity fields — required: `id` (string, format `T{number}`), `status` (enum), `type` (string, uniform lifecycle in P1), `title` (string), `createdAt` (ISO timestamp), `updatedAt` (ISO timestamp). Optional: `priority` (P1/P2/P3), `dependsOn` (string[]), `unblocks` (string[]), `metadata` (Record), `ownership` (string), `approach`, `technicalScope`, `acceptanceCriteria`.

**Guard hook contract**: Document the `TransitionGuard` interface — `canTransition(task, targetState, context): GuardResult`. Guards run in registration order; first rejection stops the transition. Built-in guards: `dependency-check` (blocks start if deps incomplete), `state-validity` (rejects impossible transitions).

**Persistence contract**: Document the file-backed JSON store at `.workspace-kit/tasks/state.json`. Schema-versioned. Loaded on engine init, saved after each transition batch. Configurable path via `src/modules/task-engine/config.md`.
**Evidence schema**: Document transition evidence fields: `transitionId`, `taskId`, `fromState`, `toState`, `guardResults[]`, `dependentsUnblocked[]`, `timestamp`, `actor`.

**Error taxonomy**: Document typed error codes: `invalid-transition` (disallowed from→to), `guard-rejected` (guard returned rejection), `dependency-unsatisfied` (deps not complete), `task-not-found`, `duplicate-task-id`, `invalid-task-schema`, `storage-read-error`, `storage-write-error`.
**CLI commands**: Document the commands the engine will expose via the module command router: `run-transition`, `get-task`, `list-tasks`, `get-ready-queue`, `import-tasks`.
**Generated TASKS.md**: Document how the engine produces a read-only `docs/maintainers/TASKS.md` from state, matching the current section structure.
acceptanceCriteria: All design decisions from the table above are documented with implementation-ready detail.
Transition graph covers every state pair (allowed and disallowed).
Entity schema, guard contract, evidence schema, and error taxonomy are specified to a level sufficient for T184 implementation.
Migration strategy for importing existing TASKS.md is documented.

tasks.title: [workspace-kit] Wire documentation module generate-document runtime through command dispatch

tasks.title: [workspace-kit] Build documentation module template library for core maintainer docs

technicalScope: Add templates for `ARCHITECTURE.md`, `PRINCIPLES.md`, `RELEASING.md`, `ROADMAP.md`, `SECURITY.md`, `SUPPORT.md`, and `TERMS.md`.

tasks.title: [workspace-kit] Harden documentation runtime config and validation matrix

tasks.title: [workspace-kit] Split document-project and generate-document into batch vs single commands

Update instruction files, RULES, README, and tests.

`generate-document` handles single-doc generation independently.

tasks.title: [workspace-kit] Generate full project documentation via document-project
tasks.approach: Use the documentation module as an AI agent to generate all 8 templates, producing AI-optimized docs in `.ai/` and human-readable docs in `docs/maintainers/`.
technicalScope: Read all 8 templates and follow `{{{ }}}` instruction blocks against real project context.
Generate AI-optimized docs in canonical `meta|v=1` pipe-delimited format for `.ai/`.
Generate human-readable prose Markdown for `docs/maintainers/`.
Skip `.ai/PRINCIPLES.md` (already exists; overwriteAi: false).
Overwrite all human docs with fresh content aligned to current project state.

tasks.title: [workspace-kit] Add CLI run command for module command dispatch
tasks.approach: Bridge runCli to the module command router so agents and scripts can invoke module commands from the terminal.
technicalScope: Add `workspace-kit run` CLI command that lists available module commands when called with no subcommand.
Add `workspace-kit run <command> [json-args]` dispatch that routes through `ModuleCommandRouter` and outputs structured JSON results.

tasks.title: [workspace-kit] Implement next-action suggestion engine
tasks.approach: Ready-queue analysis with priority sorting and blocking chain reporting.
technicalScope: Implement `get-next-actions` command that returns the ready queue sorted by priority (P1 first), with blocking chain analysis showing which completed tasks unblocked each ready task.
Include summary: how many tasks are in each state, what's blocking the most work, suggested next task to start.
Wire as instruction entry on the task-engine module with instruction file documenting agent usage.
Add tests: priority ordering, blocking chain accuracy, empty-queue behavior, all-complete behavior.
acceptanceCriteria: `workspace-kit run get-next-actions` returns prioritized ready queue with blocking chain context.
Agents can use the output to decide what to work on next without manual TASKS.md inspection.

tasks.title: [workspace-kit] Author Phase 2 config, policy, and cutover workbook
tasks.phase: Phase 2 config, policy, and local task cutover
tasks.approach: Same rigor as `T199`: binding workbook before implementation; incorporates the resolved decisions above and fills in field-level detail.
technicalScope: **Project vs module:** **Project (global)** config is maintainers’ `.workspace-kit/config.json` (domain keys and/or `modules.<moduleId>` overrides). **Module-level** config is each module’s `config.md` / registration defaults, merged in **registry dependency order** **before** the project layer applies.

**Config precedence (binding, low → high):** kit built-in defaults → **merged module-level** contributions (topological order; collision rules per workbook) → **project (global)** `.workspace-kit/config.json` **overrides** that merge → `WORKSPACE_KIT_*` env (nested keys via `__`) → per-invocation JSON on `workspace-kit run` (highest).
**Merge semantics:** document per-field behavior (scalar replace vs deep object merge) and validation failure modes.
**Typed registry:** domain keys (e.g. core, task-engine, documentation), schema validation, stable error taxonomy.
**Explain command:** JSON report: logical path → effective value → winning layer + discarded alternates.

**Policy:** enumerate sensitive **operation IDs** aligned with router/CLI (minimum: `upgrade`; `init` when it performs writes; `document-project` / `generate-document` when not `dryRun` or when configured overwrites would touch outputs; task-engine `import-tasks`, `generate-tasks-md`, `run-transition`). Read-only paths (`doctor`, `check`, `drift-check`, `list-tasks`, `get-task`, `get-ready-queue`, `get-next-actions`, dry-run generation, etc.) **excluded** unless workbook adds an explicit exception.
**Approvals + traces:** contract for agent-supplied approval fields; trace record shape (operation, actor, allowed/denied, rationale, timestamp, config snapshot hash optional).
**Actor:** document the default resolution chain (see decisions table).

**Local task cutover:** numbered steps for maintainers (backup, `import-tasks`, validate, `generate-tasks-md`, git/PR hygiene); explicit **non-goal:** no packaged migration runner or staged migration engine in `v0.4.0`.
acceptanceCriteria: Workbook exists at `docs/maintainers/phase2-config-policy-workbook.md` and is complete enough that implementers do not need ad hoc product decisions during `T187`–`T189`.
Non-goals for `v0.4.0` explicitly exclude generic migration orchestration in the npm package.

tasks.title: [workspace-kit] Harden config validation and agent resolve-config surface
tasks.approach: Fail-fast validation with stable errors; deterministic JSON for full effective config (agent-first).
technicalScope: Tighten schema validation for `.workspace-kit/config.json` and merged effective config (paths, field-level messages aligned with `explain-config` semantics).
Add a `workspace-kit run` / workspace-config command that emits the **full** effective configuration object (or per-domain slices per workbook) for automation, not only field explain.
Extend maintainer matrix or workbook notes so validation and resolve outputs are traceable to test cases.
acceptanceCriteria: Invalid configs fail with typed, path-qualified errors; valid merges remain bitwise deterministic for the same inputs.
Resolve output is JSON-only, stable field ordering or documented sort, and covered by tests.

tasks.title: [workspace-kit] Version policy traces and config-driven sensitive-operation extensions
tasks.approach: Trace schema versioning + documented extension points for sensitive op lists from effective config.
technicalScope: Add an explicit **schema version** (or equivalent) to policy trace records and document upgrade expectations for consumers reading `.workspace-kit/policy/` logs.

Wire **config-driven** extension of sensitive operation identifiers (within safe bounds: documented merge with built-in baseline, deny-by-default behavior for unknown router paths unchanged unless workbook extends).
Integration tests: traces include version field; extended op list flows through policy evaluation.
acceptanceCriteria: Traces remain machine-readable and backward-documented; version bump procedure is recorded in maintainer docs.
Extending sensitive ops via config is tested and does not weaken default deny posture for undeclared write paths.

tasks.title: [workspace-kit] Implement end-user config command surface
tasks.approach: Add a canonical CLI `config` command group that routes through typed config resolution and validation instead of exposing raw file editing as the primary interface.
technicalScope: Add `workspace-kit config` command group to the CLI surface.
Implement `config list`.
Implement `config get <key>`.
Implement `config set <key> <jsonValue>`.
Implement `config unset <key>`.
Implement `config explain <key>`.
Implement `config validate`.
Return structured JSON output suitable for agent consumption.
Provide concise human-readable summaries where appropriate.
Fail safely on invalid keys, malformed JSON values, and type violations.
acceptanceCriteria: Users can inspect and mutate config without direct raw-file editing.
CLI config commands route through canonical config logic rather than ad hoc file writes.
Invalid writes are rejected before persistence.
Command behavior is deterministic and test-covered.

tasks.title: [workspace-kit] Add canonical persisted config store for project and user layers
tasks.approach: Back the config command surface with deterministic JSON config layers that are runtime-editable and distinct from descriptive markdown docs.
technicalScope: Define canonical persisted config file locations for project-level and user-level config.
Implement JSON read/write helpers with schema-validation integration.
Support missing-file bootstrap behavior.
Normalize serialization for deterministic diffs and stable read-after-write behavior.
Ensure write behavior is atomic or rollback-safe where practical.
Keep persisted config separate from generated human docs and module contract markdown.
acceptanceCriteria: Project and user config persist independently and resolve predictably.
Persisted config format is deterministic and machine-parseable.
Invalid or unreadable persisted config is surfaced with actionable errors.
Runtime can load and resolve persisted layers reliably.

tasks.title: [workspace-kit] Implement config metadata contract for user-facing settings
tasks.approach: Define a metadata model for config keys so runtime, explain, docs, and future UI surfaces all depend on the same source.
technicalScope: Define metadata fields for user-exposed config keys.
Include key name, type, description, default, allowed values, scope, owning module, sensitivity, restart requirement, and approval requirement where relevant.
Expose config metadata through the registry/runtime APIs.
Ensure command handlers and documentation generation can consume the same metadata source.
Treat absent metadata for exposed keys as a validation failure or explicit warning state.
acceptanceCriteria: Every user-exposed config key has retrievable metadata.
Metadata is programmatically available to runtime and docs layers.
Explain and docs features can use the same canonical metadata source.
Missing or inconsistent metadata is detectable and test-covered.

tasks.title: [workspace-kit] Implement effective-value explanation and precedence diagnostics
tasks.approach: Add deterministic explain output that tells users why a config value resolved the way it did.
technicalScope: Implement explain output for a single config key.
Show effective value and winning source layer.
Show overridden lower-priority values when present.
Show default value when relevant.
Show validation constraints, config scope, and owning module.
Indicate restart, rerun, or approval implications where metadata supports it.
Keep explain output stable enough for tests and agent consumption.
acceptanceCriteria: `config explain <key>` clearly identifies the winning value and why it won.
Users can distinguish defaults, project values, user values, and runtime overrides.
Output is deterministic and understandable.
Tests cover common precedence and conflict scenarios.

tasks.title: [workspace-kit] Add config mutation guardrails and safe-write behavior
tasks.approach: Apply schema, policy, and safe-write constraints so config editing remains safe-by-default.
technicalScope: Block writes to unknown keys.
Block writes that violate schema or type rules.
Validate mutations before persistence.
Respect policy and approval hooks for sensitive keys where applicable.
Prevent partial-write corruption.
Emit explicit error codes and actionable failure messages.
Record enough mutation context to support debugging and future evidence flows.
acceptanceCriteria: Unsafe config changes are rejected before write.
Sensitive settings can be approval-gated or blocked by policy.
Failure states are explicit, deterministic, and test-covered.
Successful writes preserve config integrity.

tasks.title: [workspace-kit] Generate canonical AI and human config reference documentation
tasks.approach: Generate config reference docs from config metadata instead of maintaining a second manual truth source.
technicalScope: Generate `.ai/CONFIG.md`.
Generate `docs/maintainers/CONFIG.md`.
Include key names, types, defaults, scopes, allowed values, owning modules, and operational notes.
Clearly distinguish persisted editable config from descriptive/generated documentation.
Keep generation deterministic from metadata and resolved config contract data.
acceptanceCriteria: AI and human config docs are generated from one canonical metadata source.
Generated docs stay aligned with implementation changes.
Docs describe usage and boundaries without becoming live config state.
Output is stable and reviewable.

tasks.title: [workspace-kit] Add config command integration tests and fixture coverage
tasks.approach: Cover the full config UX end-to-end with deterministic fixtures and failure-state verification.
technicalScope: Add integration tests for list, get, set, unset, validate, and explain.
Test project vs user precedence behavior.
Test invalid key handling.
Test malformed JSON and invalid-type handling.
Test missing-file bootstrap behavior.
Test explain output correctness and stable serialization.
Verify read-after-write behavior and deterministic output shapes.
acceptanceCriteria: Full user-facing config CLI flow is integration-tested.

tasks.title: [workspace-kit] Implement optional interactive config edit workflow
tasks.approach: Add a guided editing flow as a thin layer over canonical config commands rather than a separate mutation path.
technicalScope: Add `workspace-kit config edit`.
Support browsing or selecting available keys.
Display current value, default, description, and allowed values using config metadata.
Prompt for replacement values using metadata-driven guidance.
Reuse the same validation and persistence path as direct set/unset.
Keep interactive mode optional and non-authoritative.

tasks.title: [workspace-kit] Define config scope model and exposure policy for end-user editable keys
tasks.approach: Separate user-editable, maintainer-only, and internal settings so the config UX does not expose unstable internals casually.
technicalScope: Classify config keys by exposure level.
Classify config keys by scope such as project, user, runtime, and internal.
Define which keys appear in list, docs, and guided edit flows by default.
Define how hidden or internal keys behave in debug and explain contexts.
Align exposure rules with policy and approval constraints where relevant.

tasks.title: [workspace-kit] Emit config change evidence and audit-friendly mutation records
tasks.approach: Record structured config mutation evidence compatible with the project’s evidence-first and future lineage-oriented design.
technicalScope: Record config mutation events with timestamp, actor, key, scope, result, and old/new value summaries.
Distinguish successful vs rejected mutations.
Avoid leaking sensitive raw values where inappropriate.
Store mutation evidence in a deterministic, machine-readable format.
Make mutation evidence retrievable for debugging and future approval/recommendation integration.
acceptanceCriteria: Config mutations emit structured evidence records.
Rejected changes are diagnosable from evidence output.

Design is compatible with future enhancement, approval, and lineage work.

tasks.title: [workspace-kit] Enforce compatibility contract via runtime and CI gates
tasks.approach: Convert compatibility policy into executable fail-closed checks in CLI/runtime and CI.
technicalScope: Implement compatibility validation command/path that evaluates modules/extensions against the compatibility contract and matrix.
Wire compatibility checks into CI/release-readiness so incompatibilities fail with explicit diagnostics.
Ensure conformance outputs are machine-readable and stable for automation.
Add negative-path tests proving unsupported combinations fail closed.
acceptanceCriteria: At least one runtime/CLI and one CI gate enforce compatibility rules.

tasks.title: [workspace-kit] Define canonical compatibility matrix schema and source-of-truth mapping
tasks.approach: Establish one authoritative matrix schema spanning runtime, module contract, config schema, and policy/trace schema versions.
technicalScope: Define matrix schema fields, required keys, and versioning policy.
Define supported/unsupported combination semantics and severity levels.
Define ownership and update workflow so matrix changes are auditable and release-reviewed.
Define mapping from matrix entries to validation checks and release evidence artifacts.
acceptanceCriteria: Compatibility matrix schema is versioned and validated in automated checks.

tasks.title: [workspace-kit] Add evidence retention, compaction, and redaction policy for runtime artifacts
tasks.approach: Add lifecycle controls for append-only evidence stores to preserve operability and safety at scale.
technicalScope: Define retention/rotation policy for `.workspace-kit` evidence files (policy traces, config mutations, approvals decisions, lineage, improvement state).
Define optional compaction/summarization strategy and recovery guarantees.
Define redaction/classification rules for sensitive payloads in evidence and recommendation metadata.
Add diagnostics surfacing for evidence volume growth and retention status.
acceptanceCriteria: Evidence lifecycle policy is documented and test-covered for at least one prune/rotate path.

tasks.title: [workspace-kit] Operationalize release channels with tag/dist-tag/release-label mapping

technicalScope: Define mapping between channel (`canary`/`stable`/`lts`) and git tag conventions, GitHub release labeling, and npm dist-tags.

acceptanceCriteria: Channel promotions and rollbacks are reproducible via documented command workflows.

tasks.title: [workspace-kit] Add roadmap/tasks/feature-matrix consistency guard

technicalScope: Define canonical shared fields for phase/task state across `ROADMAP.md`, `TASKS.md`, and `FEATURE-MATRIX.md`.

tasks.title: [workspace-kit] Preserve rich maintainer TASKS.md structure in task-engine round-trip
tasks.approach: Patch task-engine import/generation so maintainers can use engine state without losing prose-only planning sections in `docs/maintainers/TASKS.md`.
technicalScope: Update markdown importer to ignore non-task `###` headings (for example, design-decision headings) instead of treating them as parse errors.
Update `generate-tasks-md` to preserve existing document structure by syncing task heading status/title in-place when a target TASKS markdown file already exists.

acceptanceCriteria: Running `import-tasks` against current maintainer TASKS docs reports no skipped entries for non-task section headings.
Running `generate-tasks-md` on existing maintainer TASKS docs does not strip prose/section content unrelated to task headings.

data.listTasksCursorSemantics: Keyset pagination: opaque cursor from prior nextCursor; under concurrent updates rows may move across pages (re-run without cursor for a fresh ordering snapshot).

cae.agentRenderHint: If present, summarize CAE shadow outcome in one short paragraph; when traceId is set, operators can run cae-explain / cae-get-trace.
